questions.question: Which of the following information will "show cdp neighbors detail" reveal?
options: All layer 3 addresses limited to one per protocol
questions.explanation: [CxNx] Which of the following information will "show cdp neighbors detail" reveal? (Choose all that apply.) a) Router Platform b) All layer 3 addresses limited to one per protocol c) IOS version d) Incoming and outgoing port e) IP address only f) IP address and mask g) Router Name Answer a) Router Platform b) All layer 3 addresses limited to one per protocol c) IOS version d) Incoming and outgoing port g) Router Name I have configured the neighbo

questions.question: At what layer of the OSI model does ICMP operate?
questions.explanation: [RINx] At what layer of the OSI model does ICMP operate? a) The Application layer b) The Data link layer c) The Physical layer d) The Network layer e) The Transport layer f) The Session layer Answer d) The Network layer Below is a capture of an ICMP echo request which is the first part of a ping. (This would be followed by an echo reply.)

questions.question: Which statements about IP numbers are correct?
options: An IP number can be used to
An IP number can be used to identify a serial interface in a router.
An IP number can be used to identify a logical Ethernet network.
An IP address must be unique only on the logical network where it exists.
questions.explanation: [RINx] Which statements about IP numbers are correct? (Choose four.) a) An IP number can be used to identify a ring in a Token Ring network. b) An IP number can be used to identify a serial interface in a router. c) An IP number can be used to identify a logical Ethernet network. d) An IP address must be unique only on the logical network where it exists. e) All bits of an IP number are assigned by InterNIC and may not be modified. f) An IP addre

questions.question: Which of the following are benefits of segmenting with routers?
options: Increases the size of the frames
questions.explanation: [RxNx] Which of the following are benefits of segmenting with routers? a) Increases bandwidth available to nodes b) Controls broadcasts c) Increases the size of the frames d) Reduces the number of collisions e) None of the above Answer a) Increases bandwidth available to nodes b) Controls broadcasts d) Reduces the number of collisions On a router each port is its own broadcast and collision domain.

questions.question: Which of the following statements does NOT define a purpose of the OSI Reference Model?
options: To constrain or define the implementation of network functions
To allow areas to evolve more quickly by preventing changes in one area from impacting
To allow engineers to design and develop in a modular fashion
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Which of the following statements does NOT define a purpose of the OSI Reference Model? (Choose one.) a) To define standard interfaces for plug-and-play compatibility and multi-vendor integration b) To constrain or define the implementation of network functions c) To allow areas to evolve more quickly by preventing changes in one area from impacting other areas d) To allow engineers to design and develop in a modular fashion e) To make it

questions.question: The presentation layer of the OSI model is primarily responsible for which of the following?
options: Providing code formatting and conversion such as EBCDIC to ASCII
Coordinating applications such as NFS and SQL as they interact on different hosts
questions.explanation: [OxNx] The presentation layer of the OSI model is primarily responsible for which of the following? (Choose two.) a) Ensuring reliable end-to-end communications by using connection oriented protocols b) Providing code formatting and conversion such as EBCDIC to ASCII c) Managing encryption of data d) Coordinating applications such as NFS and SQL as they interact on different hosts e) Providing support for the intelligent segmentation and reassemb

options: A method of ensuring the delivery
The assembly of data from different layers of the OSI model into separate data streams,
A method of distinguishing the data from different applications as it travels over a
The numbering system used to identify the data portion of an outgoing (multiplexing) or

questions.question: Barney is a host with IP address 10.1.1.1 in subnet 10.1.1.0/24. Which of the fol- lowing are things that a standard IP ACL could be configured to do? (Choose two answers.)
options: Match the exact source IP address.
Match IP addresses 10.1.1.1 through 10.1.1.4 with one access-list command
Match all IP addresses in Barney’s subnet with one access-list command without
Match only the packet’s destination IP address.
questions.explanation: Many headers include a field that identifies the next header that follows inside a message. Ethernet uses the Ethernet Type field, and the IP header uses the Protocol field. The TCP and UDP headers identify the application that should receive the data that follows the TCP or UDP header by using the port number field in the TCP and UDP headers, respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following topology terms most closely describe the topology created by a Metro Ethernet Tree (E-Tree) service? (Choose two answers.)
questions.explanation: The access layer switches play the role of connecting to the endpoint devices, whether they are end-user devices or servers. Then, from the access to the distribution layer, each access layer connects to two distribution switches typically, but with no direct connections between access layer switches, creating a mesh (but a partial mesh). A two-tier design, also called a collapsed core, does not use core switches at all.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most likely technology used for an access link to a Metro Ethernet service?
questions.explanation: The access layer switches, not the distribution layer switches, play the role of connecting to the endpoint devices, whether they are end-user devices or serv- ers. Then, from the access to the distribution layer, each access layer connects to two distribution switches typically, but with no direct connections between access layer switches, creating a mesh (but a partial mesh). A three-tier design, also called a core design, does use core switche

questions.question: An enterprise uses a Metro Ethernet WAN with an Ethernet LAN (E-LAN) service, with the company headquarters plus 10 remote sites connected to the service. The enterprise uses OSPF at all sites, with one router connected to the service from each site. Which of the following are true about the Layer 3 details most likely used with this service and design? (Choose two answers.)
options: The WAN uses one IP subnet.
The WAN uses 10 or more IP subnets.
A remote site router would have one OSPF neighbor.
A remote site router would have 10 OSPF neighbors.
questions.explanation: The access layer uses access switches, which connect to endpoint devices. A single access switch with its endpoint devices looks like a star topology. The distribution layer creates a partial mesh of links between the distribution switches and access switches, so it is neither a full mesh nor a hybrid.

questions.question: An enterprise uses an MPLS Layer 3 VPN with the company headquarters connected plus 10 remote sites connected to the service. The enterprise uses OSPF at all sites, with one router connected to the service from each site. Which of the following are true about the Layer 3 details most likely used with this service and design? (Choose two answers.)
options: The WAN uses one IP subnet.
The WAN uses 10 or more IP subnets.
A remote site router would have one OSPF neighbor.
A remote site router would have 10 or more OSPF neighbors.
questions.explanation: With a SOHO LAN, one integrated device typically supplies all the neces- sary functions, including routing, switching, wireless access point (AP), and firewall. The AP uses standalone mode, without a wireless LAN controller (WLC), and without a need to encapsulate frames in CAPWAP.

questions.question: A Layer 2 switch examines a frame’s destination MAC address and chooses to for- ward that frame out port G0/1 only. That action occurs as part of which plane of the switch?
questions.explanation: The hypervisor will virtualize RAM, CPU, NICs, and storage for each VM. The hypervisor itself is not virtualized, but rather does the work to virtualize other resources. Also, as virtual machines, the VMs do not use power, so the power is not virtualized.

questions.question: A router uses OSPF to learn routes and adds those to the IPv4 routing table. That action occurs as part of which plane of the switch?
questions.explanation: Hypervisors create a virtual equivalent of Ethernet switching and cabling between the VMs and the physical NICs. The VMs use a virtual NIC (vNIC). The hypervisor uses a virtual switch (vswitch), which includes the concept of a link between a vswitch port and each VM’s vNIC. The vswitch also connects to both physical NICs. The switch can then be configured to create VLANs and trunks as needed.

questions.question: A network uses an SDN architecture with switches and a centralized controller. Which of the following terms describes a function or functions expected to be found on the switches but not on the controller?
questions.explanation: Platform as a Service (PaaS) supplies one or more virtual machines (VMs) that have a working operating system (OS) as well as a predefined set of software development tools. As for the wrong answers, Software as a Service (SaaS) supplies a predefined software application, but typically with no ability to then later install your own applications. Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) supplies one or more working VMs, optionally with an OS installed,

questions.explanation: A and B are the correct answers. Broadcast address and multicast address are the only type of address- es that can send frames to multiple devices on the Local Area Network (LAN).

Straight-through cables are used to connect computers and other end-user devices e.g. printers to networking devices such as hubs and switches. questions.explanation: Core is the correct answer. Core aggre- gates distribution switches in very large campus LANs, provid- 321 ing very high forwarding rates for the larger volume of traffic due to the size of the network.

questions.question: Your company plans to start using public cloud service and now you are considering different WAN options. options: Using private WAN connections directly to the cloud provider
Using an Internet connection without VPN
Using an Internet connection with VPN 322
questions.explanation: A and D are the correct answers. Private WAN options use technologies like Ethernet WAN and MPLS, both of which keep data private by their nature and which in- clude QoS services. Private WAN use technologies to transmit data between two or more locations, whilst prioritizing private internet traffic and avoiding public network to increase the level of security. Essen- tially it is a private network that allows a multi-sited business to share inf

questions.question: A company uses a Metro Ethernet WAN with an Ethernet LAN (E-LAN) service, with the company headquarters plus 20 remote sites connected to the service. The enterprise uses OSPF at all sites, with one router connected to the service from each site. Which of the following are true about the Layer 3 details most likely used with this service and design? (Choose all that apply)
options: The WAN uses one IP subnet
The WAN uses 20 or more IP subnets
A remote site router would have one OSPF neighbor
questions.explanation: A and D are the correct answers. An E- LAN service is one in which the Metro Ethernet service acts as if the WAN were a single Ethernet switch so that each device can communicate directly to every other device. 325 As a result, the routers sit in the same subnet. With one headquarters router and 20 remote sites, each router will have 20 OSPF neighbors.

questions.question: Which cloud “As a Service” model is a form of cloud computing that delivers fundamental compute, network, and storage resources to consumers on-demand, over the in- ternet, and on a pay-as-you-go basis?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as a Service is the correct answer. Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) is a form of cloud computing that delivers fundamental compute, network, and storage resources to consumers on-demand, over the internet, and on a pay-as-you-go basis. IaaS gives users cloud-based alternatives to on-premise in- frastructure, so businesses can avoid investing in expensive on-site resources. With IaaS, you can buy what you need, as you need it, an

questions.question: Which cloud “As a Service” model is a software distribution model in which a third-party provider hosts ap- plications and makes them available to customers over the In- ternet?
questions.explanation: Software as a Service is the correct an- swer. Software as a Service (SaaS) is a software distribution model in which a third-party provider hosts applications and makes them available to customers over the Internet. SaaS platforms make software available to users over the inter- net, usually for a monthly subscription fee. With SaaS, you don’t need to install and run software applications on your computer (or any computer). Everything is availab

questions.question: Which cloud “As a Service” model is a complete development and deployment environment in the cloud, with resources that enable you to deliver everything from simple cloud-based apps to sophisticated, cloud-enabled enterprise applications?
questions.explanation: Platform as a Service is the correct an- swer. Platform as a Service (PaaS) is a complete develop- ment and deployment environment in the cloud, with resources that enable you to deliver everything from simple cloud-based apps to sophisticated, cloud-enabled enterprise applications. PaaS is primarily used by developers who are building software or applications. A PaaS solution provides the platform for de- velopers to create unique, customizable

All interfaces are assigned to VRF 1 by default.
questions.explanation: ESS An extended service set (ESS) consists of multiple access points (APs) with overlapping coverage areas, integrated into a single network to provide continuous connectivity for clients as they move across different APs’ coverage areas.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: Which of the following cloud service models provides the most control to the customer?
questions.explanation: 5 GHz C: 6 GHz The 5 GHz and 6 GHz frequency bands used by 802.11 wireless LANs offer many non-overlapping channels, simplifying the channel selection process compared to the 2.4 GHz band. These channels can be combined to create wider channels, enabling greater data transfer rates.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: Which cloud service model offers a complete software product to the customer?
questions.explanation: 1-6-11 In the 2.4 GHz band, the 1-6-11 channel pattern is generally recommended as it uses three non-overlapping channels, reducing interference between adjacent APs. However, most countries outside of the US and Canada can use other patterns as well, such as 1-5-9-13.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: AWS, Azure, and GCP are examples of what cloud deployment model?
questions.explanation: BSSID Each AP in an ESS is uniquely identified by its basic service set identifier (BSSID), which is the AP’s MAC address.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: Which of the following are advantages of cloud computing? (select two)
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: In the context of cloud computing, what is rapid elasticity?
options: Measuring and charging based on each customer’s use of resources
The ability to deploy cloud resource with a self-service model
The ability to flexibly scale infrastructure according to need
The ability to flexibly connect to cloud infrastructure via a variety of connection methods
questions.explanation: The ability to flexibly scale infrastructure according to need Rapid elasticity is a key feature of cloud computing, enabling businesses to quickly scale their IT resources up or down based on their current requirements.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: In which model do customers rent space, cooling, electricity, etc, in a shared data center?
questions.explanation: Colocation In colocation services, customers rent space, cooling, electricity, and other data center resources in a shared facility. They typically own the hardware and are responsible for managing their equipment within the data center.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: Which of the following virtualization technologies is more lightweight, using fewer hardware resources?
options: A VM running on a type-1 hypervisor
A VM running on a type-2 hypervisor
questions.explanation: A container Containers are more lightweight and use fewer hardware resources compared to Virtual Machines (VMs). They share the host system’s kernel and do not require a full operating system for each instance, which significantly reduces their overhead. Chapter 18: Wireless LAN fundamentals
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch15: LAN Arch

questions.question: Which of the following 802.11 standards offer many non-overlapping channels? (select two)
questions.explanation: The LWAP When FlexConnect local switching is enabled, the LWAP is responsible for switching traffic between the 802.11 wireless and Ethernet wired networks; it doesn’t tunnel data traffic to the WLC.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which of the following identifiers is the MAC address of the AP’s radio?
questions.explanation: Rogue detector In Rogue Detector mode, the LWAP is dedicated to analyzing traffic on the wired LAN to detect rogue devices connected to the network. The LWAP’s radios are disabled.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which of the following AP modes functions as a wireless client on behalf of a device without wireless capabilities?
questions.explanation: Management traffic In Cisco Meraki’s cloud-based AP solution, management traffic is tunneled to the Meraki cloud. This includes configuration and monitoring data, while user data traffic is handled locally on the AP.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which 802.11 standards support both the 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands? (select three)
questions.explanation: Unified A unified WLC is a dedicated hardware appliance. Various hardware models are available, supporting anywhere from a few hundred LWAPs to as many as 6000.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: What is the term for a wireless LAN’s non-unique, human-readable name?
questions.explanation: Client authentication and association B: RF management In a split-MAC architecture, functions like client authentication, association, and RF management are centrally controlled by the WLC. This central control allows for consistent policy enforcement and network management. The LWAPs handle real-time operations like transmitting and receiving RF signals.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which type of AP in an MBSS connects to the DS?
questions.explanation: The CAPWAP control tunnel is encrypted by default. Only the CAPWAP control tunnel is encrypted by default. The data tunnel, however, is not encrypted by default; it must be enabled separately. CAPWAP encryption uses Datagram Transport Layer Security (DTLS)—a type of TLS that uses UDP instead of TCP.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: When a wireless client sends a frame to another client associated with the same AP, what is the RA of the frame when the client transmits it?
questions.explanation: TKIP Temporal Key Integrity Protocol (TKIP) was developed as an interim solution to overcome the vulnerabilities of Wired Equivalent Privacy (WEP). TKIP was implemented as part of the original Wi-Fi Protected Access (WPA) standard to provide improved security compared to WEP. TKIP uses the same underlying mechanism as WEP but includes additional features to secure wireless data transmission.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which of the following messages are used in passive scanning?
questions.explanation: Open System C: Shared Key WEP supports two authentication methods: Open System and Shared Key. Open System Authentication allows any device to authenticate and attempt to connect to the network. Shared Key Authentication uses a WEP key for authentication, requiring the client device to have the correct key before it can connect.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which of the following are not 802.11 management messages?
questions.explanation: SAE Simultaneous Authentication of Equals (SAE) is a WPA3 feature that allows the client and AP to verify that they have matching PSKs without rendering the PSK vulnerable to brute-force attacks.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which kind of AP is managed by a WLC?
questions.explanation: CCMP B: GCMP Both Counter Mode with Cipher Block Chaining Message Authentication Code Protocol (CCMP) and Galois/Counter Mode Protocol (GCMP) use Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) in counter mode for encryption. CCMP is used in WPA2 for secure data encryption, while GCMP, which offers enhanced performance and security, is utilized in WPA3.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which of the following APs typically connects to the DS via an access link?
questions.explanation: EAPoL EAPoL (EAP over LANs) is the protocol employed by 802.1X to carry EAP (Extensible Authentication Protocol) messages between a supplicant and an authenticator.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Which LWAP mode captures 802.11 frames for analysis in Wireshark?
questions.explanation: FS Forward Secrecy (FS), also called Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS), is a feature of WPA3 that ensures that a compromised PSK cannot be used to decrypt past communications. This is because the encryption keys used for each session are unique and not solely derived from the PSK.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch17: Virtualization

questions.question: Examine the configuration that follows. What is the next hop for 10.10.20.0/24?
questions.explanation: The static route is ip route 10.10.20.0 255.255. 255.0 172.16.1.1. The next hop is the last IP address shown in this command. On a point-to-point link, the local exit interface can be used as part of the command instead of the next-hop address of the next router in the path. The local exit interface can also be used in a multi-access scenario, but this use is discouraged in most cases due to the potential overhead of the Layer 2-to-Layer 3 name resolution that takes place.

questions.question: What command allows you to confirm the IP address and mask assigned to an interface?
questions.explanation: The show ip interface brief command provides a nice summary of the IP addresses assigned to interfaces and their status. It does so in an easy-to-read, table-like format. This command is often typed as simply sh ip int br. This command does not provide mask information, however. For that, you use show ip interface.

questions.question: Given the following address and mask 172.16.10.1 255.255.248.0, what is the broadcast address for the subnet?
questions.explanation: Here the increment is on 8 as there are 5 bits used for subnetting. The host range of this subnet is 172.16.8.1 to 172.16.15.254. The broadcast address for the 172.16.8.0/21 subnet is 172.16.15.255. Answer Key to Practice Exam 2

questions.question: Which should be the subnet mask for a class C address if 62 host s are required?
questions.explanation: [RINx] Which should be the subnet mask for a class C address if 62 host s are required? a) 255.255.255. 192 b) 255.255.192.0 c) 0.0.0.192 d) 255.255.255.62 e) none of the above Answer a) 255.255.255.192 To calculate the number of host or networks using the formula 2 to the nth degree - 2 where the nth degree is the number of bits that will be used to describe the host or the network. To answer this question we first have to determine how many hos

questions.question: What would the class and major network be for the IP address 191.12.45.254?
questions.explanation: [xINx] What would the class and major network be for the IP address 191.12.45.254? (Choose one.) a) Class B, 191.12.45.0 b) Class B, 191.12.0.0 c) Class C, 191.12.45.0 d) Class C, 191.12.0.0 e) Class C, 255.255.255.0 f) Class B, 255.255.0.0 Answer b) Class B, 191.12.0.0 First Octet Rules: - If the first bit is 0, it's a Class A address. - If the first two bits are10, it's a Class B address. - If the first three bits are 110 and the value is less

questions.question: What would be the maximum number of hosts that a class C address could support assuming that the default subnet mask was used?
questions.explanation: [xINx] What would be the maximum number of hosts that a class C address could support assuming that the default subnet mask was used? (Choose one.) a) 65,534 b) 255 c) 254 d) 16,777,214 e) 1024 f) 2048 Answer c) 254 A class C address provides us with a maximum of 8 host bits, which is, of course, the fourth octet. The formula of 2 N -2 (where n is the total number of host bits) provides us with the total number of hosts. The reason why we subtrac

questions.question: What is the subnet mask for a /26 prefix?
questions.explanation: A /26 prefix uses 26 bits for the network portion. The 4th octet has 2 network bits (11000000 = 192). So the mask is 255.255.255.192. Block size = 64. Four /26 subnets fit in a /24.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch13: Subnetting Part 1

questions.question: What is the broadcast address of 172.16.32.0/20?
You need to subnet 192.168.1.0/24 into the smallest possible subnets that each support at least 14 hosts. What prefix length should you use?
questions.explanation: /28 gives 2^4−2 = 14 usable hosts — exactly meets the requirement. /29 gives only 6, which is too few. /27 gives 30, which wastes addresses. Always use the smallest block that fits.

questions.question: Which address is the network address (not usable for hosts) for 10.0.0.65/26?
questions.explanation: /26 block size=64. Subnets in 4th octet: .0, .64, .128, .192. The address 10.0.0.65 falls in the .64 block. Network address = 10.0.0.64. Broadcast = 10.0.0.127. Host .65 is the first usable host.

questions.question: Using VLSM, you need to allocate subnets for: 100 hosts, 50 hosts, 25 hosts, 10 hosts from 192.168.10.0/24. What is the first (largest) subnet allocated?
questions.explanation: VLSM allocates largest to smallest. 100 hosts needs 2^7−2=126 usable → /25. First subnet = 192.168.10.0/25 (range .0–.127). Next /26 for 50 hosts (.128–.191). Then /27 (.192–.223) for 25. Then /28 (.224–.239) for 10.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch15: Subnetting (VLSM) Part 3

questions.question: What wildcard mask would you use in an OSPF "network" statement to match exactly the network 192.168.5.0/24?
questions.explanation: Wildcard mask = inverse of subnet mask. /24 = 255.255.255.0. Inverse = 0.0.0.255. In OSPF: "network 192.168.5.0 0.0.0.255 area 0" matches all interfaces in the 192.168.5.0/24 network.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch27: OSPF Part 2

questions.question: A router has interface Gi0/0 with IP 10.1.1.30/27. What is the valid host range for this subnet?
questions.explanation: /27 block=32. The address .30 falls in the 0–31 block (network .0, broadcast .31). First host = .1, last host = .30 (the .30 address itself is a valid host — the broadcast is .31). Range: 10.1.1.1–10.1.1.30.

questions.question: What does the "network address" represent in a subnet?
options: The last IP — used for broadcasts
The IP that identifies the subnet itself — not assignable to hosts
The default gateway for the subnet
questions.explanation: The network address (lowest IP in the block) identifies the subnet and cannot be assigned to a host. It's used in routing tables to represent the entire subnet. Example: in 192.168.1.0/24, the .0 is the network address.

questions.question: A packet destined to 172.16.45.10 arrives at a router. The routing table has entries: 172.16.0.0/16, 172.16.45.0/24, 0.0.0.0/0. Which route is used?
options: All three routes are used equally
questions.explanation: Longest prefix match always wins regardless of AD. /24 is more specific than /16, which is more specific than /0 (default). 172.16.45.0/24 is the longest match for destination 172.16.45.10.

questions.question: What is the network address of 192.168.200.200/21?
questions.explanation: /21 = 255.255.248.0. Block size in 3rd octet = 8. Third octet groups: 0,8,16...192,200,208... The address 200 falls in the 192 block (192+8=200>200? No: 192 ≤ 200 < 200. Wait: 192,200 — 200 is the start of the next block. 200 falls in 192 block since 192+8=200 is the NEXT network). Network = 192.168.192.0.

questions.question: A /29 subnet has how many total addresses, and how many are usable for hosts?
questions.explanation: /29 has 3 host bits. 2^3 = 8 total addresses. 8 − 2 (network + broadcast) = 6 usable host addresses. Common use: very small segments. Subnet mask = 255.255.255.248.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch13: Subnetting Part 1

questions.question: How many /28 subnets can be created from a single /24 network?
questions.explanation: Borrowed bits = 28−24 = 4 bits. 2^4 = 16 subnets. Each /28 has 16 addresses (14 usable hosts), block size 16. Subnets: .0, .16, .32 ... .240.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch14: Subnetting Part 2

questions.question: Why does the formula for the number of hosts per subnet (2H – 2) require the subtraction of two hosts?
options: To reserve the two addresses required for DHCP opera- 70 tion
To reserve addresses for the subnet broadcast ad- dress and subnet ID
questions.explanation: To reserve addresses for the subnet broadcast address and subnet ID is the correct answer. By definition, two address values in every IPv4 subnet cannot be used as host IPv4 addresses: 1. The first (lowest) numeric value in the subnet for the subnet ID. 2. The last (highest) numeric value in the subnet for the subnet broadcast address.

questions.question: Which of the following addresses are valid Class C network IDs? (Choose two answers)
questions.explanation: A and C are the correct answers. Class C networks have the first octet in the range of 192–223, inclusive, 71 and their network IDs have a 0 in the last three octets.

questions.question: Which of the following IPv4 addresses has a subnet mask 255.0.0.0? (Choose all that apply)
questions.explanation: C, D, and E are the correct answers. De- scrip- tion Class A Class B Class C First 1 – 126 128 – 191 192 – 223 octet range 76 Valid 1.0.0.0 – 128.0.0.0 – 192.0.0.0 – net- 126.0.0.0 191.255.0.0 223.255.255. work 0 num- bers Default 255.0.0.0 255.255.0.0 255.255.255.0 mask

questions.question: Which of the following ranges is a valid class B network numbers?
questions.explanation: A is the correct answer. De- scrip- tion Class A Class B Class C First 1 – 126 128 – 191 192 – 223 octet range 77 Valid 1.0.0.0 – 128.0.0.0 – 192.0.0.0 – net- 126.0.0.0 191.255.0.0 223.255.255.0 work num- bers De- 255.0.0.0 255.255.0.0 255.255.255.0 fault mask

questions.question: What information can you extract having the IP address 172.16.99.45? (Choose two answers.)
options: The default mask for the network is 255.255.255.0.
The network is a Class B network.
The number of host bits in the unsubnetted network is 16.
questions.explanation: C and D are the correct answers. The first octet (172) is in the range of values for Class B ad- dresses (128–191). As a result, the network ID can be formed by copying the first two octets (172.16) and writing 0s for the last two octets (172.16.0.0). The default mask for all Class B networks is 255.255.0.0, and the number of host bits in all unsubnetted Class B networks is 79 16. The broadcast address of this network is 172.16.255.255.

questions.question: Given the following IP address - 20.54.23.3 and subnet mask - 255.255.255.0 choose the subnet ID and the broadcast address from that particular IP.
questions.explanation: A is the correct answer. If the mask has only 255 and 0 then the process of finding the subnet ID and the broadcast address is straightforward. Use the following steps for each of the four octets to find the subnet ID: Step 1) If the mask octet = 255, copy the decimal IP address. Step 2) If the mask octet = 0, write a decimal 0. Use the following steps for each of the four octets to find the 100 subnet broadcast address: Step 1) If the mask octet

questions.question: You are working with a network engineer to de- sign a network for the new Campus. He provided the following details: The IP address is 195.240.37.43 The subnet mask is 255.255.255.224 104 You have been tasked to: 1. Find the Prefix length 2. Find the Class of the address
questions.explanation: Prefix length = 24 | Class = C is the correct answer. The Prefix Length can be found once you convert the mask to binary and count the 1s. In this case, the subnet mask 255.255.255.224 in binary is 11111111.11111111.11111111.11100000. Count the number of the 1s to find the prefix length. Prefix Length: /27. The address 195.240.37.43 is a Class C. The range of Class C public IP Networks is: 192.0.0.0 – 223.255.255.0 The number Network bits for Cla

questions.question: Which of the following masks, when used within a Class B network, would supply enough subnet bits to support 90 subnets? (Choose two)
questions.explanation: A and D are the correct answers. 124 The masks in binary define a number of binary 1s, and the number of binary 1s defines the length of the prefix (network + subnet) part. With a Class B network, the network part is 16 bits. To support 90 subnets, the subnet part must be at least 7 bits long. Six subnet bits would supply only 26 = 64 subnets, while 7 subnet bits supply 27 = 128 subnets. The /24 answer supplies 8 subnet bits, and the 255.255.255.

questions.question: A printer’s IP address is 10.109.211.107/28. What is the network address of its subnet?
options: printer’s IP address is 10.109.211.107/28. What is the network address of its
questions.explanation: 10.109.211.96 To calculate the network address, write the final octet in binary: 0d107 = 0b01101011. Then, change the hosts bits (the final 4 bits) to 0: 01100000. Finally, convert back to decimal: 0b01100000 = 0d96. Therefore, the network address is 10.109.211.96.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch11: Subnetting

questions.question: R1’s IP address is 203.0.113.237/27. What is the broadcast address of its subnet?
questions.explanation: 203.0.113.255 To determine the broadcast address, write the final octet in binary: 0d237 = 0b11101101. Then, change the host bits (the last 5 bits) to 1: 11111111. Then, convert back to deci- mal: 0b11111111 = 0d255. Therefore, the broadcast address is 203.0.113.255.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch11: Subnetting

questions.question: You have been given the 192.168.27.0/24 address block to create subnets of appropriate sizes for your organization. You have already assigned the first sub- net to support 109 hosts. The second subnet must support 60 hosts. What is the usable host address range of the second subnet?
questions.explanation: 192.168.27.129 to 192.168.27.190 The first subnet, which must support 109 hosts, is 192.168.27.0/25 (a /25 prefix length leaves 7 host bits and supports 27 − 2 = 126 hosts). The second subnet, which must support 60 hosts, should use a /26 prefix length (leaving 6 hosts bits and supporting 26 − 2 = 62 hosts). Therefore, the second subnet is 192.168.27.128/26, with a usable address range of 192.168.27.129–192.168.27.190.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch11: Subnetting

questions.question: You have been given the 10.0.2.0/23 address block to create subnets of appropri- ate sizes for your organization. You have already assigned the first subnet to sup- port 40 hosts. The second subnet must support 29 hosts. What is the broadcast address of the second subnet?
questions.explanation: 10.0.2.95 The first subnet must use a /26 prefix length, which leaves 6 host bits, to support 40 hosts (26 − 2 = 62); it is 10.0.2.0/26. The second subnet should use a /27 prefix length, leaving 5 hosts bits and supporting 25 − 2 = 30 hosts. Therefore, the second subnet is 10.0.2.64/27, and its broadcast address is 10.0.2.95 (host bits all set to 1). Chapter 12: VLANs
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch11: Subnetting

questions.question: Which of the following statements about VLANs is true?
options: VLANs divide a collision domain into multiple collision domains.
VLANs divide a broadcast domain into multiple broadcast domains.
questions.explanation: VLANs divide a broadcast domain into multiple broadcast domains. Virtual Local Area Networks (VLANs) divide a single broadcast domain into multi- ple smaller broadcast domains. This improves network performance and security by reducing the size of the broadcast domains and isolating network traffic.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch11: Subnetting

questions.question: What is the significance of :: in the IPv6 address 2001:0:11:1::1:1AB1/64?
options: It is used to represent a single section of 0000.
It is used to represent consecutive sections of 0000.
It is used to represent a single section of 1111.
It is used to represent consecutive sections of 1111.
questions.explanation: The :: may be used once in an address in order to represent consecutive sections of 0000.

questions.question: What command causes a router interface to autoconfigure its host portion of the address?
questions.explanation: The modified EUI-64 method is used in this example.

questions.question: What command allows you to see the multicast addresses that an interface has joined in IPv6?
questions.explanation: The show ipv6 interface command provides this level of detail. It shows the multicast and link-local addressing joined.

questions.question: If you are using DHCP combined with the SLAAC feature in IPv6, you are most likely using what form of DHCP?
questions.explanation: Stateless DHCP is often used with SLAAC.

questions.question: If you use the ipv6 enable command on an interface, what address do you have on that interface?
questions.explanation: The ipv6 enable command ensures that a link-local address exists. Hands-On Lab Practice Assignment IPv6 Address Configuration and Verification To complete this Hands-On Lab Practice Assignment, download the assigned Packet Tracer file from the book’s companion website and perform the lab on your locally installed version of Packet Tracer. For instructions on how to download and use the Packet Tracer files, see “Packet Tracer Hands-On Lab Practice Assignments” in the Introduct

questions.question: What must be in place before you can route other devices’ IPv6 traffic through a Cisco router?
options: A loopback 0 interface with an IPv4 address assigned
The ipv6 unicast-routing global configuration command
questions.explanation: Cisco routers are able to support IPv6 on interfaces by default. They cannot, however, route other devices’ IPv6 traffic without the global ipv6 unicast-routing command.

questions.question: What prefix is reserved for IPv6 link-local addresses?
questions.explanation: fe80::/10 is the prefix reserved for link-local addressing in IPv6.

questions.question: Which of the following is used by an IPv6 multicast address?
questions.explanation: Multicast addresses in IPv6 use the ff00::/8 prefix (per RFC 4291, section 2.7).

questions.question: What command does a client use to enable stateless autoconfiguration of IPv6 address information on an interface?
questions.explanation: The ipv6 address autoconfig command is used on the client. The ipv6 address enable command does not exist. The ipv6 enable com- mand enables IPv6 and configures only an IPv6 link-local address using EUI-64 on the interface. The ipv6 address dhcp command relies on a DHCP server to determine configuration information in order to assign an IPv6 address to the interface. The ipv6 address autoconfig command only relies on a router trans- mitting RAs to determine a global IPv6 addr

questions.question: Which of the following IPv6 addresses appears to be a unique local unicast address? (Choose two) 186
questions.explanation: C and E are the correct answers. Unique local IPv6 addresses have a similar function as IPv4 private addresses. They are not allocated by an address registry and are not meant to be routed outside their domain. Unique local IPv6 addresses begin with FD00::/8. A unique local IPv6 address is constructed by appending a randomly-generated 40-bit hexadecimal string to the FD00::/8 prefix. The subnet field and interface ID are created in the same way a

questions.question: The router R2 has been configured with the 194 ipv6 address 2000:1:1:1::1/64 command on its G0/1 interface as shown in the figure. The router creates a link-local address of FE80::FF:FE00:11 as well. The interface is working. Which of the following routes will the router add to its IPv6 routing table? (Choose two answers.)
questions.explanation: C and D are the correct answers. With an IPv6 address on a working interface, the router adds a con- nected route for the prefix (subnet) implied by the ipv6 ad- dress command. 195 It also adds a local host route (with a /128 prefix length) based on the unicast address. The router does not add a route based on the link-local address. The router will add the following routes to its IPv6 routing table: 1. A route for 2000:1:1:1::/64 2. A route for

questions.question: Which of the following are not valid IPv6 addresses? (select two)
questions.explanation: access-list 1 permit 8.8.8.8 B: access-list 1 permit 8.8.8.8 0.0.0.0 D: access-list 1 permit host 8.8.8.8 When configuring a standard ACL, there are three ways to specify a single IP address: { | } ip-address, { | } ip-address , and permit deny permit deny 0.0.0.0 { | } ip-address. Option C is incorrect because it specifies the permit deny host wildcard mask , which is equivalent to a /0 netmask; it matches all 255.255.255.255 possible IP address
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which of the following is a correctly abbreviated version of the IPv6 address 2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334?
questions.explanation: As close to the destination as possible Standard ACLs should generally be applied as close to the destination of the traffic you want to filter. Because standard ACLs filter packets based only on their source IP
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which command configures an IPv6 address using the specified prefix and an automatically generated interface ID?
questions.explanation: Data sequencing TCP provides data sequencing, ensuring that packets arrive in the correct order. UDP, being a simpler and more lightweight protocol, does not provide this feature. Both protocols support error detection (both headers include a “Checksum” field for that purpose), but only TCP ensures reliable delivery and ordered data sequencing.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: R1’s G0/1 interface’s MAC address is 7ea2.1097.ff27. What is its Modified EUI-64 interface ID?
questions.explanation: Real-time applications 566 appendiX d Chapter quiz answers C: Simple query/response protocols UDP is often chosen for real-time applications (like streaming and online gaming) due to its lower latency, and for simple query/response protocols like DNS due to its simplicity and lower overhead. TCP is most often used for reliable data transfer, like file transfers.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which MAC address was used to generate the Modified EUI-64 interface ID 1f7d:2aff:fedd:611c?
questions.explanation: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK The TCP three-way handshake involves a SYN packet sent from the client to the server, a SYN-ACK response from the server, and finally an ACK from the client to the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which type of IPv6 address is equivalent to a private IPv4 address?
questions.explanation: The sequence number of the next segment the host expects to receive. The Acknowledgement Number in a TCP segment indicates the sequence number of the next expected byte from the sender, effectively acknowledging receipt of all pre- ceding bytes.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which kind of IPv6 address is not routable?
questions.explanation: An ephemeral port Clients typically select a random ephemeral port for the source port when initiating communication to a server. Ephemeral ports are temporary ports assigned for the duration of the session.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which kind of IPv6 address must be configured on every IPv6-enabled interface?
questions.explanation: Telnet D: FTP Both Telnet and FTP use TCP, which provides reliable delivery of messages. SNMP and NTP, on the other hand, use UDP, which does not provide reliable delivery. Chapter 23: Standard access control lists
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which IPv6 multicast scope remains on the local segment?
questions.explanation: ipv6 route 2001:db8::1/128 2001:db8:23::3 A host route is a route to a single destination address, using a /128 prefix length (/32 in IPv4). A recursive route is configured by specifying a destination network and a next-hop address, so A is the correct answer.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which IPv6 multicast address can be used to achieve the same effect as a message to the IPv4 address 255.255.255.255?
questions.explanation: ipv6 route ::/0 GigabitEthernet0/1 2001:db8::1 A default route is a route to ::/0, matching all possible IPv6 addresses. A fully specified route includes both the exit interface and the next-hop address, so option C is correct. Chapter 22: Transmission Control Protocol and User Datagram Protocol
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which IPv6 address type involves configuring the same address on multiple hosts?
questions.explanation: Anycast An anycast address is indistinguishable from a unicast address; the only difference is that an anycast address is configured on multiple hosts. This is useful when providing services over the internet; by configuring the same IP address on servers in different global locations, client devices can access the server closest to their location. Chapter 21: IPv6 routing
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: What is the IPv4 equivalent of an IPv6 neighbor table?
questions.explanation: access-list 1 permit 8.8.8.8 B: access-list 1 permit 8.8.8.8 0.0.0.0 D: access-list 1 permit host 8.8.8.8 When configuring a standard ACL, there are three ways to specify a single IP address: { | } ip-address, { | } ip-address , and permit deny permit deny 0.0.0.0 { | } ip-address. Option C is incorrect because it specifies the permit deny host wildcard mask , which is equivalent to a /0 netmask; it matches all 255.255.255.255 possible IP address
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: What scope is used by IPv6 solicited-node multicast addresses?
questions.explanation: As close to the destination as possible Standard ACLs should generally be applied as close to the destination of the traffic you want to filter. Because standard ACLs filter packets based only on their source IP
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: What solicited-node multicast address is generated from the unicast address df56:9098:1010:fd:ab29::f892?
questions.explanation: Data sequencing TCP provides data sequencing, ensuring that packets arrive in the correct order. UDP, being a simpler and more lightweight protocol, does not provide this feature. Both protocols support error detection (both headers include a “Checksum” field for that purpose), but only TCP ensures reliable delivery and ordered data sequencing.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which command can be used to view a list of IPv6-address-to-Layer-2-address mappings?
questions.explanation: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK The TCP three-way handshake involves a SYN packet sent from the client to the server, a SYN-ACK response from the server, and finally an ACK from the client to the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which command can be used to configure an IPv6 with a prefix learned via RA and an automatically generated interface ID?
questions.explanation: The sequence number of the next segment the host expects to receive. The Acknowledgement Number in a TCP segment indicates the sequence number of the next expected byte from the sender, effectively acknowledging receipt of all pre- ceding bytes.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch20: IPv6 Addressing

questions.question: Which of the following IPv6 static routes will not function properly, despite appearing in the routing table?
questions.explanation: Change the sequence number of ACL 100’s first ACE to 10 and add 5 for each sub- sequent ACE. The syntax for the command is {name|number} ip access-list resequence starting-seq-num increment. The command specifies ACL 100, a starting sequence number of 10, and an increment of 5.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch21: IPv6 Routing

questions.question: Which of the following protocol and port pairs are correct? (select three)
questions.explanation: 2000 D: 199 E: 2599 Extended ACLs can use numbers from 100–199 and 2000–2699. Therefore, 2000, 199, and 2599 are valid extended ACL numbers.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch21: IPv6 Routing

questions.question: Which TCP field does a host use to indicate how much data it can receive before sending an ACK?
questions.explanation: deny tcp any host 192.0.2.1 eq 443 568 appendiX d Chapter quiz answers HTTPS uses TCP port 443. When clients send requests to an HTTPS server, they use destination port (not source port) 443. So, B is correct because it denies requests des- tined for port 443 on the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch21: IPv6 Routing

questions.question: Which of the following is provided by TCP, but not UDP?
questions.explanation: permit tcp 203.0.113.1 0.0.0.0 neq 22 any SSH uses TCP port 22. This time, we are filtering the server’s responses, so the ACE must filter based on source port 22, not destination port 22—the opposite of the pre- vious example. Use (not equal to 22) to permit everything except responses to neq 22 SSH clients (which would be sourced from port 22).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch21: IPv6 Routing

questions.question: Which of the following commands permit only the specified host’s IP address? (select three)
questions.explanation: 2000 D: 199 E: 2599 Extended ACLs can use numbers from 100–199 and 2000–2699. Therefore, 2000, 199, and 2599 are valid extended ACL numbers.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch21: IPv6 Routing

questions.question: Where should standard ACLs generally be applied?
options: As close to the source as possible
As close to the destination as possible
questions.explanation: deny tcp any host 192.0.2.1 eq 443 568 appendiX d Chapter quiz answers HTTPS uses TCP port 443. When clients send requests to an HTTPS server, they use destination port (not source port) 443. So, B is correct because it denies requests des- tined for port 443 on the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch21: IPv6 Routing

questions.question: What is a basic security measure used in some wireless networks?
questions.explanation: A simple security measure that is sometimes used in combination with stronger measures is to prevent the broadcast of the SSID; this is known as network cloaking.

questions.question: What wireless standard introduced in 2013 permits a maximum speed of 6.93 Gbps?
questions.explanation: 802.11ac, which was introduced in 2013, shattered previous maximum throughput limits. This technology uses the 5 GHz band and allows a maximum speed of 6.93 Gbps. Additional Resource Additional Resource WiFi Standards (and how they work) https://youtu.be/q64AZjPfa0Y

questions.question: What is a popular single-pane-of-glass approach to managing a Cisco WLC and other key Cisco components of the network infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Cisco DNA Center is a single-pane-of-glass solution for the manage- ment of Cisco WLCs and other Cisco devices.

questions.question: What is the total throughput possible with a Cisco 9800 series WLC?
questions.explanation: 9800 series devices offer total throughput of 5 Gbps.

questions.question: Which of the following are supported QoS settings for a WLAN built by a WLC?
questions.explanation: QoS settings are possible through profiles. You can configure mobility support, marking and policing, and even prebuilt settings based on traffic classes. Additional Resource Additional Resource Fundamentals of Wireless Controllers https://youtu.be/ZFTgI-2gWsI

questions.question: If you are running WPA3 in enterprise mode, what is the required version of AES?
questions.explanation: WPA3 provides AES-192 as an option in personal mode and as a requirement in enterprise mode. Additional Resources WiFi (Wireless) Password Security - WEP, WPA, WPA2, WPS Explained https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WZaIfyvERcA Next-Gen Wi-Fi Security - WPA3 Explained https://youtu.be/aPoe4WtX2mU

questions.question: What is the IPv4 address range 224.0.0.0 to 239.255.255.255 used for?
options: To send a packet to all systems
To send a packet to a group of systems
To send a packet to a single specific system
To send multiple packets to only a single specific system
questions.explanation: 224.0.0.0 to 239.255.255.255 is the IPv4 multicast address range. This range permits the sending of a single packet to a group of machines that “subscribe” to the traffic. This is unlike a broadcast, where a single packet is sent to all systems. In IPv6, broadcast traffic is eliminated in favor of multicast.

questions.question: Which RF band is used with 802.11ac?
questions.explanation: 802.11ac uses the 5 GHz band.

questions.question: How is a playbook expressed in Ansible?
questions.explanation: Ansible playbooks are expressed in the YAML format, which was chosen, in part, for its readability. For more information, see https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/user_guide/playbooks_intro.html.

questions.question: What wireless security standard replaces PSK with SAE?
questions.explanation: WPA3 introduces many improvements over WPA2, including the replacement of the PSK authentication method.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an HTTP method used with REST APIs?
questions.explanation: SEND is not an HTTP method.

questions.question: Which of the following addresses is a private-use-only address?
Examine the access list shown here. What is the issue with this access list?
options: The access list cannot end with a deny statement.
The access list permits the traffic before it denies any traffic.
The access list is not named, and naming is required.
Standard access lists do not support logging.
questions.explanation: The order of access list statements is very important; they are processed from top to bottom. Here, the permit statement that begins the list permits all traffic before the deny statements are processed. On many IOS routers, the subsequent “deny” access control entries wouldn’t even be allowed and would generate a message indicating a conflict due to the permit any entry already being in place.

questions.question: What information for a wireless network can you choose not to broadcast?
questions.explanation: You can choose not to broadcast the name of the network (that is, the SSID). Note that not broadcasting the SSID does not add to the overall security of the implementation.

questions.question: What port security approach is considered a mix of dynamic and static configuration?
questions.explanation: Port security sticky learning means the port will initially dynamically learn the MAC addresses and add them to the running configuration. After this, this updated running config can be saved (using the command copy running-config startup-config) in the startup configuration as a static MAC address assignment for port security for when the switch reboots.

questions.question: What networking device provides instructions to APs?
questions.explanation: A wireless LAN controller is a device in a modern network that controls and manages access points.

questions.question: Which of the following is essentially a standards-based version of HSRP?
questions.explanation: VRRP is very similar to HSRP. This FHRP is standards based.

questions.question: What vty line command causes the local username database to be checked for remote access?
questions.explanation: The login local command ensures the use of the local user account’s database on the device when connecting to the vty lines.

questions.question: What will be the correct command on a Cisco router to set the IP address of an interface assuming you are at the "router(config-if)#" prompt?
questions.explanation: [CxNx] What will be the correct command on a Cisco router to set the IP address of an interface assuming you are at the "router(config-if)#" prompt? a) ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 b) ip address 1.1.1.1 c) ip 1.1.1.1 d) ip 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 e) none of the above Answer a) ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 If you are not in the configuration mode, the ip address can be set by using the setup mode. Configuring interface Ethernet0/0: Is this i

questions.question: Which commands will display the IP addresses of all interfaces on a Cisco Router?
questions.explanation: [CxNx] Which commands will display the IP addresses of all interfaces on a Cisco Router? (Choose all that apply) a) show ip route b) show ip address c) show ip interface d) show interface e) display ip addresses Answer c) show ip interface d) show interface Examples of each of these commands are shown below: Router_A#sh int e0/0 Ethernet0/0 is up, line protocol is up Hardware is AmdP2, address is 0010.7b15.bd41 (bia 0010.7b15.bd41) Internet addre

questions.question: Which of the following layers defines data representation?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Which of the following layers defines data representation? a) Application Layer 7 b) Presentation Layer 6 c) Session Layer 5 d) Transport Layer 4 e) Network Layer 3 f) Data Link Layer 2 g) Physical Layer 1 Answer b) Presentation Layer 6 Questions 1-19 | 20-39 | 40-59 | 60-71 | 72-78 Top of Page

questions.question: Which of the following layers sends and receives binary information?
Which of the following layers provides end to end connections?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Which of the following layers provides end to end connections? a) Application Layer 7 b) Presentation Layer 6 c) Session Layer 5 d) Transport Layer 4 e) Network Layer 3 f) Data Link Layer 2 g) Physical Layer 1 Answer d) Transport Layer 4

questions.question: Which of the following layers controls access to the media?
Raw data is converted to segments at which layer?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Raw data is converted to segments at which layer? a) Application Layer 7 b) Presentation Layer 6 c) Session Layer 5 d) Transport Layer 4 e) Network Layer 3 f) Data Link Layer 2 g) Physical Layer 1 Answer d) Transport Layer 4

questions.question: Which one of the following layers "best" describes the ability of the upper layers to gain independence over LAN media access?
On an IP over Token Ring network, which answer best describes the role of ARP?
options: ARP is used to map an IP address
ARP is used to map Ring numbers to IP addresses.
ARP is used to map Ring numbers to MAC addresses of the hosts on that ring.
ARP is used to map a MAC address to an IP address.
questions.explanation: [RINx] On an IP over Token Ring network, which answer best describes the role of ARP? (Choose one.) a) ARP is used to map an IP address to a MAC address. b) ARP is used to map Ring numbers to IP addresses. c) ARP is used to map Ring numbers to MAC addresses of the hosts on that ring. d) ARP is used to map a MAC address to an IP address. e) ARP is not used on Token Ring networks. Answer a) ARP is used to map an IP address to a MAC address. Address

questions.question: Which of the following are true of an Appletalk address?
options: The address has a network and a host part.
The node part is usually the MAC address of the host.
questions.explanation: [RANx] Which of the following are true of an Appletalk address? a) The total address length is 24 bits. b) The address has a network and a host part. c) It is hierarchical in nature. d) The node part is usually the MAC address of the host. e) All of the above. Answer a) the total address length is 24 bits b) the address has a network and a host part The following is a portion of a router configuration: interface Serial1 no ip address appletalk ca

questions.question: The establishment of a window during a session setup allows for which of the following?
options: Multiple data segments to be sent
Multiple frame types to be sent over the same media type.
Multiple data segments to be sent before the
Separate data streams from different hosts to a common host.
questions.explanation: [OxNx] The establishment of a window during a session setup allows for which of the following? (Choose two.) a) Multiple data segments to be sent before the sender requires an acknowledgement. b) Multiple frame types to be sent over the same media type. c) Multiple data segments to be sent before the receiver requires an acknowledgement. d) Separate data streams from different hosts to a common host. e) More efficient use of the available bandwid

questions.question: A lightweight access point in which one of the following architectures participate?
questions.explanation: Split-MAC is the correct answer. On a lightweight AP, the MAC function is divided between the AP hardware and the WLC. The LAP-WLC division of labor is known as split-MAC architecture. The Split MAC architecture divides the implementation of the MAC functions between the AP and the controller. The 802.11 AP at its simplest level is the 802.11 radio MAC layer providing bridging to a wired network for the WLAN client as- sociated to the AP Basic Se

questions.question: Which of the following wireless security tools is used to protect the integrity of data in a wireless frame?
questions.explanation: MIC is the correct answer. A message integrity check (MIC) is a security tool that can protect against data tampering. You can think of a MIC as a way for the sender to add a secret 275 stamp inside the encrypted data frame. The stamp is based on the contents of the data bits to be transmitted. Once the recipient decrypts the frame, it can compare the se- cret stamp to its own idea of what the stamp should be, based on the data bits that were rec

questions.question: Which of the following bridges can be used to provide wireless connectivity to a non-wireless device?
questions.explanation: Workgroup bridge is the correct answer. A workgroup bridge acts as a wireless client, but bridges traf- fic to and from a wired device connected to it. In workgroup bridge mode, the device associates to another access point as a client and provides a network connection for the equipment connected to its Ethernet port. For example, if you need to provide wireless connectivity for a group of network printers, you can connect the printers to a hub o

questions.question: Which controller interface type maps a WLAN to a VLAN?
questions.explanation: Dynamic interface is the correct answer. Cisco controllers support the following interface types: 1. Dynamic interface: Used to connect a VLAN to a WLAN. 2. Management interface: Used for normal management traf- fic, such as RADIUS user authentication, WLC-to-WLC com- munication, web-based, and SSH sessions, SNMP, Network Time Protocol (NTP), syslog, and so on. The management in- terface is also used to terminate CAPWAP tunnels between the contro

questions.question: You are creating a new WLAN with the controller GUI, which of the following parameters are necessary? (Choose two)
questions.explanation: SSID and Interface are the correct an- swers. The SSID and controller interface are the only parame- ters from the list that are necessary in order to create a new WLAN.

questions.question: When FlexConnect local switching is enabled for a WLAN, which device is responsible for switching traffic between the 802.11 and Ethernet networks?
questions.explanation: 64 characters The WPA2 PSK can be configured as 64 hexadecimal characters. Alternatively, it can be configured as 8–63 ASCII characters.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which LWAP mode is dedicated to analyzing traffic on the wired LAN?
questions.explanation: Silver The Silver QoS level is the default and is used for best-effort treatment of traffic. The other levels are: Platinum (voice), Gold (video), and Bronze (background).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: In Cisco Meraki’s cloud-based AP solution, which traffic is tunneled to the Meraki cloud?
questions.explanation: Client Band Select Client Band Select is a feature that delays responses to 2.4 GHz probe requests to encourage clients to connect to the 5 GHz band, which is usually less crowded.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which WLC deployment option uses a dedicated hardware appliance?
questions.explanation: Passthrough D: Conditional Web Redirect Passthrough and Conditional Web Redirect are both Layer-3 WLAN security settings. Passthrough allows the user to access the network after accepting certain terms and conditions or viewing a mandatory message. Conditional Web Redirect redirects the user to a specific web page under certain conditions (i.e. their password is expired).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which of the following functions are centrally controlled in a split-MAC architecture? (select two)
questions.explanation: Dynamic interface Dynamic interfaces on a Cisco WLC are used to map WLANs to VLANs. Each dynamic interface is mapped to a VLAN on the wired network and a corresponding WLAN on the wireless network.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which statement about CAPWAP is true?
options: The CAPWAP control tunnel is encrypted by default.
The CAPWAP data tunnel is encrypted by default.
Both CAPWAP tunnels are encrypted by default.
questions.explanation: Wireless The Wireless tab in the Cisco WLC GUI is where you configure settings related to the LWAPs managed by the WLC.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit of cloud-based network management?
options: Highly customizable to meet complex networking requirements
Simplified management via a web-based dashboard
questions.explanation: All DS ports When you enable link aggregation group (LAG), all DS ports are included in the LAG. However, not all DS ports need to be active; as long as there is one functioning physical port in the LAG, it will be operational.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which 802.11 security protocol was developed to overcome the vulnerabilities of WEP?
questions.explanation: HTTP D: HTTPS The graphical user interface (GUI) of a Cisco wireless LAN controller (WLC) can be accessed via HTTP or HTTPS in a web browser. Telnet and SSH are used for remote access to the command-line interface (CLI), and the console port can be used to access the CLI with a console cable.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which authentication methods are available in WEP? (select two)
questions.explanation: Distribution system port A WLC’s distribution system (DS) ports are the standard network ports that connect to the DS—the switched Ethernet LAN. User data traffic is sent from and received on these ports.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which WPA3 feature protects the PSK against brute-force attacks?
questions.explanation: Service port The service port on a Cisco WLC is used for out-of-band (OOB) management—connecting to and managing the WLC via a dedicated connection that is separate from the DS ports.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which of the following use AES counter mode encryption? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: AP-manager interface The AP-manager interface on a Cisco WLC is used to establish CAPWAP tunnels with LWAPs. By default, the management interface acts as the AP-manager interface, but you can optionally configure a separate AP-manager interface. For this reason, option D is more correct than option C; although the management interface sometimes acts as the AP-manager interface, that is not always the case.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which protocol is used to carry EAP messages between a supplicant and authenticator?
questions.explanation: Service-port interface The service-port interface corresponds to the physical service port and is used for OOB management. The management interface, on the other hand, is the default interface for in-band management via the DS ports.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which WPA3 feature ensures that a compromised PSK cannot be used to decrypt past communications?
questions.explanation: REST In the northbound interface (NBI) of an SDN controller, representational state transfer (REST) APIs are commonly used, allowing applications to programmatically interact with the controller.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which of the following uses 802.1X authentication?
questions.explanation: SBI The southbound interface (SBI) of an SDN controller is used to communicate with the managed devices. The name comes from how SDN architecture is usually illustrated, with the Application Layer at the top, the Control Layer in the middle, and the Infrastructure Layer (where the managed devices are located) at the bottom.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which of the following employs SAE?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure Layer The Infrastructure Layer in SDN architecture contains the physical and virtual network devices responsible for forwarding messages across the network. This layer is where the Data Plane’s forwarding of messages takes place.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which protocol does the original WPA standard employ for data encryption and integrity?
questions.explanation: ACI Cisco’s Application-Centric Infrastructure (ACI) uses a spine-leaf architecture, optimizing east-west communication between servers in a data center.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which protocol does the WPA3 standard employ for the most secure data encryption and integrity?
questions.explanation: SSH B: NTP Secure Shell (SSH) and Network Time Protocol (NTP) are protocols used in the management layer of network systems. SSH provides secure access to the CLI of network devices, and NTP ensures accurate time synchronization across the network.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which of the following methods can be used to connect to the GUI of a Cisco WLC? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Control Plane The Control Plane is responsible for the exchange of routing protocol messages, like BGP, between routers. This plane handles the logic needed to build and maintain routing tables and other tables necessary for forwarding messages.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC port type is used to carry user data traffic?
questions.explanation: Control Plane The Control Plane in a switch is responsible for examining the source MAC addresses of incoming frames and creating MAC address table entries, which are used for making forwarding decisions. The forwarding decisions themselves are the role of the Data Plane.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC port type is used for OOB management of the WLC over the network?
questions.explanation: Data Plane The Data Plane (or Forwarding Plane) in a switch is responsible for examining the destination MAC addresses of incoming frames and making forwarding or flooding decisions based on this information. Any activities related to the actual forwarding of messages are the responsibility of the Data Plane.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC interface type is used to establish CAPWAP tunnels with LWAPs?
questions.explanation: Management plane B: Control plane C: Data plane In a traditional network architecture, all three planes are distributed. Each network device is managed individually, builds its own tables for forwarding traffic, and makes independent forwarding decisions.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC interface type is used for OOB management of the WLC over the network?
questions.explanation: The Data Plane is distributed D: The Control Plane is centralized In software-defined networking (SDN) architecture, the Data Plane remains distributed; each network device is responsible for forwarding messages across the network. The Control Plane, on the other hand, is centralized in the SDN controller, which is responsible for providing instructions about how to forward messages to the network devices.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which advanced WLAN feature encourages clients to associate with less-busy LWAPs?
questions.explanation: REST APIs are stateless. C: REST uses a client-server architecture. REST APIs are stateless, meaning that each request is a separate event, independent of all past exchanges between the client and server. Each request from the client must contain all the information needed to understand and process the request. REST is based on a client-server architecture in which the client and server are independent of each other.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch20: Wireless Sec

questions.question: When configuring a hexadecimal WPA2 PSK in the Cisco WLC GUI, how many characters must the PSK be?
questions.explanation: SBI The southbound interface (SBI) of an SDN controller is used to communicate with the managed devices. The name comes from how SDN architecture is usually illustrated, with the Application Layer at the top, the Control Layer in the middle, and the Infrastructure Layer (where the managed devices are located) at the bottom.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch20: Wireless Sec

questions.question: Which QoS level is assigned to a WLAN by default, offering best-effort treatment of traffic?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure Layer The Infrastructure Layer in SDN architecture contains the physical and virtual network devices responsible for forwarding messages across the network. This layer is where the Data Plane’s forwarding of messages takes place.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch20: Wireless Sec

questions.question: Which advanced WLAN feature delays responses to 2.4 GHz probes to encourage clients to use 5 GHz instead?
questions.explanation: ACI Cisco’s Application-Centric Infrastructure (ACI) uses a spine-leaf architecture, optimizing east-west communication between servers in a data center.

questions.question: A Layer 2 switch configuration places all its physical ports into VLAN 2. The IP addressing plan shows that address 175.28.1.150 (with mask 255.255.255.0) is reserved for use by this new LAN switch and that 175.28.1.254 is already configured on the router connected to that same VLAN. The switch needs to support SSH connections into the switch 52 from any subnet in the network. Which of the following com- mands are part of the required configuration in this case? (Choose two answers.)
options: The switch cannot support SSH because all its ports connect to VLAN 2, and the IP address must be configured on interface VLAN 1.
The ip address 175.28.1.150 255.255.255.0 com- mand in interface vlan 2 configuration mode.
The ip default-gateway 175.28.1.254 command in global configuration mode.
The ip address 172.16.2.250 255.255.255.0 command in interface vlan 1 configuration mode.
questions.explanation: B and C are the correct answers. To allow SSH or Telnet access, a switch must have a correct IP configu- ration. That includes the configuration of a correct IP address and mask on a VLAN interface. That VLAN interface must have a path out of the switch via ports assigned to that VLAN. In this case, with all ports assigned to VLAN 2, the switch must use in- terface VLAN 2 (using the interface vlan 2 configuration com- mand).

questions.question: Which of the following is a security best practice related to VLANs?
options: Leave VLAN 1 as the native VLAN but don’t assign hosts to it.
A port’s native VLAN should be the same as its access VLAN.
Configure a non-default unused VLAN as the native VLAN.
The default and native VLANs should be the same.
questions.explanation: Configure a non-default unused VLAN as the native VLAN. A security best practice for VLANs is to configure a non-default, unused VLAN as the native VLAN on trunk ports. This helps mitigate security exploits related to the native VLAN; it is recommended that you do not use the native VLAN in your networks.

The VLAN ID doesn’t have to match the subinterface ID (i.e. GigabitEth- ernet0/1.10), but it’s best to match them for simplicity’s sake.
