The switch must have both an access port associated with the VLAN and a
questions.explanation: The switch must have both an access port associated with the VLAN and a trunk port that allows the VLAN. For a switch virtual interface (SVI) to be in an up/up state, the switch must have either an access port associated with the VLAN or a trunk port that allows the VLAN; it is not necessary to have both. questions.question: When you connect an end host to a switch port without PortFast, how long does it take for the port to enter the STP forwarding state?
questions.explanation: Edge An edge port in RSTP connects to an end device (like a PC or server)—not to another switch. If an edge port’s status changes (goes up or down), it does not trigger a topology change notification because it does not affect the RSTP topology among the switches.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch14: STP

questions.question: What happens when a switch port configured with spanning-tree bpduguard receives a BPDU? enable
options: BPDU Guard is disabled on the port.
The port immediately moves to the forwarding state.
questions.explanation: Backup In RSTP, if the root bridge has multiple ports connected to the same segment via a hub, one port will be the designated port and the others will be backup ports. The backup port role is used for ports that provide a redundant path to the same segment as another port on the same switch. This is an exception to the general rule that all ports on the root bridge must be designated.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch14: STP

questions.question: Two switches, SW1 and SW2, are connected by two links using the default STP cost values. SW2 is the root bridge. SW1 G0/1 is connected to SW2 G0/2, and SW1 G0/2 is connected to SW2 G0/1. Which port will SW1 select as its root port?
questions.explanation: spanning-tree portfast The command is used to configure an RSTP edge port. spanning-tree portfast Unlike the point-to-point and shared link types, you cannot use the spanning-tree command to configure an edge port. link-type
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch14: STP

questions.question: SW3 and SW4 are part of a larger LAN. They are connected by their G0/1 ports, neither of which is a root port. Both switches have equal root costs. How will they determine which port becomes designated and which becomes non-designated?
options: The port with the lowest port ID will become designated.
The port with the highest port ID will become designated.
The port on the switch with the lowest BID will become designated.
The port on the switch with the highest BID will become designated.
questions.explanation: The port on the switch with the lowest BID will become designated. When two switches have equal root costs, the switch with the lower BID will have its port become the designated port. The other switch’s port will be non-designated (blocking).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch14: STP

questions.question: Which STP port role points away from the root bridge and is in a forwarding state?
questions.explanation: Designated STP root and designated ports are in a forwarding state once the LAN has converged. Root ports point toward the root bridge; they provide each switch’s active path to the root bridge. Designated ports, on the other hand, point away from the root bridge. Each root port always connects to a designated port.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch14: STP

questions.question: Which command is most likely to ensure that a switch becomes the root bridge for VLAN 1?
questions.explanation: spanning-tree vlan 1 priority 0 The best way to ensure that a switch becomes the root bridge for a particular VLAN is to use vlan-id to make its bridge priority 0—the spanning-tree vlan priority 0 lowest possible value. This way, it can only be usurped by another switch with a lower MAC address that also has its priority set to 0. Chapter 15: Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch14: STP

questions.question: The output of show spanning–tree on a Cisco switch states Spanning tree enabled protocol ieee. Which version of STP is the switch running?
questions.explanation: PVST+ Spanning tree enabled protocol ieee means that the switch is running Cis- co’s Per-VLAN Spanning Tree Plus (PVST+), not IEEE-standard STP. If the switch is 554 appendiX d Chapter quiz answers running Rapid PVST+, it will state Spanning tree enabled protocol rstp. Cisco switches don’t run the IEEE-standard versions of these protocols.

questions.question: If the RSTP sync mechanism succeeds, how long does it take for a port to transi- tion from the discarding state to the forwarding state?
questions.explanation: It transitions immediately. In RSTP, when the sync mechanism succeeds, a port can transition from the discarding state to the forwarding state immediately, bypassing the 30 second wait that is necessary in classic STP. This is the primary advantage of RSTP.

questions.explanation: On the port-channel interface In a Layer-3 EtherChannel, IP addressing is configured on the port-channel interface rather than on the individual member ports. This setup allows the EtherChannel to function as a single logical interface at Layer 3.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: SW1’s G0/1 port is in the up/up state but has stopped receiving BPDUs from its RSTP neighbor. How long will SW1 wait before reacting and initiating a topology change?
questions.explanation: 6 seconds In RSTP, if a switch stops receiving BPDUs on a port, it waits for three times the Hello time before initiating a topology change. The default Hello time is 2 seconds, so the switch will wait 6 seconds (3*2 seconds) before reacting and initiating a topology change. Note that the switch will only wait 6 seconds if the port remains up/up; if a hardware failure or something else causes the port to go down, the switch will react immediately
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which RSTP link type is default on full-duplex ports?
questions.explanation: Point-to-point Full-duplex ports use the RSTP point-to-point link type by default; this is the only link type that can use RSTP’s sync mechanism for rapid convergence. This link type should be used for direct connections between two switches.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Four switches are interconnected, with a total of six links between them. How many of each RSTP port role are there?
questions.explanation: 3 root, 6 designated, 3 alternate Because there are 6 links, there are a total of 12 ports (2 connected to each link). There are 4 switches, and each non-root switch must have 1 root port, so there are 3 root ports. Each link must have one designated port, so there are 6 designated ports. The remaining ports are alternate ports - they enter the discarding state to prevent loops. So, there are 3 alternate ports. There are no backup ports; hubs are
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which optional STP feature disables a port if it stops receiving BPDUs?
questions.explanation: Loop Guard When a Loop Guard-enabled port stops receiving BPDUs, it will enter the loop-incon- sistent state, effectively disabling the port. This protects against loops caused by a port erroneously transitioning to the forwarding state.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: BPDU Filter has been enabled globally for all PortFast-enabled ports. What hap- pens when one of the BPDU Filter-enabled ports receives a BPDU?
options: PortFast and BPDU Filter are disabled on the port.
The port enters the discarding state.
questions.explanation: PortFast and BPDU Filter are disabled on the port. BPDU Filter can be enabled in two ways: in interface config mode with spanning-tree bpdufilter enable, or globally on all PortFast-enabled ports with spanning-tree portfast bpdufilter default. When enabled in interface config mode, the port doesn’t send BPDUs and ignores all received BPDUs. When enabled globally, the port doesn’t send BPDUs, but if it receives a BPDU, PortFast and BPDU Filter wil
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which of the following statements about EtherChannel is false?
options: EtherChannel combines multiple physical links into a single logical link.
EtherChannel eliminates the need for STP in a LAN.
A frame flooded out of a port-channel interface will only be flooded out of
EtherChannel formation can be dynamically negotiated or statically
questions.explanation: EtherChannel eliminates the need for STP in a LAN. EtherChannel does not eliminate the need for STP in a LAN. While EtherChannel can aggregate multiple physical links into a single logical link, reducing the number of ports that need to be blocked by STP, STP still plays a critical role in the larger network topology for loop prevention. 556 appendiX d Chapter quiz answers
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which of the following dynamic EtherChannel mode combinations will result in a valid EtherChannel? (select two)
questions.explanation: passive + active D: desirable + desirable When using Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP), the mode combinations that will result in a valid EtherChannel are passive+active and active+active. When using Port Aggregation Protocol (PAgP), they are auto+desirable and desirable+desirable. PAgP and LACP modes don’t mix; a switch using PAgP won’t form an EtherChannel with a switch using LACP.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which of the following settings doesn’t have to match among the member ports of an EtherChannel?
questions.explanation: Description A description doesn’t affect a port’s operation, so each member port of an EtherChan- nel can have a unique description. However, settings like speed, duplex, and access/ trunk mode must match among all ports in an EtherChannel.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which dynamic EtherChannel modes can form a valid EtherChannel with a neighbor configured with ? channel-group 1 mode on
questions.explanation: None of the above Just as PAgP and LACP modes can’t form an EtherChannel with each other, neither can form an EtherChannel with a switch configured to use static EtherChannel. To form a working static EtherChannel, both sides must be configured with . mode on
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch15: RSTP

questions.question: Which protocol can be used to negotiate an EtherChannel with a non-Cisco switch?
questions.explanation: AD When routes to the same destination are learned via different routing protocols, the administrative distance (AD) is used to determine the preferred route. The route with the lowest AD is selected. Each routing protocol calculates route metric values differ- ently, so they can’t be directly compared.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch16: EtherChannel

questions.question: Port-channel 1 and its member ports currently operate in access mode. What will happen after you configure on the port-channel 1 switchport mode trunk interface?
options: The port-channel is disabled until you configure trunk mode on the member
The configuration is inherited by the member ports.
The member ports are disabled until you configure trunk mode on them.
questions.explanation: EIGRP = 90 C: OSPF = 110 Here are some default AD values in Cisco IOS: connected = 0, static = 1, External BGP (EBGP) = 20, EIGRP = 90, OSPF = 110, IS-IS = 115, RIP = 120, Internal BGP (IBGP) = 200, unusable = 255.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch16: EtherChannel

questions.question: When connecting two switches with a Layer-3 EtherChannel, where should each switch’s IP address be configured?
options: On both the port-channel interface and the member ports
questions.explanation: On the port-channel interface In a Layer-3 EtherChannel, IP addressing is configured on the port-channel interface rather than on the individual member ports. This setup allows the EtherChannel to function as a single logical interface at Layer 3. Chapter 17: Dynamic routing
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch16: EtherChannel

questions.question: Which type of dynamic routing protocol is used to exchange routing information within an AS?
questions.explanation: IGP Interior gateway protocols (IGPs) are used to exchange routing information within an autonomous system (AS). Examples include OSPF, EIGRP, RIP, and IS-IS. This is in contrast to exterior gateway protocols (EGPs) like BGP, which are used to exchange routing information between ASs.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch16: EtherChannel

questions.question: Which of the following dynamic routing protocols use a link-state algorithm? (select two)
questions.explanation: IS-IS C: OSPF IS-IS and OSPF are both link-state routing protocols. They maintain a complete map of the network topology to make routing decisions.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch16: EtherChannel

questions.question: Which type of routing protocol is also known as “routing by rumor”?
questions.explanation: Distance-vector Distance-vector routing protocols are often referred to as “routing by rumor” because the router doesn’t build a complete map of the network; it only knows what its neigh- bors tell it.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch16: EtherChannel

questions.question: If a router cannot find a better match, what might the router use to route traffic?
questions.explanation: Routers use the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to send packets that have no other better match in the routing table.

questions.question: When a router forwards packets onto Ethernet, what is rewritten?
options: The source and destination IP addresses
The source and destination MAC addresses
questions.explanation: The router must rewrite the source and destination MAC addresses in the Ethernet header before forwarding a frame.

questions.question: What does the routing protocol code B indicate in the routing table?
What command permits you to view the IPv6 routing table?
questions.explanation: The show ipv6 route command is the equivalent of show ip route but shows the IPv6 routing table.

questions.question: What type of static route is shown in the following example?
questions.explanation: This static route is for a network that needs to be reached. The network is 172.16.1.0/24, and the next hop address to reach that network is 192.168.2.100.

questions.question: What command enters router configuration mode for OSPF version 2?
questions.explanation: The router ospf 1 command enters router configuration mode for OSPF. It uses the local process ID 1.

questions.question: You have configured OSPF on a router by using the command network 10.10.0.0 0.0.255.255 area 0. On which interface is OSPF running?
questions.explanation: The network 10.10.0.0 0.0.255.255 area 0 command ensures that OSPF runs on any interfaces that have IP addresses that have 10.10 in the first two octets. This is the Gi0/1 interface in this question.

questions.question: What does not have to match in order for an OSPF neighborship to form?
options: Use of the network command versus the ip ospf command
questions.explanation: Neighborships can form in OSPF if one router uses the network command and the other uses the interface-level ip ospf command. Process IDs do not need to match between routers either.

questions.question: You have allowed a router to self-assign its router ID. What is the first option considered for the assignment on the device?
options: The IP address on the highest-numbered interface name
The highest IP address on a physical interface
The highest IP address on a loopback interface
questions.explanation: If you do not manually configure a router ID, the highest IP address on a loopback interface is used as the router ID. If there are no loopback interfaces, the router uses the highest IP address on a physical interface. If OSPF cannot find a configured IPv4 address, the OSPF process does not start.

questions.question: Which network type in OSPF features the use of a DR and a BDR?
questions.explanation: The DR and BDR devices are used in the broadcast and non- broadcast network types. For instructions on how to download and use the Packet Tracer files, see “Packet Tracer Hands-On Lab Practice Assignments” in the Introduction of thi

questions.question: How is the load sharing of GLBP ultimately accomplished?
options: Using a round robin approach based on the physical IP addresses of gateways
Using the routing table of the IGP
questions.explanation: Virtual MAC addresses are used to distribute the load from clients among healthy routers in the infrastructure.

questions.question: In HSRP, what is the term for routers that are not forwarding traffic for the LAN but are waiting to see if they are needed?
questions.explanation: HSRP uses the concept of active routers and standby routers.

questions.question: In VRRP, what is the name of the router that is forwarding traffic for the clients?
questions.explanation: The forwarding device in VRRP is termed the virtual router master. questions.question: What command identifies the inside NAT interface?
questions.explanation: The ip nat inside command identifies the inside NAT interface.

questions.question: What command allows you to view the NAT translations at the CLI?
questions.explanation: The show ip nat translations command allows you to see all the translations currently on the device.

questions.question: Examine the following command: ip nat inside source list 1 interface fa0/0 overload. What is the inside global address for translation?
options: The IP address on interface fa0/0
The virtual address on interface fa0/0
The address in the NAT pool named interface
questions.explanation: The IP address on the interface specified here is the inside global address. questions.question: Examine the topology shown in the figure. If OSPF, EIGRP, and RIPv2 were all enabled on each interface on each router, which routing protocol would R4 use to determine the best route to 10.77.67.0/24?
options: EIGRP because its metric considers bandwidth and delay
OSPF because its cost is derived from bandwidth and delay
OSPF because it is an advanced distance-vector routing protocol
questions.explanation: Here EIGRP is preferred due to its lower administrative distance.

questions.question: Which command displays a quick view of OSPF neighbor status?
questions.explanation: You use show ip ospf neighbor to quickly verify peer OSPF devices. Answer Key to Practice Exam 1

questions.question: What command permits you to see your locally used OSPF routes?
options: show ospf routes local Practice Exam 1
questions.explanation: Use show ip route ospf to see the OSPF routes in your local routing table.

questions.question: Which of the following does not need to match when configuring OSPF version 2 peering?
questions.explanation: Many values must match in order for an OSPF peering to form. The local process ID is not one of them.

questions.question: OSPF is properly configured and working on both routers shown in the depicted network. The following is added to Router X: ip route 10.255.0.128 255.255.255.224 10.255.0.75 89. What will be the result?
options: The static route will have an administrative distance that is better than that of OSPF.
There will be a Layer 2 loop.
Router X will attempt to send to PC 1 packets destined to subnet 10.255.0.128/27.
A broadcast storm will occur. Practice Exam 2
questions.explanation: Here the AD of the static route (89) is less (better) than the default AD of OSPF, which is 110. Note that unless an exam question explicitly states otherwise, you must consider only default settings. As a result of the static route, packets destined to 10.255.0.128/27 will be forwarded to the router 10.255.0.75 (not shown in the diagram, so it might not exist). As long as there is no ARP reply from the router at 10.255.0.75 (the static route’s target), Router X will keep sen

questions.question: What two options exist for the next-hop information in a static route?
questions.explanation: The next-hop property can take an IP address of the next router or the local exit interface of the sending router. Setting a multi-access interface (such as an Ethernet interface) as a next hop is not recommended and should be avoided. Just because you can do something doesn’t mean you should.

questions.question: Which of the following explains the correct definition of administrative distance as it pertains to Cisco’s IOS?
options: The process where a router will select the best path to a destination network.
The process where a router will distance itself from other routers due to it’s
The process where a router will prioritize routing protocols so that in the event two

options: IP is to TCP. as IPX is to SPX.
RTMP is to Appletalk as IP RIP is to IP.

Answer i) a, b and c are true.

questions.question: Which of the following would NOT appear in a routing table?
questions.explanation: [RxNx] Which of the following would NOT appear in a routing table? a) Next hop address b) Interface Port references c) MAC address d) Network address e) Metrics Answer c) MAC address A router is a device that operates at layer 3 where logical addresses are used, not physical addresses. A switch or a bridge would contain MAC addresses in the bridge table.

questions.question: What is the default route on an IP network?
options: This is the route that will be
The address of the nearest downstream neighbor in a Token ring network.
At the router, it is the route used to direct frames for which the next hop has not
Any static route listing in a routing table.
questions.explanation: [CxNx] What is the default route on an IP network? Choose one. a) This is the route that will be chosen first by IP for delivery of a datagram. b) The address of the nearest downstream neighbor in a Token ring network. c) At the router, it is the route used to direct frames for which the next hop has not been explicitly listed in the routing table. d) Any static route listing in a routing table. e) When all conditions are equal, the best route fo

questions.explanation: [RxNx] What routing protocols are configured? a) EIGRP, IGRP, RIP b) IGRP RTMP IPX NLSP c) IPX RIP, IGRP, RTMP d) EIGRP, RTMP, IPX RIP e) IPX RIP, IGRP Answer c) IPX RIP, IGRP, RTMP Appletalk's RTMP and Novell's IPX RIP are the default routing protocols. questions.question: Which statement best describes a "Triggered Update"?
options: A method of setting a specific
A triggered update is a new routing table that is sent to neighbor routers based upon
Triggered updates in conjunction with hold-down timers help prevent routing loops
Triggered updates cannot be used if hold-downs are also being used.

(Choose two.) a) A method of setting a specific time for route tables to be exchanged. b) A triggered update is a new routing table that is sent to neighbor routers based upon some change. c) Triggered updates in conjunction with hold-down timers help prevent routing loops d) Triggered updates cannot be used if hold-downs are also being used. e) With triggered updates, nodes are prevente

questions.question: Which one of the following is true as it pertains to IPX RIP Load Balancing?
options: The parallel paths must have the
The parallel paths must have the same bandwidth.
The parallel paths must have the same clock rate.
The parallel paths must have the same tick count and the same hop count.

Answer d) The parallel paths must have the same tick count and the same hop count. IPX RIP uses TICK and HOP co

questions.question: A network engineer has configured a Layer 3 142 switch with SVIs for VLANs 4 and 5. Hosts in the subnets using VLANs 4 and 5 can ping each other with the Layer 3 switch routing the packets. The next week, the network engineer re- ceives a call that those same users can no longer ping each other. If the problem is with the Layer 3 switching function, which of the following could have caused the problem? (Choose two answers.)
options: A shutdown command issued from VLAN 4 configu- ration mode
VTP on the switch removing VLAN 5 from the switch’s VLAN list
1 out of 10 working VLAN 4 access ports failing due to physical problems
questions.explanation: A and B are the correct answers. A Layer 3 switch will not route packets on a VLAN interface unless it is in an up/up state. A VLAN interface will only be up/up if the matching VLAN (with the same VLAN number) exists on the switch. If VTP deletes the VLAN, then the VLAN interface moves to a down/down state, and the routing on that interface stop. Also, disabling VLAN 4 with the shutdown command in VLAN 143 configuration mode also causes the match

questions.question: Which of the following commands lists the router’s entire routing table?
questions.explanation: show ip route is the correct answer.

questions.question: You are connected on a router R1, Which com- mand will you type the command to connect to R2 using SSH. Use the following login credentials in order to get access to R2. R2’s IP address: 145.167.2.1 username: userexamsdigest password: passexamsdigest
questions.explanation: A is the correct answer. The ssh -l user- name host command is used to connect on the R2 using SSH client. The -l flag means that the next parameter is the login user- name. In this case, the user begins logged in to Router R1 and then uses the ssh -l userexamsdigest 145.167.2.1 command to SSH to Router R2. R2 expects a username/password of userex- amsdigest/passexamsdigest, with userexamsdigest supplied in the command and passexamsdigest supplie

questions.question: After configuring a working router interface with IP address/mask 10.1.1.100/26, which of the following routes would you expect to see in the output of the show ip route command? (Choose two answers.)
questions.explanation: A and C are the correct answers. First, for the subnetting math, address 10.1.1.100 with mask /26 im- plies a subnet ID of 10.1.1.64. Also, mask /26 converts to a DDN mask of 255.255.255.192. For any working router inter- face, after adding the ip address command to configure an 153 address and mask, the router adds a connected route for the subnet. In this case, the router adds a connected route for subnet 10.1.1.64 255.255.255.192. The router a

questions.question: You are responsible to configure a static route for the network 156.187.45.0/24 using the IP address 156.187.80.45 as the next-hop IP. Which command will you type to meet the requirement?
questions.explanation: D is the correct answer. The correct syn- tax lists a subnet number, then a subnet mask in dotted-deci- mal form, and then either an outgoing interface or a next-hop IP address. The command to configure a static route for the network 156.187.45.0/24 using the IP address 156.187.80.45 as the next-hop IP is: ip route 156.187.45.0 255.255.255.0 156.187.80.45 155 156 CHAPTER 5 OSPF ROUTING PROTOCOL Questions 106-120

Codes: L – local, C – connected, S – static, R – RIP, M – mobile, B – BGP D – EIGRP, EX – EIGRP external, O – OSPF, IA – OSPF inter area N1 – OSPF NSSA external type 1, N2 – OSPF NSSA external type 2 E1 – OSPF external type 1, E2 – OSPF external type 2 ! Additional lines omitted for brevity Gateway of last resort is not set 10.0.0.0/8 is variably subnetted, 9 subnets, 2 masks O 10.1.1.0/24 [110/2] via 10.1.14.1, 00:19:24, GigabitEthernet0/0/0 O 10.1.2.0/24 [110/2] via 10.1.14.1, 00:19:24, GigabitEthernet0/0/0 C 10.1.4.0/24 is directly connected, Vlan3 L 10.1.4.4/32 is directly connected, Vlan3 O 10.1.12.0/24 [110/2] via 10.1.14.1, 00:17:24, GigabitEthernet0/0/0 168 O 10.1.13.0/24 [110/2] via 10.1.14.1, 00:14:15, GigabitEthernet0/0/0 C 10.1.14.0/24 is directly connected, GigabitEthernet0/0/0 L 10.1.14.4/32 is directly connected, GigabitEthernet0/0/0 O 10.1.23.0/24 [110/3] via 10.1.14.1, 00:15:35, GigabitEthernet0/0/0
questions.explanation: show ip route is the correct answer.

questions.question: When multiple routes to the same destination network are learned via the same routing protocol, which parameter is used to select which route(s) will enter the routing table?
questions.explanation: 192.168.222.127 To calculate this, write the final octet in binary: 0d101 = 0b01100101. Then, change the bits of the host portion (the last 6 bits) all to 1: 01111111. Then convert back to deci- mal: 0b01111111 = 0d127. Therefore, the broadcast address is 192.168.222.127.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch9: Routing Fundamentals

questions.question: When multiple routes to the same destination network are learned via different routing protocols, which parameter is used to select which route(s) will enter the routing table?
questions.explanation: 4096 subnets A /12 address block has 20 host bits (32 − 12 = 20). To create subnets that support at least 200 usable host addresses, 8 host bits are needed (28 − 2 = 254). That leaves 12 bits that you can borrow to make subnets (20 − 8 = 12), allowing for 212 = 4096 subnets.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch9: Routing Fundamentals

questions.question: Which of the following accurately represent IOS default AD values? (select two)
questions.explanation: /25 Making 1024 subnets requires 10 borrowed bits (since 210 = 1024). Therefore, the answer is /25, adding 10 to the original /15 prefix length.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch9: Routing Fundamentals

questions.question: What kind of static route acts as a backup for another route, only entering the routing table if the more preferred route is removed from the routing table?
questions.explanation: ip route 5.0.0.0 255.0.0.0 gigabitethernet0/1 This route relies on proxy ARP because it specifies only the exit interface; it’s a directly connected static route. This makes the router behave as if it were directly connected to the destination, and it will therefore send an ARP request directly to the destination IP address (instead of to the next-hop router’s IP address). This will only work if the next- hop router has proxy ARP enabled and can
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch9: Routing Fundamentals

questions.question: A router learns the following routes via manual configuration and dynamic rout- ing protocols. Which route(s) will it insert into its routing table? (select all that apply)
options: router learns the following routes via manual configuration and dynamic rout-
questions.explanation: Collisions D: Late collisions If there is a duplex mismatch and the connected devices transmit at the same time, the half-duplex device will interpret it as a collision, which can lead to incrementing colli- sion and late collision counters. Chapter 9: Routing fundamentals
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch9: Routing Fundamentals

questions.question: R1 is part of an OSPF network, and all of its OSPF-enabled interfaces are in area 1. Furthermore, it connects to the Internet and advertises a default route to other OSPF routers. What OSPF router types apply to R1? (select all that apply).
questions.explanation: /29 To create 16 subnets, you need to borrow 4 bits from the host portion (since 24 = 16). This results in a /29 prefix length, adding 4 to the original /25 prefix length.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch9: Routing Fundamentals

questions.question: A router receives a packet destined for 192.0.2.29. It has the following four routes in its routing table. Which route will it select to forward the packet?
options: router receives a packet destined for 192.0.2.29. It has the following four routes
questions.explanation: R 192.0.2.0/27 [120/5] via 10.0.0.2 When selecting which route should be used to forward a particular packet, the router only considers one thing: the most specific matching route. AD and metric are not con- sidered. In this case, only the route to 192.0.2.0/27 (option A) matches the packet’s destination IP address, so the router will forward the packet according to that route’s instructions.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: R1’s G0/1 interface has the IP address 192.168.209.101/30. Which of the follow- ing commands would activate OSPF on the interface? (select all that network apply)
questions.explanation: network 192.168.209.100 0.0.0.3 area 0 B: network 192.168.209.101 0.0.0.0 area 0 C: network 192.168.0.0 0.0.255.255 area 0 The OSPF command is quite flexible; as long as the appropriate bits match network between the interface’s IP address and the address in the command (those network indicated by a “0” bit in the wildcard mask), OSPF will be activated on the interface. In this case, all three statements match the appropriate bits in the interfac
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: What command should you configure on a router to ensure that all GigabitEther- net interfaces have an OSPF cost of 10?
questions.explanation: auto-cost reference-bandwidth 10000 OSPF’s automatic cost calculation uses the formula “reference bandwidth/interface bandwidth”. The command is configured in auto-cost reference-bandwidth Mbps, so to make a 1 Gbps (1000 Mbps) interface have a cost of 10, the reference bandwidth should be 10000 Mbps (10000/1000 = 10).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: What is the effect of the passive-interface command on an OSPF-activated interface?
options: The router will attempt to form OSPF neighbor relationships on the interface,
The router will advertise the interface’s network to other OSPF routers, but
The router will send hello messages out of the interface, but won’t advertise
The router will send hello messages out of the interface, but won’t attempt to
questions.explanation: The router will advertise the interface’s network to other OSPF routers, but won’t send hello messages out of the interface. The command in OSPF stops the router from sending OSPF passive-interface hello packets out of the interface, preventing OSPF neighbor relationships from
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: Which of the following represents the order of priority when a router decides its OSPF RID?
options: 1) Manual configuration, 2) Highest IP address on a loopback interface, 3)
1) Manual configuration, 2) Lowest IP address on a loopback interface, 3)
1) Manual configuration, 2) Highest IP address on a physical interface, 3)
1) Manual configuration, 2) Lowest IP address on a physical interface, 3) Low-
questions.explanation: Metric When a router learns multiple routes to the same destination from the same routing protocol, the route with the lowest metric is selected for the routing table.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: R1 has multiple OSPF neighbor relationships. R1’s OSPF RID is 192.168.1.1, which was inherited from a physical interface’s IP address. You then config- ure the IP address 10.0.0.2 on a loopback interface and configure router-id in router config mode for the OSPF process. What is R1’s RID after you 1.1.1.1 issue the command? router-id 1.1.1.1
questions.explanation: AD When routes to the same destination are learned via different routing protocols, the administrative distance (AD) is used to determine the preferred route. The route with the lowest AD is selected. Each routing protocol calculates route metric values differ- ently, so they can’t be directly compared.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: Which of the following commands activates OSPF on an interface without using the command? network
questions.explanation: EIGRP = 90 C: OSPF = 110 Here are some default AD values in Cisco IOS: connected = 0, static = 1, External BGP (EBGP) = 20, EIGRP = 90, OSPF = 110, IS-IS = 115, RIP = 120, Internal BGP (IBGP) = 200, unusable = 255.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch17: Dynamic Routing

questions.question: Four routers connected to a segment with the OSPF broadcast network type have the default OSPF priority. R4 is the DR and R3 is the BDR. You configure ip on R2’s interface connected to the segment and reset OSPF ospf priority 100 on R4 with . Which of the following statements is true clear ip ospf process after you reset R4’s OSPF process?
options: R2 is the DR and R3 is the BDR.
R3 is the DR and R2 is the BDR.
R2 is the DR and R4 is the BDR.
R3 is the DR and R4 is the BDR
questions.explanation: R3 is the DR and R2 is the BDR. When the OSPF process of R4 (the DR) is reset, R3 (the BDR) immediately takes over its role as the DR, even though R2’s interface now has the highest priority (100, whereas others have the default of 1). Then, an election is held to determine the new BDR; R2 wins this due to its higher priority and becomes the BDR.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which of the following commands can be used to prevent routers from electing a DR/BDR?
questions.explanation: Router(config-if)# ip ospf network point-to-point Whereas OSPF neighbors using the broadcast network type hold DR/BDR elections, those using the point-to-point network type do not. The default network type of Eth- ernet interfaces is broadcast, but you can use to ip ospf network point-to-point configure the point-to-point network type.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which of the following are requirements for two routers to become OSPF neigh- bors? (select two)
options: Hello and dead timers must match.
questions.explanation: RIDs must be unique. C: Hello and dead timers must match. For two routers to become OSPF neighbors, their RIDs must be unique, and their hello and dead timers must match. The other two options are incorrect because the area numbers must match (not be unique) and the RIDs must be unique (not match).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which of the following represents the order of priority when routers elect a DR/ BDR?
questions.explanation: 1) Highest interface priority, 2) Highest RID The router with the highest interface priority is elected as the DR of an OSPF broad- cast network. If there is a tie, the router with the highest RID becomes the DR. Then, an election using identical rules is held to determine the BDR. Chapter 19: First hop redundancy protocols
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the virtual IP and virtual MAC used by HSRP are true?
options: The active and standby routers share a VIP, but each uses a different virtual
The active and standby routers each use a different VIP, but share a virtual
The active and standby routers share a VIP and virtual MAC address.
The active and standby router each use different VIPs and virtual MAC
questions.explanation: The active and standby routers share a VIP and virtual MAC address. The Hot Standby Router Protocol (HSRP) active and standby routers share a virtual IP address (VIP) and virtual MAC address. This allows for a seamless failover from the active router to the standby router; end hosts don’t have to update their default gate- way IP address or ARP table when a failover occurs.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: During HSRP failover, what is the purpose of the new active router sending GARP messages?
options: To make end hosts update their ARP table.
To make end hosts update their default gateway IP address.
To make switches update their MAC address table.
To inform the former active router of the failover.
questions.explanation: To make switches update their MAC address table. As the previous explanation mentioned, end hosts don’t have to update their default gateway IP address or ARP table when a failover from active to standby occurs. Instead, the gratuitous ARP (GARP) messages sent by the new active router serve to make switches update their MAC address tables. This allows them to correctly forward frames to the new active router.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which virtual MAC address is used by VRRP group 12?
questions.explanation: 0000.5e00.010c The Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) virtual MAC uses the format 0000.5e00.01XX, where XX is the VRRP group number. However, because MAC addresses are written in hexadecimal, the virtual MAC format for VRRP group 12 is 0000.5e00.010c (0d12 = 0xc), not 0000.5e00.0112.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: R1 is a master router, actively forwarding packets between the LAN and external networks. R2 is a backup router, waiting to take over in case R1 fails. Which FHRP are R1 and R2 using?
questions.explanation: VRRP In VRRP, the terms “master” and “backup” are used. HSRP uses the terms “active” and “standby”, and GLBP uses “Active Virtual Gateway (AVG)” and “Active Virtual For- warder (AVF)”.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which FHRP facilitates load balancing of traffic within a single subnet?
questions.explanation: 192.168.1.1 Once a router has determined its RID and established an OSPF neighbor relationship with another router, the only way to change the RID is to manually configure it with the rid command and then reset the OSPF process with router-id clear ip ospf . However, the scenario does not indicate that you reset the OSPF process, so process R1’s RID remains 192.168.1.1.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: R1 and R2 use VRRP to provide a redundant default gateway for end hosts. R1 was the master router, but a hardware failure on R1 caused R2 to take over the master role. Given the default settings, what happens when R1 recovers from its hardware failure?
options: R1 becomes the master router again.
questions.explanation: R1(config-if)# ip ospf 1 area 0 You can use the process-id area-id in interface config mode to ip ospf area activate OSPF on the interface. This is generally preferred over the command network due to its simplicity.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which command configures the HSRP VIP?
questions.explanation: R3 is the DR and R2 is the BDR. When the OSPF process of R4 (the DR) is reset, R3 (the BDR) immediately takes over its role as the DR, even though R2’s interface now has the highest priority (100, whereas others have the default of 1). Then, an election is held to determine the new BDR; R2 wins this due to its higher priority and becomes the BDR.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: What is the correct order of priority when electing the HSRP active router?
options: 1) Lowest interface priority, 2) Lowest interface IP address
1) Lowest interface IP address, 2) Lowest interface priority
questions.explanation: Router(config-if)# ip ospf network point-to-point Whereas OSPF neighbors using the broadcast network type hold DR/BDR elections, those using the point-to-point network type do not. The default network type of Eth- ernet interfaces is broadcast, but you can use to ip ospf network point-to-point configure the point-to-point network type.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol1 — Ch18: OSPF

questions.question: Which of the following NAT types provide one-to-one translations only?
questions.explanation: Buffer traffic that exceeds the configured rate limit Traffic shaping buffers excess traffic to conform to a configured rate limit, smoothing out the rate at which packets are sent.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which Cisco NAT term describes the IP address of a host in the internal network from the perspective of hosts in the external network?
questions.explanation: Tail drop Tail drop, which occurs when packets are dropped from the end of a full queue, can lead to TCP global synchronization, where multiple TCP flows reduce their window sizes simultaneously, leading to network underutilization. This can lead to a global TCP window size increase, leading to congestion and tail drop again— a vicious cycle. Random Early Detection (RED) and Weighted RED (WRED) are techniques to avoid tail drop, not causes of it.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: In a typical inside source NAT scenario, which of the following addresses is usually a private address?
questions.explanation: Marking Marking modifies fields in Layer-2 (CoS) and Layer-3 (DSCP) headers, making the classification of messages for QoS much simpler.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: R1 must use NAT to allow hosts in its connected LAN to communicate over the internet. Which of the following commands should be used to enable this? (select two)
options: ip nat inside on the LAN-facing interface
ip nat outside on the internet-facing interface
ip nat internal on the LAN-facing interface
ip nat external on the internet-facing interface
questions.explanation: Shaping Traffic shaping is often used to control traffic rates to comply with ISP-imposed rate limits, buffering and delaying excess traffic. The ISP, on the other hand, often uses policing to strictly enforce the configured rate limit.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which of the following NAT types provides many-to-one translations?
questions.explanation: Classification Classification identifies the traffic type of a packet based on defined criteria, such as the marking in the DSCP field of the IP header.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: In a typical inside source NAT scenario, which of the following addresses should be identical in the output of show ip nat translations ? (select two)
questions.explanation: AF43 AF43 (Assured Forwarding class 4, drop precedence 3) indicates high priority but also a high likelihood of being dropped in times of congestion. AF defines four priority classes (1–4) and three drop precedence values (1–3).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which of the following commands creates a pool for dynamic PAT?
questions.explanation: Policing Policing drops or re-marks traffic that exceeds a configured rate limit, enforcing a strict bandwidth limit without buffering.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: ACL 1 only permits packets sourced from the 10.0.0.0/24 subnet. After issuing the ip nat inside source list 1 gigabitethernet0/0 command on R1, what action will it take on packets denied by ACL 1?
options: It will forward them without translation.
It will buffer them until they can be translated.
It will translate their source IP to GigabitEthernet0/0’s IP.
questions.explanation: It will forward them without translation. In the context of NAT, an ACL is used to determine which packets are eligible for NAT; it isn’t used to decide which packets will be forwarded or discarded. Packets denied by the ACL are not translated, but will be forwarded as normal according to the router’s routing table. However, this will likely result in those untranslated packets being discarded by the ISP’s router due to their private addresses.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which of the following commands configures dynamic PAT?
options: ip nat inside source list 1 pool POOL1 overload
ip nat inside source list 1 pool POOL1
ip nat inside source list 1 pat POOL1
ip nat inside source list 1 203.0.113.1 overload
questions.explanation: ip nat inside source list 1 pool POOL1 overload This command configures dynamic PAT using the specified access list and pool, with the overload keyword enabling multiple inside local addresses to share one inside global address.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: When PAT is configured, what happens when two pre-translation messages use the same source port number?
options: The second packet is buffered until the port number becomes available.
The second packet’s port number is translated to a new number.
The second packet is forwarded without translation.
questions.explanation: The second packet’s port number is translated to a new number. In PAT, if two packets have the same source port, NAT will assign a unique port number for the second packet. The inside global IP address of each packet will be identical after translation, so a unique source port number is needed for identification. Chapter 10: Quality of service
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: What does traffic shaping do?
options: Buffer traffic that exceeds the configured rate limit
Drop traffic that exceeds the configured rate limit
Decide the order in which queued packets are transmitted
Categorize packets according to their markings
questions.explanation: Buffer traffic that exceeds the configured rate limit Traffic shaping buffers excess traffic to conform to a configured rate limit, smoothing out the rate at which packets are sent.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which of the following often leads to TCP global synchronization?
questions.explanation: Tail drop Tail drop, which occurs when packets are dropped from the end of a full queue, can lead to TCP global synchronization, where multiple TCP flows reduce their window sizes simultaneously, leading to network underutilization. This can lead to a global TCP window size increase, leading to congestion and tail drop again— a vicious cycle. Random Early Detection (RED) and Weighted RED (WRED) are techniques to avoid tail drop, not causes of it.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which QoS feature modifies specific fields in the Layer-2 and Layer-3 headers to facilitate the categorization of messages?
questions.explanation: Marking Marking modifies fields in Layer-2 (CoS) and Layer-3 (DSCP) headers, making the classification of messages for QoS much simpler.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which feature would an ISP’s customer likely implement to avoid exceeding the ISP’s rate limit?
questions.explanation: Shaping Traffic shaping is often used to control traffic rates to comply with ISP-imposed rate limits, buffering and delaying excess traffic. The ISP, on the other hand, often uses policing to strictly enforce the configured rate limit.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which QoS feature involves identifying the traffic type of a particular packet?
questions.explanation: Classification Classification identifies the traffic type of a packet based on defined criteria, such as the marking in the DSCP field of the IP header.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which of the following markings indicates a high priority and a high drop precedence?
questions.explanation: AF43 AF43 (Assured Forwarding class 4, drop precedence 3) indicates high priority but also a high likelihood of being dropped in times of congestion. AF defines four priority classes (1–4) and three drop precedence values (1–3).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which QoS feature drops or re-marks traffic that exceeds the configured rate limit?
questions.explanation: Policing Policing drops or re-marks traffic that exceeds a configured rate limit, enforcing a strict bandwidth limit without buffering.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which scheduling method combines a priority queue with a weighted round-robin scheduler?
questions.explanation: It will forward them without translation. In the context of NAT, an ACL is used to determine which packets are eligible for NAT; it isn’t used to decide which packets will be forwarded or discarded. Packets denied by the ACL are not translated, but will be forwarded as normal according to the router’s routing table. However, this will likely result in those untranslated packets being discarded by the ISP’s router due to their private addresses.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which marking is typically used for voice call traffic?
questions.explanation: ip nat inside source list 1 pool POOL1 overload This command configures dynamic PAT using the specified access list and pool, with the overload keyword enabling multiple inside local addresses to share one inside global address.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: Which marking is typically used for best effort traffic?
questions.explanation: The second packet’s port number is translated to a new number. In PAT, if two packets have the same source port, NAT will assign a unique port number for the second packet. The inside global IP address of each packet will be identical after translation, so a unique source port number is needed for identification. Chapter 10: Quality of service
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch9: NAT

questions.question: What attack method locks out a system or even encrypts it and then offers to unlock it in exchange for money?
questions.explanation: Ransomware is an attack that locks a system or encrypts it. The attacker then offers to accept payment to unlock the affected system.

questions.question: What is a bug in a system that might be a target of an exploit?
questions.explanation: A vulnerability is a weakness or a break in a system that can be exploited. Additional Resources Additional Resources What Is a DDoS Attack? https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/security/what-is-a-ddos-attack.html Exploit Kits https://www.cyber.nj.gov/threat-profiles/exploit-kits User Security Configuration Guide https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/ios-xml/ios/sec_usr_cfg/configuration/15-mt/ sec-usr-cfg-15-mt-book/sec-autosecure.html

questions.question: What is an alternative to using local authentication on a Cisco router?
questions.explanation: The most common and most powerful configuration for authentication is to centralize this function with AAA.
questions.source_ref: Exam Cram Ch23: Device Access Control

questions.question: What happens if you issue the command no service password-encryption?
options: The device no longer encrypts future passwords.
The device removes all encrypted passwords.
questions.explanation: This command has no effect on passwords that have already been encrypted on the device. No passwords created in the future will be encrypted. All hashed passwords (MD5 hashed secrets) will remain hashed regardless of the service password-encryption command.
questions.source_ref: Exam Cram Ch23: Device Access Control

questions.question: Why might you set the source interface in traffic?
options: In order to enhance processing speed
In order to eliminate the use of send buffers
questions.explanation: Source address control is often used for enhanced reliability and security.
questions.source_ref: Exam Cram Ch23: Device Access Control

questions.question: What command do you use to create the public/private key pair for SSH?
questions.explanation: This command creates the keying material needed by SSH.
questions.source_ref: Exam Cram Ch23: Device Access Control

questions.question: What AAA security protocol communicates from network devices to a central security server using UDP?
questions.explanation: RADIUS provides low overhead. One of the ways it does this it by using UDP in its operations.
questions.source_ref: Exam Cram Ch23: Device Access Control

questions.question: What type of key technology is used with public key cryptography?
questions.explanation: Public key cryptography uses an asymmetric key approach.

questions.question: What types of keys are used in public key cryptography?
questions.explanation: Private and public keys are the primary mechanisms through which asymmetric (public key) cryptography functions. Additional Resources Additional Resources What Is a VPN? https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/security/ vpn-endpoint-security-clients/what-is-vpn.html How Virtual Private Networks Work https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/security-vpn/ ipsec-negotiation-ike-protocols/14106-how-vpn-works.html

questions.question: What field can a standard ACL filter on?
questions.explanation: A standard ACL can filter on source IP address only.

questions.question: Why might deny any log appear at the end of an ACL?
options: It is used to track traffic that matches no previous ACL entries.
It is required as the final ACE.
It is used to send emails to security team members.
questions.explanation: This is matched before the implicit deny all. An explicit deny all is used for tracking entries that match the end of the list.

questions.question: What command assigns a standard ACL for filtering on an interface?
questions.explanation: You use the ip access-group command in this case.

questions.question: What command permits you to verify that an access list is assigned to an interface in a specific direction?
questions.explanation: You use show ip interface to verify assignment of ACLs to interfaces. Hands-On Lab Practice Assignment Configuring ACLs To complete this Hands-On Lab Practice Assignment, download the assigned Packet Tracer file from the book’s companion website and perform the lab on your locally installed version of Packet Tracer. For instructions on how to download and use the Packet Tracer files, see “Packet Tracer Hands-On Lab Practice Assignments” in the Introduction of this book.

questions.question: What is the port status when there is no issue with the port but port-security is enabled on it?
questions.explanation: The state is Secure-up.

questions.question: What command sets the maximum number of MAC addresses permitted to 4?
questions.explanation: The command is switchport port-security maximum 4.

questions.question: What interface command creates a static entry to allow frames sources from MAC address aaaa.bbbb.cccc on an interface?
questions.explanation: The command is switchport port-security mac-address aaaa. bbbb.cccc.

questions.question: Under which configuration mode is the command ip dhcp snooping used to enable DHCP snooping?
questions.explanation: As a first step with DHCP snooping, you must enable the feature globally in global configuration mode with ip dhcp snooping. This command is often the last command you enter after you have configured the VLANs for DHCP snooping and set your trusted interfaces. That way, when you actually turn on the feature globally, it is configured (and tuned) as you need it.

questions.question: What is the result of trusting an interface with the DAI feature?
options: An automatic rate limit feature engages with the standard ARP inspection.
The DAI functionality proceeds, and the DHCP snooping state changes to trusted.
Trusted interfaces have no meaning for DAI; they have meaning only for DHCP snooping.
Inspection of ARP packets does not take place on the interface.
questions.explanation: Setting an interface as trusted for DAI disables the checking of ARP packets on that interface.
