For instructions on how to download and use the Packet Tracer files, see “Packet Tracer Hands-On Lab Practice Assignments” in the Introduc

questions.question: What is the name of the “database” used with SNMP?
questions.explanation: SNMP relies on a Management Information Base (MIB) for storing variables and their values.

questions.question: How do you configure a Cisco router to use its internal clock as the reference time source and then become an NTP server?
options: Use the ntp clock command. Practice Exam 2
questions.explanation: The ntp master command sets the local device’s internal clock to act as a time source for the network. Any Cisco router that has a time source to synchronize to (either an internal clock using the ntp master command or an external NTP server configured with the ntp server command) will act as a time source for any other NTP clients that try to get time from the local device.

questions.question: Which of the following applications requires the connection-oriented layer four protocol, TCP?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Which of the following applications requires the connection-oriented layer four protocol, TCP? (Choose all that apply) a) Telnet b) FTP c) TFTP d) SNMP e) none of the above Answer a) Telnet b) FTP TFTP and SNMP use layer 4 UDP.

questions.question: A PC connects to a LAN and uses DHCP to lease an IP address for the first time. Of the usual four DHCP messages that flow between the PC and the DHCP server, which ones do the client send? (Choose two answers.)
questions.explanation: The client sends a Discover message, with the server returning an Offer mes- sage. The client then sends a Request, with the server sending back the IP address in the Acknowledgment message.

questions.question: Which of the following kinds of information are part of a DHCP server configura- tion? (Choose two answers.)
options: Ranges of IP addresses in subnets that the server should lease
Ranges of IP addresses to not lease per subnet
The default router IP and MAC address in each subnet
questions.explanation: The two correct answers list the two primary facts that impact which IP addresses the server will lease to clients. For the incorrect answer about DNS servers, the DHCP server does supply the IP address of the DNS servers, but not the host- names of the DNS servers. Also, the DHCP server supplies the IP address (but not the MAC address) of the default gateway in each subnet.

questions.question: Which answers list a criterion for choosing which router interfaces need to be config- ured as a DHCP relay agent? (Choose two answers.)
options: If the subnet off the interface does not include a DHCP server
If the subnet off the interface does include a DHCP server
If the subnet off the interface contains DHCP clients
If the router interface already has an ip address dhcp command
questions.explanation: A router needs to act as a DHCP relay agent if DHCP clients exist on the connected subnet and there is no DHCP server in that subnet. If a DHCP exists in the subnet, the router does not need to forward DHCP messages to a remote DHCP server (which is the function of a DHCP relay agent). The answer that mentions the ip address dhcp command makes the router interface act as a DHCP client and has noth- ing to do with DHCP relay agent.

questions.question: A router connects to an Internet Service Provider (ISP) using its G0/0/0 interface, with the ip address dhcp command configured. What does the router do with the DHCP- learned default gateway information?
options: The router ignores the default gateway value learned from the DHCP server.
The router uses the default gateway just like a host, ignoring its routing table.
The router forwards received packets based on its routing table but uses its
The router adds a default route based on the default gateway to its IP routing
questions.explanation: The ip address dhcp command tells the router to obtain its address using DHCP. The router learns all the same information that a normal DHCP client would learn. The router uses the address listed as the default gateway to build a default route, using the

questions.question: R1 and R2 attach to the same Ethernet VLAN, with subnet 10.1.19.0/25, with addresses 10.1.19.1 and 10.1.19.2, respectively, configured with the ip address inter- face subcommand. Host A refers to 10.1.19.1 as its default router, and host B refers to 10.1.19.2 as its default router. The routers do not use an FHRP. Which of the fol- lowing is a problem for this LAN?
options: The design breaks IPv4 addressing rules because two routers cannot connect to
If one router fails, neither host can send packets off-subnet.
If one router fails, both hosts will use the one remaining router as a default
If one router fails, the host that uses that router as a default router cannot send
questions.explanation: QoS tools manage bandwidth, delay, jitter, and loss. C

questions.question: Given the diagram below, complete the missing configuration command in order to make the static NAT func- tional. Configuration snippet R1# show running-config 297 ! Lines omitted for brevity ! interface FastEthernet0/0 ip address 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.0 ip nat inside interface GigabitEthernet0/0 ip address 190.2.2.21 255.255.255.0 ip ____________________ (missing command) ip nat inside source static 192.168.1.2 200.1.1.2 ip nat inside source static 192.168.1.1 200.1.1.1
questions.explanation: nat outside is the correct answer. The final configuration command should be like this: interface FastEthernet0/0 ip address 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.0 ip nat inside interface GigabitEthernet0/0 ip address 190.2.2.21 255.255.255.0 ip nat outside ip nat inside source static 192.168.1.2 200.1.1.2 298 Static NAT configuration requires only a few configuration steps. Each static mapping between a local (private) address and a global (public) address m

questions.question: A Cisco switch and a Juniper switch use a Layer-2 discovery protocol to share information about each other. Which protocol are they likely using?
questions.explanation: LLDP Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) is a vendor-neutral Layer-2 discovery protocol used by various networking devices, including those from Cisco, Juniper, and most other vendors. Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP), on the other hand, is Cisco proprietary (although it has been implemented by a few other vendors).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: Which of the following commands doubles the frequency at which a device sends CDP messages?
questions.explanation: cdp timer 30 The cdp timer command sets the frequency at which CDP messages are sent. The default timer is 60 seconds, so setting it to 30 seconds would double the frequency.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: Which command allows the device to control how long its LLDP neighbors wait before removing its entry from their neighbor tables?
questions.explanation: lldp holdtime The lldp holdtime command sets the amount of time a device’s neighbors should hold information before discarding it. This time is communicated in LLDP messages sent by the device.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: Which command globally enables CDP on a device?
questions.explanation: cdp run The cdp run command—issued in global config mode—globally enables CDP on a Cisco device. This command is required to activate CDP if it has been previously disabled (although CDP is globally enabled by default).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: You issue show cdp neighbors on R1. SW1’s entry shows Local Intrfce: Gig 0/0 and Port ID: 0/1 . Which of the following statements is true?
options: R1 G0/0 is connected to SW1 G0/1.
R1 G0/1 is connected to SW1 G0/0.
questions.explanation: R1 G0/0 is connected to SW1 G0/1. In the output of show cdp neighbors , the Local Intrfce column indicates the interface of this device (R1), and the Port ID column indicates the interface of the neighboring device (SW1).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: Which of the following statements about LLDP is false?
options: An interface must have an IP address to send LLDP messages.
LLDP Tx and Rx can be controlled separately.
LLDP is disabled on Cisco devices by default.
Any vendor can implement LLDP on its devices.
questions.explanation: An interface must have an IP address to send LLDP messages. LLDP doesn’t require an IP address on an interface to send (or receive) messages—the same applies to CDP. CDP and LLDP are Layer-2 discovery protocols; they function even on interfaces that don’t have Layer-3 IP addresses.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: Which command(s) can be used to disable LLDP on an interface?
options: no lldp transmit and no lldp receive
questions.explanation: no lldp transmit and no lldp receive To completely disable LLDP on an interface, both transmission and reception of LLDP messages must be turned off. You can do this with the no lldp transmit and no lldp receive commands in interface config mode.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch1: CDP/LLDP

questions.question: The output of show clock shows an asterisk ( * ) before the time. What is the significance of this asterisk?
options: The device’s clock was manually configured.
The time was learned via NTP.
questions.explanation: The time is not authoritative. An asterisk before the time, such as *08:56:18.831 UTC Mon Aug 28 2023 , means that the clock’s time is not authoritative—not considered to be accurate. This disappears after manually setting the time or syncing to an NTP server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: Which command manually configures a Cisco device’s clock?
questions.explanation: R1# clock set 12:45:00 January 19 2024 The command to set the software clock is clock set hh:mm:ss month day year . Note that the month and day arguments can be reversed: January 19 and 19 January are both valid.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: Which kind of NTP server operates as both an NTP client and server?
questions.explanation: Secondary server A secondary NTP server operates both as an NTP client (synchronizing its time with a more authoritative server) and as a server (providing the time to clients of its own).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: Which of the following commands configures a Cisco device in NTP client/server mode?
questions.explanation: ntp server The ntp server ip-address command configures a Cisco device in NTP client/server mode. The device will become an NTP client of the server specified in the command. It will also function as an NTP server, capable of providing the time to clients of its own. This makes the device a secondary NTP server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: R1 is an NTP client. The output of show ntp associations on R1 shows st: 3 . What does this output mean?
options: R1 has a total of three NTP associations.
R1 learns the time from a server whose NTP stratum is 3.
questions.explanation: R1 learns the time from a server whose NTP stratum is 3. The show ntp associations command lists the device’s NTP associations—NTP servers that it learns the time from. The st column lists the association’s NTP stratum. In this case, the value of 3 means that R1 learns the time from a server whose NTP stratum is 3.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: Which command allows a Cisco device to function as an NTP server without being an NTP client?
questions.explanation: ntp master The ntp master command can be used to make the device function as an NTP server even if it isn’t an NTP client of another server. This command can be useful in isolated environments where an external NTP server isn’t available. It can also function as a backup; if the device loses connectivity to its NTP servers, it can still function as an NTP server and provide the time to its own clients.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: Which commands are necessary to configure a Cisco IOS device as an NTP client, authenticating the server it learns the time from? (select three)
questions.explanation: ntp authentication-key 1 md5 P3sSw!rd D: ntp trusted-key 1 E: ntp server 10.0.0.1 key 1 The ntp authentication-key key-number md5 key command is necessary to create an authentication key. Then, ntp trusted-key key-number is necessary to identify the key as “trusted”. Finally, ntp server ip-address key key-number is necessary to specify that the key should be used to authenticate the specified server. The ntp authentication command is not necessar
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch2: NTP

questions.question: Which part of the URL “https://www.jeremysitlab.com/ccna-resources” is the authority?
questions.explanation: www.jeremysitlab.com In a URL, the authority specifies the domain name or IP address of the server where the resource is hosted. In this case, “www.jeremysitlab.com” is the authority.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: What is the TLD of “mail.google.com.”?
questions.explanation: com Domains that are immediately under the root (which is represented by “.”) are called top-level domains (TLDs). In this URL, the TLD is “com”. Other common TLDs include “net”, “org”, “edu”, etc.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: Which kind of DNS server holds the definitive set of records for a specific domain?
questions.explanation: Authoritative DNS server The DNS server that holds the definitive set of records for a specific domain is called an “authoritative DNS server”.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: Which kind of DNS query asks for a definite answer (the IP address or an error message)?
questions.explanation: Recursive DNS A recursive DNS query expects a definite answer from the DNS server. If the DNS server does not have the answer, it will query other DNS servers until it finds the answer, or reply with an error message if it can’t.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: A recursive resolver receives a query for a domain name that does not exist in its cache. What kind of DNS query does the recursive resolver send to the root server to resolve the domain name?
questions.explanation: Iterative DNS When a recursive resolver sends a query to a root server, it typically performs an iterative query. The root server responds with a referral to a TLD server, and the recursive resolver continues querying down the DNS hierarchy to find the answer.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: Which of the following DNS record type descriptions are correct? (select three)
options: A: Points to an IPv6 address
CNAME: Points to another domain name
PTR: Used for reverse DNS lookups
NS: Specifies the domain’s mail server(s)
questions.explanation: CNAME: Points to another domain name C: PTR: Used for reverse DNS lookups F: SOA: Provides administrative information such as admin contact A CNAME record maps a domain name to another domain name. PTR records are used for reverse DNS lookups and map an IP address to a domain name. SOA records provide administrative information such as admin contact details, a serial number, etc. The others are incorrect: An A record points to an IPv4 address, an
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: Which command allows a Cisco IOS device to respond to DNS queries?
questions.explanation: ip dns server The ip dns server command enables a Cisco router to act as a DNS server, allowing it to respond to DNS queries.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: Which configuration is needed on a Cisco router for it to forward DNS request packets between a PC and a DNS server?
questions.explanation: No DNS configuration is needed. For a Cisco router to forward DNS requests, no specific DNS configuration is needed. The router will simply forward these packets based on its routing table. The ip dns server , ip domain-lookup , and ip name- server commands are used for different DNS functionalities on the router itself, not for forwarding DNS requests.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch3: DNS

questions.question: Which of the following DHCP messages are sent by a DHCP server? (select two)
questions.explanation: ACK D: OFFER In the standard Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) DISCOVER, OFFER, REQUEST, ACK (DORA) exchange, the DISCOVER and REQUEST messages are sent by the client, and the OFFER and ACK messages are sent by the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: When configuring a DHCP pool, which of the following DHCP settings is configured in global configuration mode?
options: The range of addresses to assign to clients
questions.explanation: The excluded address range(s) When configuring a DHCP pool on a Cisco router, most settings are configured in DHCP configuration mode (accessed with ip dhcp pool name ). However, any ranges of excluded addresses—addresses that should not be leased to clients—must be configured in global configuration mode with ip dhcp excluded-address low-ip high-ip .
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: Which of the following commands configures a Cisco router as a DHCP client?
questions.explanation: R1(config-if)# ip address dhcp To configure a Cisco router interface to obtain its IP address via DHCP, use the ip address dhcp command in interface configuration mode. The router will send DHCP DISCOVER messages out of the interface to attempt to lease an IP address.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: Which command displays the list of clients that a Cisco router, functioning as a DHCP server, has leased addresses to using DHCP?
questions.explanation: show ip dhcp binding The show ip dhcp binding command displays the current address bindings and lease times for a Cisco router operating as a DHCP server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: R1’s G0/1 interface is connected to the WAN and G0/2 is connected to a LAN with DHCP clients. Where should the ip helper-address command be configured to make R1 function as a DHCP relay agent?
questions.explanation: Interface configuration mode for G0/2 To configure R1 as a DHCP relay agent, the ip helper-address ip-address command should be applied to the LAN-facing interface (G0/2). This command forwards clients’ broadcast DHCP messages received on the interface to the DHCP server specified in the command.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: Which command shows detailed information about a Windows host’s network configuration?
questions.explanation: ipconfig /all The ipconfig /all command in Windows displays detailed information about the host’s network configuration, including IP address, subnet mask, default gateway, DNS servers, and DHCP status.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: Which command can be used to clear all of a router’s DHCP bindings?
questions.explanation: clear ip dhcp binding * The clear ip dhcp binding * command is used to clear all dynamic DHCP bindings from a Cisco router’s DHCP server database.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: Which of the following DHCP messages are always broadcast? (select two)
questions.explanation: REQUEST C: DISCOVER In the DHCP DORA exchange, messages sent by clients (DISCOVER and REQUEST) are always broadcast. Messages sent by the server can be either unicast or broadcast, depending on what the client indicates in its messages.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch4: DHCP

questions.question: Which SNMP message type is an unacknowledged notification from a managed device to the NMS?
questions.explanation: Trap A Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) trap is an unacknowledged message sent from a managed device to a Network Management Station (NMS). Traps are used to notify the NMS of significant events or conditions on the managed device, but the NMS doesn’t acknowledge them with Response messages.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch6: SNMP

questions.question: Which SNMP message type allows the NMS to retrieve a large amount of information from a managed device without specifying each individual OID?
questions.explanation: GetBulk The GetBulk message type is used to retrieve large amounts of data from a managed device. It allows the NMS to request a range of OIDs in a single message, making it more efficient than specifying each OID individually.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch6: SNMP

questions.question: Which message type triggers a response from the NMS?
questions.explanation: Inform An Inform message is similar to a Trap, but it requires an acknowledgment from the NMS. After receiving an Inform message, the NMS sends a Response message back to the managed device.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch6: SNMP

questions.question: Which SNMP version(s) use community-string authentication? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: SNMPv1 C: SNMPv2c SNMP versions 1 and 2c use community-string authentication; a community string is basically a password. SNMPv2 (no “c”) is not widely implemented; it doesn’t use community strings. SNMPv3 uses a more advanced user-based security model.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch6: SNMP

questions.question: Where does an SNMP managed device store OIDs?
questions.explanation: The MIB Object Identifiers (OIDs) in SNMP are stored in a database called the Management Information Base (MIB) of a managed device. Each OID is a variable containing a value, and SNMP works by interacting with these variables and their values.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch6: SNMP

questions.question: Which SNMP versions support a user-based security model? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: SNMPv3 SNMPv3 is the only version of SNMP that supports a user-based security model, which provides a more secure framework than the simple community-string authentication used in SNMPv1 and SNMPv2c.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch6: SNMP

questions.question: Which command enables logging to an external Syslog server with the informational severity level and higher?
questions.explanation: logging trap 6 The logging trap [ severity ] command can be used to control the severity level of messages logged to a Syslog server. “Informational” is severity level 6, so the logging trap 6 command enables logging to an external Syslog server with the informational severity level and higher.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: Which of the following Syslog severity level and IOS keyword pairs are correct? (select three)
questions.explanation: informational = 6 F: emergencies = 0 G: notifications = 5 Here are the Syslog severity levels in order: • emergencies = 0 • alerts = 1 • critical = 2 • errors = 3 • warnings = 4 • notifications = 5 • informational = 6 • debugging = 7
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: Which command is necessary to enable real-time logging to the VTY lines for the current session?
questions.explanation: R1# terminal monitor The command terminal monitor is used in the command-line interface to enable real-time logging to the VTY lines for the current session. Although the logging monitor command enables/disables logging to the VTY lines, terminal monitor must be used to enable it for the current session.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: Which of the following statements accurately describes the effect of the logging buffered notifications command?
options: Logs of severity level 5 and numerically higher will be logged.
Logs of severity level 5 and numerically lower will be logged.
Logs of severity level 5 will be logged.
questions.explanation: Logs of severity level 5 and numerically lower will be logged. logging buffered notifications enables logging at severity level 5. However, it doesn’t only enable logging of messages of severity level 5; it enables logging of all messages with a severity level of 5 and numerically lower (more severe). So, the command enables logging of messages of severity levels 0–5.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: What is the facility of the Syslog message Jan 20 08:05:52: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/2, changed state to up ?
options: Interface GigabitEthernet0/2, changed state to up
questions.explanation: LINK The format of a Syslog message is seq:timestamp: %facility-severity-mnemonic: description . In this case, LINK in the facility, indicating the service, application, or other component of the device that generated the message.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: What is the mnemonic of the Syslog message Jan 20 08:36:29: %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console by jeremy on vty3 (192.168.1.224) ?
options: Configured from console by jeremy on vty3 (192.168.1.224)
questions.explanation: CONFIG_I CONFIG_I is the mnemonic of this Syslog message, a short text string providing a brief description of the event that triggered the Syslog message. In this case, we know it is something related to the configuration of the device.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: Which command specifies an external Syslog server to send log messages to?
questions.explanation: logging host 10.0.0.10 Although the logging trap command is used to enable/disable logging to external Syslog servers, the logging host ip-address command is used to specify a Syslog server to send log messages to.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: Which severity levels will be logged to the console line as a result of the logging console 2 command? (select three)
questions.explanation: Alerts D: Emergencies F: Critical The logging console 2 command configures the console to log messages with a severity of critical (level 2) and numerically lower, which includes alerts (level 1) and emergencies (level 0).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch7: Syslog

questions.question: Why does TFTP use UDP, not TCP?
options: TFTP is a real-time application that is sensitive to delay.
TFTP is a simple query/response protocol that doesn’t need the overhead of setting up a connection.
TFTP provides its own reliability and doesn’t need TCP’s overhead.
TFTP only sends trivial files that don’t need reliable data delivery.
questions.explanation: TFTP provides its own reliability and doesn’t need TCP’s overhead. Although file transfer protocols typically use TCP to provide reliable delivery of messages, the TFTP protocol itself uses lock-step communication to provide its own reliability. For that reason, it can use the lightweight UDP as its Layer-4 protocol instead of TCP.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which type of IOS file system is used to represent external FTP/TFTP servers?
questions.explanation: Network Cisco IOS uses the ftp: and tftp: file systems to represent external FTP/TFTP servers. Both file systems use the “network” file system type.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of TFTP?
options: Clients can only issue read or write requests
Establishes two connections between the client and server
Clients can navigate through a server’s file directories
questions.explanation: Clients can only issue read or write requests Unlike FTP, which provides more robust capabilities, TFTP is a simple protocol that only allows clients to send read requests (to download a file) and write requests (to upload a file) to the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which command can be used to transfer a file from the local device to an external FTP server?
questions.explanation: copy flash: ftp: The IOS command to transfer files is copy source destination . To transfer a file from the local device to an external FTP server, the source should be a file or directory on the local system (i.e. flash: ) and the destination should be ftp: .
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which of the following are characteristics of FTP? (select two)
options: Provides reliable delivery at the Application Layer
Establishes two connections between the client and server
questions.explanation: Establishes two connections between the client and server C: Uses TCP ports 20 and 21 FTP establishes two connections between the client and server. The first is the control connection (TCP port 21), through which the server authenticates the client and the client sends commands to the server. The second is the data connection (TCP port 20), through which file transfers occur. With a single control connection, the client can establish multiple da
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which command can be used to specify which IOS file will be loaded upon the next system reboot?
questions.explanation: R1(config)# boot system The boot system file-path command, used in global configuration mode, is used to specify the IOS file that the device should load upon the next reboot.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which commands configure the default FTP user credentials? (select two)
questions.explanation: ip ftp username jeremy C: ip ftp password password123 The commands ip ftp username username and ip ftp password password are used to configure the default username and password for FTP connections initiated from the device.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which connection does an FTP client use to send commands to the server?
questions.explanation: The control connection The client uses the FTP control connection to send commands to the server. One example is RETR (retrieve), which is used to download a copy of a file from the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch8: FTP/TFTP

questions.question: Which of these statements is false regarding the Cisco ACI solution?
options: ACI controls the network and uses state awareness with a controlled device.
ACI offers robust support for a wide range of APIs.
ACI uses a high-speed fabric underlay.
ACI is an example of controller-less SDN.
questions.explanation: Cisco ACI uses a controller-based approach to SDN.

questions.question: VXLAN is an example of what type of SDN technology?
questions.explanation: VXLAN technology permits the virtualization of the underlying physical network. VXLAN is used to build an overlay architecture. Additional Resources Additional Resources SDN Controllers https://www.ajsnetworking.com/sdn/ SDN APIs https://www.ajsnetworking.com/sdn-apis/ 5 SDN Concepts You’ve Gotta Know https://youtu.be/RdCLmakGZL0

questions.question: What type of APIs are often used with networking and cloud technologies?
questions.explanation: REST APIs work very well with HTTP and therefore are often used with networking and cloud technologies.

questions.question: Which is not a term in the developer CRUD actions?
questions.explanation: The R in CRUD stands for Read.

questions.question: What technology is used to permit Ansible to connect to managed nodes for configuration purposes?
questions.explanation: Ansible uses SSH to connect to and configure managed nodes in a secure fashion.

questions.question: What is the name for an ordered list of tasks in Ansible?
questions.explanation: Ansible uses playbooks in order to run many automation tasks together.

questions.question: What technology provides multiple default gateways in a redundant manner and automates load sharing?
questions.explanation: The FHRPs you should know are HSRP, VRRP, and GLBP. GLBP is a Cisco invention that automates load balancing with redundant gateways.

questions.question: What is the term for an API that communicates from a network management station to the SDN controller?
questions.explanation: The northbound APIs are used to communicate between those configuring the network and the SDN controller. The southbound APIs represent the communications from the controller to the network devices.

questions.question: What component does Ansible use to gather plays that perform tasks on a network?
questions.explanation: Ansible uses a component called a playbook to enable automation on a network. This playbook consists of a list of tasks (known in Ansible as plays). Glossary Numerics 2-tier spine-leaf topology A newer network topology design from Cisco Systems that is used in technologies such as Cisco ACI. The two tiers are called spine and leaf. Each leaf device is connected to every spine device in a full mesh. The spine is considered the backbone or the core of the network. The spine is

questions.question: In Cisco Software-Defined Access (SDA), which term refers to the devices and cabling, along with configuration that allows the network device nodes enough IP connectivity to send IP packets to each other?
questions.explanation: The data plane includes all networking device actions related to the receipt, pro- cessing, and forwarding of each message, as in the case described in the question. The term table plane is not used in networking. The management plane and control plane are not concerned with the per-message forwarding actions.

questions.question: In Cisco Software-Defined Access (SDA), which term refers to the functions that deliver endpoint packets across the network using tunnels between the ingress and egress fabric nodes?
questions.explanation: The control plane includes all networking device actions that create the informa- tion used by the data plane when processing messages. The control plane includes functions like IP routing protocols and Spanning Tree Protocol (STP). The term table plane is not used in networking. The management plane and data plane are not concerned with collecting the information that the data plane then uses.

questions.question: In Software-Defined Access (SDA), which of the answers are part of the overlay data plane?
questions.explanation: Although many variations of SDN architectures exist, they typically use a central- ized controller. That controller may centralize some or even all control plane functions in the controller. However, the data plane function of receiving messages, matching them based on header fields, taking actions (like making a forwarding decision), and forwarding the message still happens on the network elements (switches) and not on the controller. For the i

questions.question: Which answers best describe options of how to implement security with scalable groups using DNA Center and SDA? (Choose two answers.)
options: A human user from the DNA Center GUI
A human user using the CLI of an SDA fabric edge node
questions.explanation: The OpenDaylight Controller uses an Open SDN model with an OpenFlow south- bound interface as defined by the Open Networking Foundation (ONF). The ONF SDN model centralizes most control plane functions. The APIC model for data centers partially centralizes control plane functions. The APIC-EM controller (as of time of publication) makes no changes to the control plane of routers and switches, leaving those to run with a completely distributed con

questions.question: Which answer best describes the meaning of the term configuration drift?
options: Changes to a single device’s configuration over time versus that single device’s
Larger and larger sections of unnecessary configuration in a device
Changes to a single device’s configuration over time versus other devices that
Differences in device configuration versus a centralized backup copy
questions.explanation: The SDA underlay consists of the network devices and connections, along with configuration that allows IP connectivity between the SDA nodes, for the purpose of supporting overlay VXLAN tunnels. The fabric includes both the underlay and overlay, while VXLAN refers to the protocol used to create the tunnels used by the overlay.

questions.question: An enterprise moves away from manual configuration methods, making changes by editing centralized configuration files. Which answers list an issue solved by using a version control system with those centralized files? (Choose two answers.)
options: The ability to find which engineer changed the central configuration file on a
The ability to find the details of what changed in the configuration file over time
The ability to use a template with per-device variables to create configurations
The ability to recognize configuration drift in a device and notify the staff
questions.explanation: The overlay includes the control plane and data plane features to locate the end- points, decide to which fabric node a VXLAN tunnel should connect, direct the frames into the tunnel, and perform VXLAN tunnel encapsulation and de-encapsula- tion. The SDA underlay exists as network devices, links, and a separate IP network to provide connectivity between nodes to support the VXLAN tunnels. The fabric includes both the underlay and overlay, while V

questions.question: Configuration monitoring (also called configuration enforcement) by a configuration management tool generally solves which problem?
options: Tracking the identity of individuals who changed files, along with which files
Listing differences between a former and current configuration
Testing a configuration change to determine whether it will be rejected or not
questions.explanation: The SDA overlay creates VXLAN tunnels between SDA edge nodes. Edge nodes then create a data plane by forwarding frames sent by endpoints over the VXLAN tun- nels. LISP plays a role in the overlay as the control plane, which learns the identifiers of each endpoint, matching the endpoint to the fabric node that can teach the endpoint, so that the overlay knows where to create VXLAN tunnels. For the other incorrect answers, note that while GRE is a

questions.question: Which of the following configuration management tools uses a push model to config- ure network devices?
questions.explanation: As with any SDA feature, the configuration model is to configure the feature using DNA Center, with DNA Center using southbound APIs to communicate the intent to the devices. The methods to configure the feature using DNA Center include using the GUI or using the northbound REST-based API. Of the incorrect answers, you would not normally configure any of the SDA devices directly. Also, while DNA Center can use NETCONF as a southbound protocol to

questions.question: Which of the following configuration manage- ment tools uses agentless architecture for managing network devices?
questions.explanation: Ansible is the correct answer. Agentless architecture means that the configuration management tools do not rely on any code (agent) running on the network device. This is accomplished by communicating with the software that is already installed on the computer, including the operating system and natively installed components. Ansible uses Agentless architecture and relies on features typ- ical in network devices, namely SSH and/or NETCONF, to mak

questions.question: A Layer 2 switch examines a frame’s destination MAC address and forwards that frame out of the port G0/2. That action occurs as part of which plane of the switch?
questions.explanation: Data plane is the correct answer. A Data plane refers to the tasks that a networking device does to for- ward a message. In other words, anything to do with receiving data, processing it, and forwarding that same data—whether you call the data a frame, a packet, or, more generically, a mes- sage—is part of the data plane. The Control plane refers to any action that controls the data 339 plane. Most of these actions have to do with creating the ta

questions.question: Which answer correctly describes the format of the JSON text below? (Choose two answers)
options: One JSON object that has one key:value pair
One JSON object that has two key:value pairs
One JSON object that has three key:value pair 340
Two JSON objects that have two key:value pair
questions.explanation: B and E are the correct answers. JSON defines variables as key:value pairs, with the key on the left of the colon (:) and always enclosed in double quota- tion marks, with the value on the right. The value of an object can be a simple value, array or even another object. The JSON object shown here includes two keys: The first key is: “variable1” The second key is: “var” That means we have an object with a total of two keys, making the second answ

questions.question: Which of the following features of Cisco DNA Center discovers the actual path the packets will take from the source to the destination based on the current forwarding ta- bles?
questions.explanation: Path trace is the correct answer. Encrypted traffic analysis – Enables Cisco DNA to use algo- rithms to recognize security threats even in encrypted traffic. Device 360 and Client 360 – Gives a comprehensive view of the health of the device. Network time travel – Shows past client performance in a timeline for comparison to current behavior. Path trace – Discovers the actual path packets would take from source to destination based on current forw

questions.question: Which HTTP method is generally equivalent to a CRUD “create” operation?
questions.explanation: Ansible is written in Python. D: The Ansible control node connects to port 22 of managed devices. Ansible is written in Python and employs an agentless model in which it uses SSH (TCP port 22) to connect to managed devices. Unlike Puppet and Chef, Ansible uses a push model; the Ansible control node pushes configurations to managed devices, instead of managed devices retrieving configurations from the control node.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which HTTP methods are generally equivalent to a CRUD “update” operation? (select two)
questions.explanation: Puppet is written in Ruby. C: Puppet uses a pull model. Puppet is written in Ruby. Unlike Ansible and Terraform, Puppet is agent-based and employs a pull model in which the Puppet agent on managed devices connects to the REST API on the Puppet master to retrieve configurations.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which HTTP method is used to replace the specified resource?
questions.explanation: YAML Most Ansible files, including inventory and playbook files, are written in YAML , which is popular for its readability and ease of use.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which HTTP response code indicates that the request resource was not found?
questions.explanation: Chef uses a pull model. C: A Chef client must be installed on managed devices. Like Puppet, Chef is agent-based and requires an agent called the “Chef client” to be installed on managed devices. It also uses a pull model, in which the Chef client connects to the Chef server’s REST API to retrieve configurations.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which of the following HTTP response code classes are correct? (select three)
questions.explanation: Ansible B: Chef D: Puppet Of these four infrastructure as code (IaC) tools, Ansible, Chef, and Puppet are primarily used for configuration management, whereas Terraform is primarily used as a provisioning tool.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which of the following statements about REST APIs are true? (select two)
questions.explanation: It can perform complex tasks with less effort than general languages. C: It is a declarative language. Terraform uses HashiCorp Configuration Language (HCL), a domain-specific language (DSL)—a type of computer language specialized for a particular purpose, unlike general-purpose languages like Python or Go. HCL is declarative, meaning that it specifies the desired outcome rather than the specific procedures to achieve that outcome. It can often p
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which statement about caching is accurate with regard to REST APIs?
options: Resources must be marked as cacheable or non-cacheable.
questions.explanation: Puppet C: Chef Both Puppet and Chef use an agent-based model where agents installed on managed devices communicate with a central server (Puppet master or Chef server) to retrieve configuration information and apply it.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which of the following HTTP response codes indicates a successful request?
questions.explanation: Playbook In Ansible, playbooks are the primary files where automation tasks are defined. Playbooks are written in YAML and describe the configurations of managed devices.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: Which REST API authentication method allows a client to access a resource on a server on behalf of the resource’s owner?
questions.explanation: Terraform primarily uses a declarative approach.C: Terraform is written in Go. Terraform is an IaC tool written in the Go programming language. It primarily uses a declarative approach, in which you define the desired end state for the infrastructure rather than specifying the step-by-step instructions for how to achieve that desited state.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.question: In which REST API authentication method does a client authenticate with an authorization server to receive a token that the client includes in its API calls?
questions.explanation: Write, plan, apply The basic Terraform workflow consists of three main steps. The first is write, in which you define the desired state of your infrastructure resources in configuration files using HCL. The second is plan, in which you verify the changes that will be executed. The third is apply, in which you execute the plan to provision and manage the infrastructure resources.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch22: Automation

questions.explanation: Puppet C: Chef Both Puppet and Chef use an agent-based model where agents installed on managed devices communicate with a central server (Puppet master or Chef server) to retrieve configuration information and apply it.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid JSON strings? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Playbook In Ansible, playbooks are the primary files where automation tasks are defined. Playbooks are written in YAML and describe the configurations of managed devices.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch23: REST APIs

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of MPLS over traditional IP routing?
options: Labels enable hardware-speed switching without IP lookup at each router hop
Provides built-in encryption for WAN traffic
Requires no configuration on provider equipment
questions.explanation: MPLS (Multiprotocol Label Switching) adds a 32-bit label between L2 and L3 headers. Label Switching Routers (LSRs) swap labels at each hop — faster than IP table lookups. Provider uses Label Edge Routers (LERs) to add/remove labels at the edge. Supports VPNs, QoS, and traffic engineering.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch53: WAN Architectures

questions.question: What is a leased line in WAN terminology?
options: A dedicated point-to-point circuit providing consistent bandwidth 24/7
A VPN tunnel over the internet
questions.explanation: A leased line (T1/E1, T3/E3) is a dedicated, always-on point-to-point WAN circuit rented from a service provider. Consistent bandwidth is guaranteed (T1=1.544 Mbps, T3=44.7 Mbps). More expensive than broadband but with predictable SLAs. Being replaced by Ethernet WAN and SD-WAN.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch53: WAN Architectures

questions.question: What differentiates SD-WAN from traditional WAN architecture?
options: SD-WAN uses software policies to route traffic across any transport (MPLS, broadband, LTE) intelligently
SD-WAN eliminates the need for routers entirely
SD-WAN is a wireless-only WAN technology
questions.explanation: SD-WAN abstracts the transport layer. Policies route traffic based on application type, path quality metrics, and cost. Can use cheap broadband + MPLS + LTE simultaneously. Application-aware routing: voice goes over low-latency MPLS, bulk data over broadband. Centrally managed — zero-touch provisioning for branches.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch53: WAN Architectures

questions.question: What is the purpose of a demarcation point (demarc) in a WAN connection?
options: The physical boundary between customer equipment (CPE) and the service provider network
A firewall between LAN and WAN
The default gateway for the WAN subnet
questions.explanation: The demarc is where the service provider's responsibility ends and the customer's begins. Typically a jack/port in a telecommunications room. Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) connects on the customer side. Used in legal/billing disputes about who owns/maintains equipment.

questions.question: What WAN technology uses virtual circuits over a shared network infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Frame Relay (legacy) and MPLS use virtual circuits — logical connections across a shared provider network. Permanent Virtual Circuits (PVCs) are always active; Switched Virtual Circuits (SVCs) are on-demand. Unlike leased lines, physical infrastructure is shared but logical circuits appear dedicated.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch53: WAN Architectures

questions.question: What is PPPoE and where is it commonly used?
options: Point-to-Point Protocol over Ethernet — used by DSL ISPs to authenticate subscribers
questions.explanation: PPPoE encapsulates PPP frames within Ethernet frames. Used by DSL providers to authenticate customers (username/password) before granting internet access. The router dials in via PPPoE — CHAP authentication to the ISP. Creates a virtual interface (dialer/PPPoE client) on the router.

questions.question: Three virtual machines run on one physical server. Which of the following server resources are commonly virtualized so each VM can use the required amount of that resource? (Choose three answers.)
questions.explanation: A Metro Ethernet E-Tree service uses a rooted point-to-multipoint Ethernet Virtual Connection (EVC), which means that one site connected to the service (the root) can communicate directly with each of the remote (leaf) sites. However, the leaf sites cannot send frames directly to each other; they can only send frames to the root site. Topology design like this that allows some but not all pairs of devices in the group to communicate is called a p

questions.question: Eight virtual machines run on one physical server; the server has two physical Ethernet NICs. Which answer describes a method that allows all eight VMs to communicate?
options: The VMs must share two IP addresses and coordinate to avoid using duplicate
The hypervisor acts as an IP router using the NICs as routed IP interfaces.
Each VM uses a virtual NIC that is mapped to a physical NIC.
Each VM uses a virtual NIC that logically connects to a virtual switch.
questions.explanation: Metro Ethernet uses Ethernet access links of various types. Time-division multi- plexing (TDM) links such as serial links, even higher-speed links like T3 and E3, do not use Ethernet protocols, and are less likely to be used. MPLS is a WAN technology that creates a Layer 3 service. Two answers refer to Ethernet standards usable as the physical access link for a Metro Ethernet service. However, 100BASE-T supports cable lengths of only 100 meters,

questions.question: Which of the following cloud services is most likely to be used for software development?
questions.explanation: An E-LAN service is one in which the Metro Ethernet service acts as if the WAN were a single Ethernet switch so that each device can communicate directly to every other device. As a result, the routers sit in the same subnet. With one headquar- ters router and 10 remote sites, each router will have 10 OSPF neighbors.

questions.question: Which of the following cloud services is most likely to be purchased and then used to later install your own software applications?
questions.explanation: A Layer 3 MPLS VPN creates an IP service with a different subnet on each access link. With one headquarters router and 10 remote sites, 11 access links exist, so 11 subnets are used. As for the OSPF neighbor relationships, each enterprise router has a neighbor relation- ship with the MPLS provider edge (PE) router, but not with any of the other enterprise (customer edge) routers. So each remote site router would have only one OSPF neigh- bor rela

questions.question: Which term refers to the phenomenon of an electromagnetic wave bending when it passes from one medium to another one with a different density?
questions.explanation: The AP’s MAC When a wireless client sends a frame to another client associated with the same AP, the receiver address (RA) is set to the AP’s MAC address when the client transmits the frame. The AP then forwards the frame to the destination client.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch16: WAN

questions.question: Which term refers to the erratic spreading out of an electromagnetic wave as the result of encountering a surface or medium with irregularities?
questions.explanation: Beacon messages In passive scanning, a wireless client listens for beacon messages broadcasted by APs. These beacon messages contain information about the network, such as the SSID and supported data rates.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch16: WAN

questions.question: Which kind of service set consists of multiple APs’ coverage areas, integrated into a coherent whole offering connectivity for clients?
questions.explanation: CTS/RTS Clear to Send (CTS) and Request to Send (RTS) are 802.11 control messages, not management messages. Management messages include Probe request/response, Authentication request/response, and Association request/response.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch16: WAN

questions.question: Which frequency bands used by 802.11 wireless LANs offer many non-overlapping channels? (select two)
questions.explanation: Lightweight AP A lightweight access point (LWAP) is managed by a wireless LAN controller (WLC). The LWAP relies on the WLC for management functions, configuration, and control.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch16: WAN

questions.question: Which 2.4 GHz channel pattern is generally recommended to avoid interference between adjacent APs?
questions.explanation: LWAP in local mode An LWAP in local mode typically connects to the DS via an access link. Because all data traffic is tunneled to the WLC using Control and Provisioning of Wireless Access Points (CAPWAP), the LWAP’s connection to the distribution system (DS) typically only needs to support the management VLAN.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch16: WAN

questions.question: Which of the following uniquely identifies each AP in an ESS?
questions.explanation: Sniffer In Sniffer mode, an LWAP captures 802.11 frames, which can be exported and analyzed using tools like Wireshark.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch16: WAN

questions.question: What are the three main requirements for VoIP traffic that QoS must guarantee?
options: High bandwidth, low cost, no jitter
questions.explanation: Cisco's VoIP SLA requirements: one-way delay under 150ms (perceived as natural conversation), jitter under 30ms (variation in delay causes choppy audio), packet loss under 1% (drops cause audio gaps). Without QoS, congestion can violate all three simultaneously.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch47: QoS Part 2

questions.question: What is the purpose of marking packets with DSCP values?
options: To tag the IP header so downstream devices can identify and queue traffic by priority
questions.explanation: DSCP (Differentiated Services Code Point) is a 6-bit field in the IP ToS byte. Routers/switches use the DSCP value to select queues: EF(46)=voice, AF41=video, CS1=scavenger. Marking should happen at the trust boundary (typically access layer) — not by end-user devices.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch47: QoS Part 2

questions.question: What is LLQ (Low Latency Queuing) and why is it used for voice?
options: A queue that drops voice packets
A strict priority queue — voice packets are always serviced first, before other queues
A shared queue with equal weighting
A buffer that delays voice for synchronization
questions.explanation: LLQ (also called PQ-CBWFQ) adds a strict priority queue to CBWFQ. Packets in the priority queue are ALWAYS dequeued first. Voice is placed in LLQ so it always gets bandwidth immediately, guaranteeing low delay and jitter. Risk: starvation of other queues if voice exceeds its allocation — use police() to limit.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch47: QoS Part 2

questions.question: What is the trust boundary in a QoS deployment?
options: The firewall between LAN and WAN
The point in the network where device markings are trusted and not re-marked
The router interface facing the ISP
The DSCP value assigned to voice traffic
questions.explanation: Trust boundary = where the network accepts a device's DSCP marking as-is. IP phones are typically trusted (mark EF/CS5 themselves). PCs are NOT trusted — switches re-mark PC traffic to CS1 or BE at the access layer. Place the trust boundary as close to trusted devices as possible; never trust end users to mark their own traffic correctly.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch47: QoS Part 2

questions.question: A router is receiving more traffic than its WAN link can send. What QoS mechanism buffers excess traffic and sends it smoothly?
options: Shaping — buffers and delays excess traffic
questions.explanation: Traffic shaping queues excess traffic and releases it at the configured rate (e.g., to match an ISP's CIR). Smooth output — no abrupt drops, but adds delay/jitter. Used on the customer side toward the ISP. Policing drops (or re-marks) excess instead. Choice depends on whether you prefer delay (shaping) or drops (policing).
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch47: QoS Part 2

questions.question: What DSCP value should be used for VoIP RTP (voice payload) traffic?
questions.explanation: EF (Expedited Forwarding, DSCP 46) is reserved for voice payload (RTP). Provides the highest priority treatment. CS5 (40) marks VoIP call signaling (SIP/H.323). AF41 (34) marks interactive video. CS1 (8) marks scavenger class (lowest priority — P2P, YouTube backfill). BE (0) = best effort/default.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab — Ch47: QoS Part 2

questions.question: Which of the following summarized subnets represent routes that could have been created for CIDR’s goal to reduce the size of Internet routing tables?
questions.explanation: CIDR’s original intent was to allow the summarization of multiple Class A, B, and C networks to reduce the size of Internet routing tables. Of the answers, only 200.1.0.0 255.255.0.0 summarizes multiple networks.

questions.question: A malicious user who intends to harm the security of an organization’s systems is an example of what?
questions.explanation: The port’s operational mode is determined by DTP. To enable Port Security on a port, you must manually configure it as an access or trunk port with switchport mode { access | trunk } . If the switch’s mode is dynamic auto or dynamic desirable (meaning it uses DTP to determine its operational mode), the switchport port-security command will be rejected.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: A DHCP exhaustion attack is an example of which attack types? (select two)
questions.explanation: Discard the violating frames B: Increment the violation counter E: Error-disable the port The default Port Security violation mode is “shutdown”. This means that, if a Port Security violation occurs on a port, the switch will discard violating frames, increment the violation counter, and error-disable the port— effectively shutting it down.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of social engineering? (select three)
questions.explanation: Dynamic secure MAC addresses don’t age out by default. One key difference between secure MAC addresses and regular MAC addresses in a switch’s MAC address table is that dynamic secure MAC addresses configured for Port Security do not age out by default, while regular dynamic MAC addresses do age out after a certain period of inactivity.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following are examples of physical access control? (select two)
questions.explanation: errdisable recovery cause psecure-violation The command to enable ErrDisable Recovery is errdisable recovery cause cause , and the keyword to enable it for ports disabled by Port Security is psecure-violation .
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following are common advantages of using a password manager? (select three)
questions.explanation: SW1(config-if)# switchport port-security mac-address sticky The switchport port-security mac-address sticky command in interface config mode enables the sticky learning feature, which saves dynamically-learned secure MAC addresses in the device’s running-config.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following commands configures an enable secret using type-9 hashing?
questions.explanation: enable algorithm-type scrypt secret AcingTheCCNAExam! The command to configure an enable secret with a specific hashing algorithm is enable algorithm-type algorithm secret secret . The scrypt algorithm (Cisco’s recommendation), is type 9.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of MFA?
options: Entering a username/password and a PIN
Performing a fingerprint scan and a retina scan
Scanning a badge and entering a PIN
Scanning a badge and tapping a phone notification
questions.explanation: Scanning a badge and entering a PIN MFA involves verifying a user’s identity via at least two “factors”: knowledge (something you know), possession (something you have), and inherence (something you are). A badge is an example of something you have, and a PIN is an example of something you know.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following provides PNAC?
questions.explanation: 802.1X 802.1X is a security protocol that provides port-based network access control (PNAC), ensuring that only authenticated devices can access network resources.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which AAA element consists of granting a user or device appropriate access?
questions.explanation: Authorization AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting. Authentication is the process of verifying identity, authorization is the process of granting appropriate access, and accounting is the process of keeping track of activities.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which AAA protocol combines authentication and authorization into a single operation?
questions.explanation: RADIUS Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service (RADIUS) is an AAA protocol that combines authentication and authorization into a single operation, unlike Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System Plus (TACACS+), which keeps all three AAA components separate.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of stateless packet filtering?
options: An ACL applied to a router interface
questions.explanation: An ACL applied to a router interface Stateless packet filtering, such as an ACL applied to a router interface, filters packets on a per-packet basis without considering other context such as the packet’s relation to other packets.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which component of 802.1X is the network device that the client connects to?
questions.explanation: Authenticator 802.1X defines three roles: the supplicant (the client device that wants to connect to the network), the authenticator (the network device that the supplicant connects to), and the authentication server (the server that verifies the supplicant’s credentials). Chapter 12: Port Security
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch10: QoS

questions.question: Which OSI layer handles logical IP addressing and inter-network routing?
questions.explanation: Layer 3 (Network) handles IP addressing and routing decisions. Routers are Layer 3 devices. Layer 2 uses MAC addresses. Layer 4 handles port numbers and reliability (TCP/UDP).

questions.question: What device operates at Layer 2 and forwards frames using MAC addresses?
questions.explanation: A switch operates at OSI Layer 2 and makes forwarding decisions using its MAC address table (CAM table). Hubs are Layer 1 — they repeat all traffic to all ports with no intelligence. Routers operate at Layer 3.

questions.question: What protocol automatically assigns IP address, subnet mask, default gateway, and DNS to hosts?
questions.explanation: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) provides IP configuration via DORA: Discover → Offer → Request → Acknowledge. Operates on UDP ports 67 (server) and 68 (client).

questions.question: Which protocol maps IP addresses to MAC addresses on a local network?
questions.explanation: ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) resolves Layer 3 IP addresses to Layer 2 MAC addresses. Results are cached in the ARP table (arp -a on most OS). Gratuitous ARP is used to announce or update mappings.

questions.question: What is the difference between TCP and UDP?
options: TCP is faster; UDP is slower
TCP is reliable/connection-oriented; UDP is best-effort/connectionless
TCP is for LAN only; UDP is for WAN
TCP uses port numbers; UDP does not
questions.explanation: TCP provides ordered, reliable, error-checked delivery via acknowledgments and retransmissions. UDP is connectionless with no delivery guarantee — lower overhead, used for DNS, VoIP, streaming, TFTP where speed matters more than reliability.

questions.question: A ping from PC1 to PC2 fails. Both are on the same /24 subnet. What is the FIRST troubleshoot step?
options: Ping the loopback address 127.0.0.1 to verify the IP stack
questions.explanation: Troubleshooting follows near-to-far: (1) ping 127.0.0.1 — loopback tests local IP stack. (2) Ping own IP. (3) Ping default gateway. (4) Ping remote host. (5) Ping DNS server. This isolates whether the problem is local, on the LAN, or upstream.

questions.question: What does ICMP do and what command uses it?
options: Provides error reporting and diagnostics — used by ping and traceroute
questions.explanation: ICMP (Internet Control Message Protocol) is a Layer 3 protocol used for error reporting and diagnostics. Ping uses ICMP Echo Request/Reply. Traceroute uses ICMP Time Exceeded messages with incrementing TTL values to map network hops.

questions.question: What is the purpose of TTL (Time to Live) in an IP packet?
options: Encrypts the packet for secure transit
Limits packet lifetime — prevents infinite routing loops
Specifies the packet priority for QoS
questions.explanation: TTL is decremented by 1 at each router hop. When TTL reaches 0, the router discards the packet and sends an ICMP "Time Exceeded" message back to the source. This prevents routing loops from consuming bandwidth indefinitely. Windows default TTL=128, Linux=64.

questions.question: What port does HTTPS use and what does TLS provide?
options: Port 443 — TLS provides encryption and authentication
questions.explanation: HTTPS = HTTP over TLS, port 443. TLS (Transport Layer Security) provides: (1) Encryption — data is unreadable in transit. (2) Authentication — certificates verify the server. (3) Integrity — ensures data hasn't been tampered with. TLS 1.3 is current standard; SSLv3 and TLS 1.0/1.1 are deprecated.

questions.question: What is the difference between a hub and a switch?
options: Hub is faster; switch is more secure
Hub floods all traffic to all ports (shared bandwidth); switch forwards intelligently per MAC
Hub is Layer 3; switch is Layer 2
Hub supports VLANs; switch does not
questions.explanation: Hubs are Layer 1 "dumb" repeaters — every frame goes to every port, creating a shared collision domain (CSMA/CD needed). Switches are Layer 2 intelligent — each port is its own collision domain, traffic is forwarded only to the correct port based on MAC table.

questions.question: What is the difference between a broadcast domain and a collision domain?
options: Collision domain = shared media segment; broadcast domain = area where broadcasts propagate
Broadcast domain = single switch port; collision domain = entire VLAN
Routers segment collision domains; switches segment broadcast domains
questions.explanation: Collision domain: a network segment where two devices can simultaneously transmit and cause a collision. Each switch port = one collision domain. Broadcast domain: all devices that receive a broadcast frame. Routers separate broadcast domains (each router interface = one broadcast domain).

questions.question: What is the purpose of DNS, and on which port does it operate?
questions.explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) translates FQDNs (e.g., www.google.com) to IP addresses. Runs on UDP port 53 (small queries) and TCP port 53 (large responses, zone transfers). Hierarchy: Root (.) → TLD (.com) → SLD (google) → subdomain (www).

questions.question: What is NAT and why is it used?
options: Translates private IPs to public IPs for internet access; conserves IPv4 space
A QoS mechanism for prioritizing traffic
questions.explanation: NAT (Network Address Translation) maps private RFC1918 IPs to public IPs. PAT (Port Address Translation) — the most common form — allows many private hosts to share one public IP using different source port numbers. NAT creates a stateful translation table to track sessions.

questions.question: What happens at each layer when a PC sends data to a web server?
options: Layer 7 first, then down to Layer 1 (encapsulation)
Layer 1 first, then up to Layer 7 (de-encapsulation)
questions.explanation: Sending (encapsulation): App data → TCP segment (L4 adds port/seq) → IP packet (L3 adds src/dst IP) → Ethernet frame (L2 adds MAC) → bits (L1). Receiving (de-encapsulation): bits → frame → packet → segment → data. Each layer adds/removes its header.

questions.question: What is the IPv4 loopback address?
questions.explanation: 127.0.0.1 (or the entire 127.0.0.0/8 range) is reserved for loopback — traffic sent to this address never leaves the host. Pinging 127.0.0.1 verifies the TCP/IP stack is working. In IPv6, ::1 is the loopback.

questions.question: What is an APIPA address and when is it assigned?
options: A static IP assigned by a sysadmin
A 169.254.x.x address auto-assigned when DHCP fails
A public IP assigned by an ISP
questions.explanation: APIPA (Automatic Private IP Addressing) assigns a 169.254.0.0/16 address when a device can't reach a DHCP server. The device can only communicate with other APIPA devices on the same link. Seeing 169.254.x.x means DHCP is unreachable — check the DHCP server or connectivity.

questions.question: What is the function of STP (Spanning Tree Protocol)?
options: Speeds up convergence in routing tables
Prevents Layer 2 loops by blocking redundant paths
questions.explanation: STP (802.1D) creates a loop-free Layer 2 topology by electing a Root Bridge and blocking redundant paths. Port states: Blocking → Listening → Learning → Forwarding. RSTP (802.1w) converges in ~1-2 seconds vs. STP's 30-50 seconds. PVST+ runs per VLAN.

questions.question: What does the "ip default-gateway" command do on a Layer 2 switch?
options: Sets the management traffic default gateway so admins can reach the switch from other networks
Assigns an IP to a VLAN
questions.explanation: A Layer 2 switch with a management IP (SVI) needs ip default-gateway to forward its OWN traffic to other networks (e.g., SSH from a remote subnet). This does NOT enable routing. For hosts, the default gateway is set differently. On L3 switches, ip routing replaces this.

questions.question: /24 subnet has 8 host bits. How many usable host IPs?
questions.explanation: Formula: 2^(host bits) − 2. 2^8 = 256 total. Minus network (.0) and broadcast (.255) = 254 usable. This formula works for any prefix except /31 (RFC3021, 2 hosts no subtract) and /32 (single host).

questions.question: A /30 subnet is used for point-to-point router links. How many usable hosts?
questions.explanation: /30 = 2 host bits. 4 total addresses. Minus network+broadcast = 2 usable. Perfect for router-to-router WAN links. /31 (RFC3021) gives 2 hosts with no subtraction, used on modern P2P links.

questions.question: What is the network address of 192.168.10.130/25?
questions.explanation: /25 mask = 255.255.255.128. Block size=128. Subnets: .0 and .128. Since 130>128, the network is 192.168.10.128. Broadcast=.255. Range:.129-.254.

questions.question: How many /26 subnets can you create from a /24?
questions.explanation: Going from /24 to /26 borrows 2 bits (26−24=2). 2^2=4 subnets. Each /26 has 64 addresses, 62 usable hosts. The subnets are .0, .64, .128, .192.

questions.question: You need 500 hosts in a subnet. What is the minimum prefix length?
questions.explanation: Need ≥500 hosts. 2^9−2=510 usable. 9 host bits = /23 (32−9=23). /24 only gives 254 — too few. /23 gives 510 — just right.

questions.question: What wildcard mask corresponds to /24?
questions.explanation: Wildcard mask = inverse of subnet mask. /24 = 255.255.255.0. Inverse: 0.0.0.255. Cisco ACLs and OSPF network statements use wildcard masks. 0=must match, 1=ignore (don't care).

questions.question: What is the valid host range for 172.16.0.0/12?
questions.explanation: /12 = 255.240.0.0. Network=172.16.0.0. The host portion spans the last 20 bits. Second octet ranges 16-31 (16 values). Full range: 172.16.0.1 to 172.31.255.254. This is one of the three RFC1918 private ranges.

questions.question: VLSM: A /24 block needs subnets for 100, 50, 20, and 10 hosts. What prefixes are used (best fit)?
During a TFTP transfer using Cisco Routers what is the numeric value, in decimal, of the destination port field of the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) header?
questions.explanation: Trivial File Transfer (TFTP) (port 69) Domain Name System (port 53) IEN-116 Name Server (port 42) Time service (port 37) NetBIOS Name Server (port 137) NetBIOS Datagram Server (port 138) BootP client (port 68) BootP server (port 67) File Transfer Protocol (port 20) TACACS service (port 49) Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (25) File Transfer Protocol (21) Simple Network Management Protocol (161)

questions.question: Reverse Address Resolution Protocol (RARP) is the process:
options: Where an IP host resolves it's IP address by broadcasting it's MAC address to the network and a RARP server assigns it an IP address.
Where an IP host resolves it's MAC address by broadcasting to the local network it's IP address.
Where an IP host tries to resolve the MAC address of a destination by sending a network broadcast. The destination responds to the broadcast with it's MAC address.
Where an IP host resolves a destination IP address by sending a broadcast on the local network and the destination host responds with it's IP address.

questions.question: Of the following commands, identify the correct one for clearing the IP arp table in memory of the router.
Collisions occur when
options: multiple packets are placed on a serial link
multiple stations listen for traffic and transmit at the same time
when multiple tokens are on a ring simultaneously
questions.explanation: Token ring is a token passing process a station can transmit only when he has the token. There never can be multiple tokens on the ring. Early token release allows up to two data frames to be transmitted on a token ring LAN at the same time. Collisions do not occur on a token ring.

questions.question: How many subnets are possible with a Class C address and a subnet mask of 255.255.255.252?
Which of the following are true of access lists?
options: Access list should have at least one permit statement.
The last configured line should always be a permit statement.
Every access-list will implicitly deny all traffic.

questions.question: If the access-group command is configured on an interface and there is no access-list created which of the following is most correct?
options: The command will be executed and deny all traffic out.
The command will be executed and permit all traffic out.
The command will be executed and permit all traffic in and out.
The command will be executed and deny all traffic in and out.
questions.explanation: 1. Create the list

questions.question: What is the result of the command? " access-list 101 permit tcp any 172.16.0.0 0.0.255.255 established"
options: telnet sessions will be permitted regardless of the source address
telnet sessions will be denied regardless of the source address
telnet sessions will be denied if initiated from any address other than 172.16.0.0 network
telnet sessions will be permitted to the 172.16.0.0 network only
telnet sessions will be denied to the 172.16.0.0 network only

questions.question: If Host A sends a packet to Host B over ethernet and Host B is not active:
options: The packet will be removed by Host A.
The NVRAM of Host B will remove the packet.
The packet will "die" when it reaches the terminator.
The packet will be removed by the token monitor.

questions.question: If Host A sends a packet to Host B over token ring, and Host B is not active:
options: The packet will be removed by Host A.
The NVRAM of Host B will remove the packet.
The packet will "die" when it reaches the terminator.
The packet will be removed by the token monitor.

questions.question: If Host A sends a packet to Host B over token ring: Host A dies after transmitting, and Host B is not active:
options: The packet will be removed by Host A.
The NVRAM of Host B will remove the packet.
The packet will "die" when it reaches the terminator.
The packet will be removed by the Active monitor.

questions.question: BECN and FECN are:
options: Used to provide keepalives in a Frame Relay environment
Used to provide error checking in a Frame Relay environment
Used to provide congestion information in a Frame Relay environment
Used to provide flow control parameters in a Frame Relay environment
BECN and FECN are not used in a Frame Relay environment.
