questions.explanation: Backward Explicit Congestion Notification (BECN) - Bit set by a frame relay network in frames traveling in opposite direction of frames encountering a congested path. DTE receiving frames with the BECN bit set can request higher level protocols take flow control action.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a ROUTING protocol:
Which statement is NOT true about link state protocols:
options: Link state advertisements are sent to all routers in an area.
Each router executes the SPF algorithm.
Link state routers keep a topological database of the network.
Link state routers include the originating router's routing table.
Link state routers know of possible alternate paths to a particular network.

questions.question: Given the IP address of 193.243.12.43 and a subnet mask of 255.255.255.128, what is the subnet address?
questions.explanation: I have used the slash format to represent the mask. This means that the first 25 bits represent the address of the wire.

questions.question: What is the default route on an IP network? Choose one.
options: This is the route that will be chosen first by IP for delivery of a datagram.
The address of the nearest downstream neighbor in a Token ring network.
At the router, it is the route used to direct frames for which the next hop has not been explicitly listed in the routing table.
Any static route listing in a routing table.
When all conditions are equal, the best route for delivery of IP datagrams.

questions.question: A caret symbol (^) inserted into a failed command indicates which of the following. Choose one.
options: Numeric data is required for this command.
The point in a command where you can simply press return to complete the syntax string.
The point in a command where you have entered an incorrect command, keyword or argument.
The point in a command where you must enter a required value.
This is a normal indicator for any failed command.

questions.question: The metric used by IPX RIP is:
Which one of the following masks has the nickname of the "serial mask"?
The Catalyst 5000 uses which switching mode?
questions.explanation: Cut-through, on the other hand, starts forwarding the frame as soon as the destination address is read. This mode reduces latency in the switch.

questions.question: Which of the following layers would most likely provide connection oriented service.
Which of the following layers defines network addressing and determines the best path?
Segments are converted to Datagrams at which layer?
Which of the following could be a feature of a connection-oriented service?
Simple Network Management Protocol uses what numeric value, in decimal, of the destination port field of the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) header?
When a Destination Service Access Point (DSAP) and Source Service Access Point (SSAP) are set to AA:
options: All stations with AA contained in their MAC addresses would receive this frame.
All stations would receive this frame, because it acts like a broadcast.
The encapsulated protocol contained in the frame must be IP.
The encapsulated protocol contained in the frame is proprietary.
questions.explanation: The above capture is Cisco's proprietary Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP).

questions.question: What Cisco feature of RIP will reduce convergence time?
options: Cisco uses full horizons as opposed to split horizons
The hold down timer is not activated
There is no Cisco feature to enhance the performance of IP RIP
questions.explanation: I. Interior Routing Protocols

questions.question: What condition does Serial 0 down & protocol up represent?
options: The interface is disconnected but keep alives are being received
The interface is working properly but keep alives are not being received
The interface is bad and should be replaced
questions.explanation: Let's review all the possibilities:

questions.question: What is the purpose of TTL?
options: Remind engineers that life is short
To prevent IP datagrams from living long and fruitful lives
To prevent IP datagrams that are caught in a routing loop from traversing the network forever.
questions.explanation: Usually 255 on an ethernet segment

questions.question: The size of a window in a TCP segment establishes what? (Choose one.)
options: The maximum number of bytes that a single frame may not exceed
The allowable bandwidth for this TCP session
The number of octets that the receiver must be willing to accept
The number of 32-bit words in the header
The number of octets that the sender is willing to accept

questions.question: TCP "source" port number(s): (Choose the one that best describes.)
options: Depends on the application being accessed on the destination host.
Depends on the application running on the source host.
Are usually assigned by the source host, and are typically some number greater than 1023.
Are assigned by the source host and are usually one of the "well known" port numbers.
Must be negotiated by the sender and receiver during session setup.

questions.question: In a TCP acknowledgment, the ack number refers to: (Choose one.)
options: A sequential number beginning with 1, and incremented by 1 with each exchange, that identifies this exchange of packets as being complete
The number of the packet expected next
The number of the packet expected next, plus 1
A number agreed upon at session establishment, which will be used by the receiver to indicate a successful exchange of packets
questions.explanation: Check out the sequence and the acknowledgement numbers below:

questions.question: What would the class and major network be for the IP address 191.12.45.254? (Choose one.)
questions.explanation: - If the first bit is 0, it's a Class A address. - If the first two bits are10, it's a Class B address. - If the first three bits are 110 and the value is less than 224, it's a class C address. - No address can have a value of zero (0) in the first octet. - The address of 127 will be reserved for loopback addresses. - Addresses with a value of 10 will be reserved for private use.

questions.question: What statement about the IP address 0.10.10.10 is most correct? (Choose one.)
options: For a Class A address, indicates "this route."
This is not a legal address.
This address can only be used for a network that is not exposed to the Internet.
questions.explanation: - If the first bit is 0, it's a Class A address - If the first two bits are10, it's a Class B address. - If the first three bits are 110 and the value is less than 224, it's a class C address. - No address can have a value of zero (0) in the first octet - The address of 127 will be reserved for loopback addresses - Addresses with a value of 10 will be reserved for private use

questions.question: What would be the maximum number of hosts that a class C address could support assuming that the default subnet mask was used? (Choose one.)
questions.explanation: The formula of 2 N -2 (where n is the total number of host bits) provides us with the total number of hosts.

questions.question: The Class addresses that an administrator would most likely assign to a host are: (Choose one.)
options: Class A and B addresses only
Class A, B, C and D addresses
Class A, B, C, D and E addresses
Class A, B, and C addresses
questions.explanation: Questions 1-7 | 8-12

questions.question: The way that TCP uses acknowledgements is best referred to as: (Choose one.)
questions.explanation: Refer to the captures below:

questions.question: The protocol IP is best described as: (Choose one.)
options: Determines the data link address for known MAC addresses
Determines network addresses when data link addresses are known
Provides connectionless, best effort delivery routing of datagrams
Provides connection oriented, reliable delivery of datagrams
Provides connection oriented, best effort delivery routing of datagrams

questions.question: The protocol ICMP is best described as: (Choose one.)
options: Helps IP by determining the data link addresses for known IP addresses
Resolves MAC addresses to IP addresses
Resolves IP addresses to MAC addresses
Adds a reliable connection-oriented service to IP
Helps IP by providing control and messaging capabilities
questions.explanation: ICMP echo request:

questions.question: What device makes forwarding decisions based on MAC addresses and operates at Layer 2?
questions.explanation: Switches operate at Layer 2 and forward Ethernet frames using MAC address tables (CAM tables). Routers are Layer 3. Hubs are Layer 1 — they flood everything to all ports.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Which protocol automatically assigns IP configuration (IP, mask, gateway, DNS) to hosts?
questions.explanation: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) dynamically leases IP configuration. The 4-step DORA process is: client broadcasts Discover → server responds Offer → client sends Request → server confirms Acknowledge.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: In what order does encapsulation happen as your laptop sends the packet?
questions.explanation: Encapsulation is top-down: App data → TCP/UDP Segment (L4) → IP Packet (L3) → Ethernet Frame (L2) → Bits (L1). The receiving end de-encapsulates in reverse.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the function of ARP?
options: Map IP addresses to MAC addresses on a LAN
questions.explanation: ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) resolves Layer 3 IP addresses to Layer 2 MAC addresses. A device broadcasts "Who has IP x.x.x.x?" and the owner replies with its MAC. Results are cached in the ARP table.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: TCP is connection-oriented. What three-step process establishes a TCP connection?
questions.explanation: The TCP 3-way handshake: Client SYN → Server SYN-ACK → Client ACK. Only after this exchange does data transfer begin. Firewalls track this "state" to allow return traffic — that's stateful inspection from your Sec+.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What protocols use ports 443 and 23, and why block 23?
options: HTTPS (443) and Telnet (23) — Telnet is plaintext
HTTP (443) and FTP (23) — FTP is slow
HTTPS (443) and SSH (23) — SSH is deprecated
questions.explanation: 443 = HTTPS (TLS-encrypted). 23 = Telnet — transmits everything including passwords in cleartext. SSH (port 22) replaced Telnet. Blocking 23 is basic network security hygiene you'd recognize from Sec+.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is a unicast vs broadcast vs multicast transmission?
questions.explanation: Unicast: one-to-one. Broadcast (255.255.255.255): one-to-all on segment. Multicast (224.x.x.x): one-to-subscribed group (e.g., OSPF uses 224.0.0.5). IPv6 eliminates broadcast entirely — uses multicast instead.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the purpose of NAT (Network Address Translation)?
options: Encrypt traffic between private and public networks
Translate private IP addresses to public IPs for internet communication
Assign IPv6 addresses to IPv4 devices
questions.explanation: NAT translates private RFC1918 IPs (e.g., 192.168.x.x) to one or more public IPs as traffic leaves the network. PAT (Port Address Translation) is the most common form — many private IPs share one public IP using different port numbers. This is why your home has one public IP for many devices.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Which protocol resolves domain names (like google.com) to IP addresses?
questions.explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) translates human-readable names to IPs. It operates on UDP port 53 (TCP for large responses). Your devices query a DNS server first before making any web connection. Hierarchical: Root → TLD (.com) → Authoritative DNS.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the difference between half-duplex and full-duplex Ethernet?
options: Half=one direction at a time (CSMA/CD), Full=simultaneous send and receive
questions.explanation: Half-duplex: one direction at a time, uses CSMA/CD collision detection (old hubs). Full-duplex: simultaneous send/receive, no collisions — modern switched networks are all full-duplex. When you connect to a switch port, it should auto-negotiate to full-duplex.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: A /24 has 8 host bits. How many usable host IPs?
questions.explanation: 2^8 = 256 total. Subtract 1 for network (.0) and 1 for broadcast (.255) = 254 usable hosts. Formula always: 2^(host bits) - 2.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: /30 subnets are used for point-to-point router links. How many usable hosts?
questions.explanation: /30 = 2 host bits. 4 total addresses. Minus network + broadcast = 2 usable. Perfect for a router-to-router link (one IP per router interface). You'll see /30 and /31 on every real network.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the network address for host 192.168.10.130/25?
questions.explanation: /25 mask = 255.255.255.128. Block size = 128. Two subnets: .0-.127 and .128-.255. Since 130 > 128, host is in the second subnet. Network = 192.168.10.128, broadcast = .255, range = .129-.254.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Which subnet fits all 50 Sales hosts with minimal waste?
questions.explanation: /26 = 64 total, 62 usable. Fits 50 with room to grow. /25 wastes 76 addresses. /27 (30) is too small. Right-sizing = less waste, better security segmentation.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: You need 6 subnets from 192.168.5.0/24. What is the minimum prefix length?
questions.explanation: /27 creates 2^3 = 8 subnets from /24 (each has 30 usable hosts). /26 only creates 4 subnets. /27 gives you 8 subnets, meeting the 6-subnet requirement with 2 spare.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the valid host range for the private 172.16.0.0/12 network?
questions.explanation: /12 mask = 255.240.0.0. Network = 172.16.0.0. The variable portion starts at the 2nd octet. 172.16 through 172.31 (16 values for second octet). Range: 172.16.0.1 to 172.31.255.254. This is one of the RFC1918 private ranges.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does VLSM (Variable Length Subnet Masking) allow you to do?
options: Use different subnet sizes within the same major network
questions.explanation: VLSM lets you use different prefix lengths within the same major network. For example: /30 for router links (2 hosts), /27 for small offices (30 hosts), /24 for large floors. Without VLSM (classful routing), you'd waste huge blocks on tiny links.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: How many host bits does a /20 subnet have, and how many usable hosts does it support?
questions.explanation: 32 - 20 = 12 host bits. 2^12 = 4,096 total. 4,096 - 2 = 4,094 usable hosts. /20 is common for large enterprise VLANs or AWS VPC subnets.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does a router use to make forwarding decisions?
questions.explanation: The routing table (RIB) maps destination networks to outgoing interfaces or next-hop IPs. It contains: directly connected routes, static routes, and dynamically learned routes from OSPF, EIGRP, etc.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Which concept determines which routing source a router trusts MORE when multiple protocols know a route?
questions.explanation: Administrative Distance (AD) ranks routing source trustworthiness. Connected=0, Static=1, EIGRP=90, OSPF=110, RIP=120. When two protocols know the same destination, lower AD wins. Only one route enters the routing table (the one with lowest AD).
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Where does R1 forward a packet to 10.1.1.50?
questions.explanation: Longest Prefix Match: all three routes match 10.1.1.50, but /24 is more specific than /16, which is more specific than /0. Router always picks longest match, regardless of AD or metric. This is fundamental to how all IP routing works.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the purpose of a default gateway on a host?
options: Forward traffic to networks the host doesn't know about
questions.explanation: The default gateway is the local router's IP on the host's subnet. Any traffic destined outside the local subnet is forwarded to the default gateway. Without it, the host is isolated to its own subnet — it can't reach anything beyond.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What type of routing protocol is OSPF, and what algorithm does it use?
questions.explanation: OSPF (Open Shortest Path First) is a Link State protocol. Routers flood LSAs (Link State Advertisements) to build a complete topology database, then run Dijkstra's Shortest Path First (SPF) algorithm to find best routes. EIGRP is hybrid/advanced distance vector using DUAL. BGP is path vector.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Which route wins for destination 10.1.5.1?
questions.explanation: When prefix length is equal, Administrative Distance decides. Static=1, OSPF=110. Static route wins. To prefer OSPF, you'd need to adjust AD or remove the static route. This is why knowing AD values matters on the exam.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does the "ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 [next-hop]" command create?
options: A host route for one specific IP
A default route — matches any destination not in the routing table
A summary route for all RFC1918 space
A null route to blackhole traffic
questions.explanation: "ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0" creates the default route — the routing table's "catch-all." Any packet without a more specific match gets forwarded via this route. On a branch router, this typically points toward the ISP. Equivalent to "ip default-gateway" on L2 switches.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of VLANs?
options: Logically segment a network into separate broadcast domains on the same switch
questions.explanation: VLANs create separate Layer 2 broadcast domains without separate hardware. VLAN 10=Sales, VLAN 20=Engineering — broadcasts don't cross VLANs. Security, performance, and management benefit. Think: separate isolated bus systems on same physical hardware.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is a trunk port on a switch?
options: A port connecting to end devices (PCs)
A port carrying multiple VLANs simultaneously using 802.1Q tags
A port used only for management
A port that blocks all traffic
questions.explanation: Trunk ports carry multiple VLANs between switches or to a router using 802.1Q (dot1q) tagging. Each frame gets a 4-byte VLAN tag inserted. Access ports carry one VLAN with no tag — used for end devices. Switch-to-switch and switch-to-router links are trunks.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Why not, and how do you fix it?
options: VLANs are separate L2 domains; needs a router or L3 switch for inter-VLAN routing
questions.explanation: VLANs are separate broadcast domains by design — no direct L2 communication. Solutions: (1) Router on a Stick: router with 802.1Q subinterfaces on a trunk. (2) Layer 3 switch with SVIs (Switched Virtual Interfaces). This is inter-VLAN routing.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) prevent?
options: Layer 2 broadcast storms from switching loops
questions.explanation: STP (802.1D) prevents Layer 2 loops by blocking redundant paths, creating a loop-free tree topology. Without STP, broadcast storms saturate all bandwidth instantly — no TTL on L2 frames. Modern networks use RSTP (802.1w) for sub-second convergence. Port states: Blocking → Listening → Learning → Forwarding.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the native VLAN on a trunk port?
options: The management VLAN for switch access
The VLAN for untagged traffic on a trunk link
The highest-numbered VLAN on the switch
The VLAN used for OSPF hello packets
questions.explanation: Native VLAN is where untagged frames are placed on a trunk. Default is VLAN 1. A common security best practice: change native VLAN to an unused VLAN (e.g., VLAN 999) and tag all traffic. VLAN hopping attacks exploit mismatched native VLANs — relevant from your Sec+ background.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does a Layer 3 switch do that a Layer 2 switch cannot?
options: Route packets between VLANs using IP addresses (SVIs)
questions.explanation: Layer 3 switches have routing capability via SVIs (Switched Virtual Interfaces) — one IP per VLAN that acts as the default gateway for hosts in that VLAN. No external router needed for inter-VLAN routing. More efficient than Router-on-a-Stick at scale.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What happens if a 3rd MAC address appears on this port?
options: The port enters err-disabled state and shuts down
The oldest MAC is removed to make room
questions.explanation: With violation mode "shutdown," the port goes into err-disabled state and shuts down immediately. The port won't recover until an admin does "shutdown" then "no shutdown." Alternatives: "restrict" (drops + logs) and "protect" (drops silently). This is MAC flooding prevention from your Sec+.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Standard ACL vs Extended ACL on Cisco IOS — what's the key difference?
options: Standard=src IP only; Extended=src, dest, protocol, port
questions.explanation: Standard ACLs (1-99, 1300-1999) filter on source IP only — place near destination. Extended ACLs (100-199, 2000-2699) filter on source, destination, protocol, and port — place near source to stop traffic early. You know ACLs as firewall rules from Sec+.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does "0.0.0.255" represent in a Cisco ACL?
questions.explanation: Wildcard masks are the inverse of subnet masks. 0.0.0.255 = match first 3 octets exactly, ignore last octet — matches entire /24. 0.0.0.0 = match exact IP. 255.255.255.255 = any. Critical: don't confuse wildcard masks with subnet masks on the exam!
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What attack does Port Security on Cisco switches help prevent?
options: MAC flooding that overflows the CAM table
questions.explanation: MAC flooding overflows the CAM table. A full CAM table causes the switch to flood all frames out all ports (fail-open), allowing the attacker to capture traffic. Port Security limits MACs per port and shuts the port on violation. Direct bridge between your Sec+ and CCNA switching.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is DHCP Snooping and what attack does it prevent?
options: Validates DHCP messages and blocks rogue DHCP servers handing out false gateways
Limits lease time to prevent hoarding
questions.explanation: DHCP Snooping marks ports as trusted (uplinks, real DHCP server) or untrusted (end devices). DHCP Offers from untrusted ports are dropped. Prevents rogue DHCP MITM attacks. Works alongside Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI). Combined, these defeat many MITM attack vectors.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the purpose of SSH vs Telnet for device management?
options: SSH is faster; Telnet is more compatible
SSH encrypts the management session; Telnet sends everything in plaintext
They are functionally identical on Cisco
SSH requires a username; Telnet doesn't
questions.explanation: SSH (Secure Shell, port 22) encrypts the entire management session including credentials. Telnet (port 23) sends everything plaintext — anyone capturing traffic sees your password. On Cisco: "transport input ssh" on vty lines, generate RSA keys, enable "ip ssh version 2." Always disable Telnet on managed devices.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What traffic does this extended ACL permit, and what's the significance of the implicit/explicit deny at the end?
options: Only HTTPS from 192.168.1.0/24 is permitted; all other traffic is logged and denied
All TCP is permitted; UDP is logged
Only HTTP is permitted; HTTPS is blocked
questions.explanation: Line 1: permits TCP from 192.168.1.0/24 to any destination on port 443 (HTTPS). The explicit "deny ip any any log" at the end creates log entries for denied packets — useful for troubleshooting. Every ACL has an implicit "deny any" at the end even if not written. Explicit + log gives you visibility.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: How many bits is an IPv6 address, and how is it written?
options: 128 bits in hexadecimal groups separated by colons
128 bits in decimal groups separated by dots
64 bits in hex separated by dashes
questions.explanation: IPv6 = 128 bits written as 8 groups of 4 hex digits separated by colons. Example: 2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334. You can compress with :: (consecutive zero groups) and drop leading zeros: 2001:db8:85a3::8a2e:370:7334.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What does :: mean in an IPv6 address?
options: One or more consecutive groups of all zeros (can only appear once)
The host portion of the address
questions.explanation: :: replaces one or more consecutive groups of 0000. 2001:db8::1 = 2001:0db8:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0001. Rules: :: can only appear once per address. The loopback address is ::1 (127 zero groups + 1). The unspecified address is :: (all zeros).
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is the IPv6 link-local address range and what is it used for?
options: FE80::/10 — automatically configured, used on local link only
questions.explanation: FE80::/10 addresses are link-local — auto-configured on every IPv6 interface, never forwarded beyond the local link. Used for: neighbor discovery, router discovery, routing protocol hellos (like OSPF). Generated using EUI-64 from the MAC address. Every IPv6 interface will have at least a link-local address.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What replaced ARP in IPv6?
questions.explanation: NDP (Neighbor Discovery Protocol) replaces ARP in IPv6, using ICMPv6 messages. Neighbor Solicitation (NS) = ARP request. Neighbor Advertisement (NA) = ARP reply. NDP also handles Router Discovery (RS/RA), Duplicate Address Detection (DAD), and SLAAC address configuration. More secure than ARP (no broadcasts).
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is SLAAC in IPv6?
options: A routing protocol for IPv6 networks
Stateless Address Autoconfiguration — hosts self-generate a global IPv6 address from the router's prefix
A replacement for NAT in IPv6
questions.explanation: SLAAC (Stateless Address Autoconfiguration) lets hosts auto-configure global IPv6 addresses without DHCP. Process: (1) Host gets /64 prefix from Router Advertisement. (2) Host generates 64-bit interface ID (from MAC via EUI-64 or random). (3) Combines prefix + interface ID = global unicast address. (4) Runs DAD to verify uniqueness.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: An organization receives a /48 prefix. How many /64 subnets can they create?
questions.explanation: From /48 to /64 = 64-48 = 16 bits for subnets. 2^16 = 65,536 possible /64 subnets. Each /64 has 2^64 ≈ 18.4 quintillion host addresses. IPv6 is designed so every subnet is a /64, giving enormous scale. No more fighting over host bits!
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What is EUI-64 and how does it generate an IPv6 interface ID?
options: A method to derive a 64-bit interface ID from a 48-bit MAC address by inserting FFFE and flipping the 7th bit
A routing protocol metric for IPv6
A compression algorithm for IPv6 headers
questions.explanation: EUI-64 converts a 48-bit MAC to a 64-bit interface ID: (1) Split MAC at 24-bit boundary. (2) Insert FF:FE in the middle. (3) Flip bit 7 (universal/local bit) of the first byte. Example: MAC 00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E → 02:1A:2B:FF:FE:3C:4D:5E. Privacy concern: this embeds MAC in IP, enabling tracking — hence RFC4941 temporary addresses.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What IPv6 address type replaced IPv4 broadcast?
questions.explanation: IPv6 eliminates broadcast entirely. Instead: FF02::1 = all-nodes multicast (like a broadcast to all IPv6 devices). FF02::2 = all-routers multicast. FF02::5 = OSPF routers. FF02::6 = OSPF DRs. Multicast is more efficient — only subscribed devices process it, reducing CPU load on non-interested hosts.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: What would Building 1's /64 subnet be using the 4th hextet for the subnet ID?
questions.explanation: From /48 prefix 2001:db8:cafe::/48, the 4th hextet is the subnet field (bits 49-64). Building 1 = 2001:db8:cafe:0001::/64 (or :1::/64 compressed). Building 2 = :2::/64, etc. Each building gets 2^64 host addresses — effectively unlimited. The interface ID (last 64 bits) is all zeros for the network address.
questions.source_ref: CCNA Field Ops Trainer v2.1; Jeremy's IT Lab CCNA 200-301

questions.question: Which statement correctly compares traditional networks and controller-based networks?
options: A. Only traditional networks offer a centralized control plane
B. Only traditional networks natively support centralized management
C. Traditional and controller-based networks abstract policies from device configurations
D. Only controller-based networks decouple the control plane and the data plane
questions.explanation: Most traditional devices use a distributed architecture, in which each control plane is resided in a networking device. Therefore they need to communicate with each other via messages to work correctly. In contrast to distributed architecture, centralized (or controller-based) architectures centralizes the control of networking devices into one device, called SDN controller -> Answer D is correct.

questions.question: How does HSRP provide first hop redundancy?
options: A. It load-balances traffic by assigning the same metric value to more than one route to the same destination in the IP routing table.
B. It load-balances Layer 2 traffic along the path by flooding traffic out all interfaces configured with the same VLAN.
C. It forwards multiple packets to the same destination over different routed links in the data path
D. It uses a shared virtual MAC and a virtual IP address to a group of routers that serve as the default gateway for hosts on a LAN
questions.explanation: This virtual IP address is in the same subnet as the interface IP address, but it is a different IP address. The router then automatically creates the virtual MAC address. All the cooperating HSRP routers know these virtual addresses, but only the HSRP active router uses these addresses at any one point in time. The virtual router is responsible for host communications such as an ARP request for the host’s default gateway. Technically, this is served by the active router since it is hosting the virtual router. However, it is the virtual router’s IP address and MAC address that are used for outgoing packets.

questions.question: Which 802.11 frame type is association response?
questions.explanation: There are three main types of 802.11 frames: the Data Frame, the Management Frame and the Control Frame. Association Response belongs to Management Frame. Association response is sent in response to an association request.

questions.question: Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 4 Which API is used in controller-based architectures to interact with edge devices?
questions.explanation: overlay: the virtual network underlay: the physical network nothbound: interacts with the server

questions.question: Which statement identifies the functionality of virtual machines?
options: A. Virtualized servers run most efficiently when they are physically connected to a switch that is separate from the hypervisor
B. The hypervisor can virtualize physical components including CPU, memory, and storage
C. Each hypervisor can support a single virtual machine and a single software switch
D. The hypervisor communicates on Layer 3 without the need for additional resources
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: Which type of address is the public IP address of a NAT device?
options: E. outside public F. inside public
questions.explanation: NAT use four types of addresses: * Inside local address - The IP address assigned to a host on the inside network. The address is usually not an IP address assigned by the Internet Network Information Center (InterNIC) or service provider. This address is likely to be an RFC 1918 private address. * Inside global address - A legitimate IP address assigned by the InterNIC or service provider that represents one or more inside local IP addresses to the outside world. * Outside local address - The IP address of an outside host as it is known to the hosts on the inside network. * Outside global address - The IP address assigned to a host on the outside network. The owner of the host assigns this address.

questions.question: Which option about JSON is true? Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 5
options: A. uses predefined tags or angle brackets () to delimit markup text
B. used to describe structured data that includes arrays
D. similar to HTML, it is more verbose than XML
questions.explanation: JSON data is written as name/value pairs. A name/value pair consists of a field name (in double quotes), followed by a colon, followed by a value: "name":"Mark" JSON can use arrays. Array values must be of type string, number, object, array, boolean or null.. For example: { "name":"John", "age":30, "cars":[ "Ford", "BMW", "Fiat" ] }

questions.question: Which attribute does a router use to select the best path when two or more different routes to the same destination exist from two different routing protocols?
questions.explanation: Administrative distance is the feature used by routers to select the best path when there are two or more different routes to the same destination from different routing protocols. Administrative distance defines the reliability of a routing protocol.

questions.question: Which command prevents passwords from being stored in the configuration as plaintext on a router or switch?
questions.explanation: enable password <string> - Sets the enable password, and stores that password in plaintext in the config. enable secret <string> - Sets the enable password, and stores that password as an md5 hash in the config. service password-encryption - For any passwords in the config that are stored in plaintext, this command changes them to be stored as hashed values instead.

questions.question: What is a benefit of using a Cisco Wireless LAN Controller?
options: A. Central AP management requires more complex configurations
B. Unique SSIDs cannot use the same authentication method
C. It supports autonomous and lightweight APs
D. It eliminates the need to configure each access point individually
questions.explanation: 15 A wireless LAN (or WLAN) controller is used in combination with the Lightweight Access Point Protocol (LWAPP) to "manage light-weight access points in large quantities" by the network administrator or network operations center.

questions.question: Which network allows devices to communicate without the need to access the Internet?
questions.explanation: This question asks about the private ranges of IPv4 addresses. The private ranges of each class of IPv4 are listed below: Class A private IP address ranges from 10.0.0.0 to 10.255.255.255 Class B private IP address ranges from 172.16.0.0 to 172.31.255.255 Class C private IP address ranges from 192.168.0.0 to 192.168.255.255 Only the network 172.28.0.0/16 belongs to the private IP address (of class B).

questions.question: Which result occurs when PortFast is enabled on an interface that is connected to another switch?
options: A. Spanning tree may fail to detect a switching loop in the network that causes broadcast storms
B. VTP is allowed to propagate VLAN configuration information from switch to switch automatically.
C. Root port choice and spanning tree recalculation are accelerated when a switch link goes down
D. After spanning tree converges PortFast shuts down any port that receives BPDUs.
questions.explanation: Enabling the PortFast feature causes a switch or a trunk port to enter the STP forwarding-state immediately or upon a linkup event, thus bypassing the listening and learning states. Note: To enable portfast on a trunk port you need the trunk keyword "spanning-tree portfast trunk"

questions.question: Two switches are connected and using Cisco Dynamic Trunking Protocol SW1 is set to Dynamic Desirable What is the result of this configuration?
options: A. The link is in a downstate.
B. The link is in an error disables state
C. The link is becomes an access port.
D. The link becomes a trunkport.
questions.explanation: Dynamic Auto - Makes the Ethernet port willing to convert the link to a trunk link. The port becomes a trunk port if the neighboring port is set to trunk or dynamic desirable mode. This is the default mode for some switchports. Dynamic Desirable - Makes the port actively attempt to convert the link to a trunk link. The port becomes a trunk port if the neighboring Ethernet port is set to trunk, dynamic desirable or dynamic auto mode.

questions.question: Which MAC address is recognized as a VRRP virtual address?
questions.explanation: With VRRP, the virtual router's MAC address is 0000.5E00.01xx , in which xx is the VRRP group.

questions.question: Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 17 in Which way does a spine and-leaf architecture allow for scalability in a network when additional access ports are required?
options: A. A spine switch and a leaf switch can be added with redundant connections between them
B. A spine switch can be added with at least 40 GB uplinks
C. A leaf switch can be added with a single connection to a core spine switch.
D. A leaf switch can be added with connections to every spine switch
questions.explanation: Spine-leaf architecture is typically deployed as two layers: spines (such as an aggregation layer), and leaves (such as an access layer). Spine-leaf topologies provide high-bandwidth, low-latency, nonblocking server-to-server connectivity. Leaf (aggregation) switches are what provide devices access to the fabric (the network of spine and leaf switches) and are typically deployed at the top of the rack. Generally, devices connect to the leaf switches. Devices can include servers, Layer 4-7 services (firewalls and load balancers), and WAN or Internet routers. Leaf switches do not connect to other leaf switches. In spine-and-leaf architecture, every leaf should connect to every spine in a full mesh.

questions.question: Which type of wireless encryption is used for WPA2 in pre-shared key mode?
questions.explanation: We can see in this picture we have to type 64 hexadecimal characters (256 bit) for the WPA2 passphrase so we can deduce the encryption is AES-256, not AES-128. 18

questions.question: What makes Cisco DNA Center different from traditional network management applications and their management of networks?
options: A. It only supports auto-discovery of network elements in a green field deployment.
B. It modular design allows someone to implement different versions to meet the specific needs of an organization
C. It abstracts policy from the actual device configuration
D. It does not support high availability of management functions when operating in cluster mode
questions.explanation: Automation: Using controllers and open APIs, Cisco DNA simplifies network management through abstraction and centralized policy enforcement that allows IT to focus on business intent and consistently apply configurations to improve service and keep operations consistently secure from the core to the edge.

questions.question: A network engineer must back up 20 network router configurations globally within a customer environment. Which protocol allows the engineer to perform this function using the Cisco IOS MIB?
questions.explanation: SNMP is an application-layer protocol that provides a message format for communication between SNMP managers and agents. SNMP provides a standardized framework and a common language used for the monitoring and management of devices in a network. The SNMP framework has three parts: + An SNMP manager + An SNMP agent + A Management Information Base (MIB) The Management Information Base (MIB) is a virtual information storage area for network management information, which consists of collections of managed objects. With SNMP, the network administrator can send commands to multiple routers to do the backup. 20

questions.question: Which action is taken by a switch port enabled for PoE power classification override? Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 21
options: A. When a powered device begins drawing power from a PoE switch port a syslog message is generated
B. As power usage on a PoE switch port is checked data flow to the connected device is temporarily paused
C. If a switch determines that a device is using less than the minimum configured power it assumes the device has failed and disconnects
D. If a monitored port exceeds the maximum administrative value for power, the port is shutdown and err-disabled
questions.explanation: PoE monitoring and policing compares the power consumption on ports with the administrative maximum value (either a configured maximum value or the port's default value). If the power consumption on a monitored port exceeds the administrative maximum value, the following actions occur: - A syslog message is issued. - The monitored port is shut down and error-disabled. - The allocated power is freed.

questions.question: Which mode must be used to configure EtherChannel between two switches without using a negotiation protocol?
questions.explanation: The Static Persistence (or "on" mode) bundles the links unconditionally and no negotiation protocol is used. In this mode, neither PAgP nor LACP packets are sent or received.

questions.question: An engineer configured an OSPF neighbor as a designated router. Which state verifies the designated router is in the proper mode?
questions.explanation: Full is the state for adjacent routers that have fully synchronised databases.

questions.question: Which configuration is needed to generate an RSA key for SSH on a router?
options: A. Configure the version of SSH
C. Create a user with a password.
D. Assign a DNS domain name
questions.explanation: In order to generate an RSA key for SSH, we need to configure the hostname and a DNS domain 23 name on the router (a username and password is also required). Therefore in fact both answer C and answer D are correct.

questions.question: An organization has decided to start using cloud-provided services. Which cloud service allows the organization to install its own operating system on a virtual machine?
questions.explanation: Below are the 3 cloud supporting services cloud providers provide to customer: + SaaS (Software as a Service): SaaS uses the web to deliver applications that are managed by a third- party vendor and whose interface is accessed on the clients' side. Most SaaS applications can be run directly from a web browser without any downloads or installations required, although some require plugins. + PaaS (Platform as a Service): are used for applications, and other development, while providing cloud components to software. What developers gain with PaaS is a framework they can build upon to develop or customize applications. PaaS makes the development, testing, and deployment of applications quick, simple, and cost-effective. With this technology, enterprise operations, or a third-party provider, can manage OSes, virtualization, servers, storage, networking, and the PaaS software itself. Developers, however, manage the applications. + IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service): self-service models for accessing, monitoring, and managing remote datacenter infrastructures, such as compute (virtualized or bare metal), storage, networking, and networking services (e.g. firewalls). Instead of having to purchase hardware outright, users can purchase IaaS based on consumption, similar to electricity or other utility billing. In general, IaaS provides hardware so that an organization can install their own operating system.

questions.question: Which design element is a best practice when deploying an 802.11b wireless infrastructure?
options: A. disabling TPC so that access points can negotiate signal levels with their attached wireless devices.
B. setting the maximum data rate to 54 Mbps on the Cisco Wireless LAN Controller
C. allocating non overlapping channels to access points that are in close physical proximity to one another
D. configuring access points to provide clients with a maximum of 5 Mbps
questions.explanation: Selecting the proper WiFi channel can significantly improve your WiFi coverage and performance. In the 2.4 GHz band, 1, 6, and 11 are the only non-overlapping channels. Selecting one or more of these channels is an important part of setting up your network correctly.

questions.question: A router running EIGRP has learned the same route from two different paths. Which parameter does the router use to select the best path?
questions.explanation: If a router learns two different paths for the same network from the same routing protocol, it has to decide which route is better and will be placed in the routing table. Metric is the measure used to decide which route is better (lower number is better). Each routing protocol uses its own metric. For example, RIP uses hop counts as a metric, while OSPF uses cost.

questions.question: What is the primary different between AAA authentication and authorization?
options: A. Authentication verifies a username and password, and authorization handles the communication between the authentication agent and the user database.
B. Authentication identifies a user who is attempting to access a system, and authorization validates the users password
C. Authentication identifies and verifies a user who is attempting to access a system, and authorization controls the tasks the user can perform.

D. Authentication controls the system processes a user can access and authorization logs the activities the user initiates Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 30
questions.explanation: AAA stands for Authentication, Authorization and Accounting. + Authentication: Specify who you are (usually via login username & password) + Authorization: Specify what actions you can do, what resource you can access + Accounting: Monitor what you do, how long you do it (can be used for billing and auditing) An example of AAA is shown below: + Authentication: "I am a normal user. My username/password is user_tom/learnforever" + Authorization: "user_tom can access LearnCCNA server via HTTP and FTP" + Accounting: "user_tom accessed LearnCCNA server for 2 hours". This user only uses "show" commands.

questions.question: When a floating static route is configured, which action ensures that the backup route is used when the primary route fails?
options: A. The floating static route must have a higher administrative distance than the primary route so it is used as a backup
B. The administrative distance must be higher on the primary route so that the backup route becomes secondary.
C. The floating static route must have a lower administrative distance than the primary route so it is used as a backup
D. The default-information originate command must be configured for the route to be installed into the routing table
questions.explanation: By default, IOS considers static routes better than OSPF-learned routes. By default, IOS gives static routes an administrative distance of 1 A floating static route floats or moves into and out of the IP routing table depending on whether the better (lower) administrative distance route learned by the routing protocol happens to exist currently.

questions.question: How do TCP and UDP differ in the way that they establish a connection between two endpoints?
options: A. TCP uses synchronization packets, and UDP uses acknowledgment packets.
B. UDP uses SYN,SYN ACK and FIN bits in the frame header while TCP uses SYN,SYN ACK and ACK bits
C. UDP provides reliable message transfer and TCP is a connectionless protocol
D. TCP uses the three-way handshake and UDP does not guarantee message delivery
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: When a site-to-site VPN is used, which protocol is responsible for the transport of user data?
questions.explanation: 32 A site-to-site VPN allows offices in multiple fixed locations to establish secure connections with each other over a public network such as the Internet. A site-to-site VPN means that two sites create a VPN tunnel by encrypting and sending data between two devices. One set of rules for creating a site-to-site VPN is defined by IPsec.

questions.question: What is the primary effect of the spanning-tree port fast command?
options: B. It minimizes spanning-tree convergence time
C. It immediately puts the port into the forwarding state when the switch is reloaded
D. It immediately enables the port in the listening state
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: How will Link Aggregation be implemented on a Cisco Wireless LAN Controller?
options: A. To pass client traffic two or more ports must be configured.
B. The EtherChannel must be configured in "mode active"
C. When enabled the WLC bandwidth drops to 500 Mbps
D. One functional physical port is needed to pass client traffic
questions.explanation: Link aggregation (LAG) is a partial implementation of the 802.3ad port aggregation standard. It bundles all of the controller's distribution system ports into a single 802.3ad port channel. Restriction for Link aggregation: - LAG requires the EtherChannel to be configured for `mode on' on both the controller and the Catalyst switch -> Answer B is not correct. - If the recommended load-balancing method cannot be configured on the Catalyst switch, then configure the LAG connection as a single member link or disable LAG on the controller -> Answer A is not correct while answer D is correct.

questions.question: Which set of action satisfy the requirement for multi-factor authentication?
options: A. The user swipes a key fob, then clicks through an email link
B. The user enters a user name and password, and then clicks a notification in an authentication app on a mobile device
C. The user enters a PIN into an RSA token, and then enters the displayed RSA key on a login screen
D. The user enters a user name and password and then re-enters the credentials on a second screen
questions.explanation: This is an example of how two-factor authentication (2FA) works: 1. The user logs in to the website or service with their username and password. 2. The password is validated by an authentication server and, if correct, the user becomes eligible for the second factor. 3. The authentication server sends a unique code to the user's second-factor method (such as a smartphone app). 4. The user confirms their identity by providing the additional authentication for their second-factor method.

questions.question: Which mode allows access points to be managed by Cisco Wireless LAN Controllers?
options: B. lightweight Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 35
questions.explanation: A Lightweight Access Point (LAP) is an AP that is designed to be connected to a wireless LAN (WLAN) controller (WLC). APs are "lightweight," which means that they cannot act independently of a wireless LAN controller (WLC). The WLC manages the AP configurations and firmware. The APs are "zero touch" deployed, and individual configuration of APs is not necessary.

questions.question: Router A learns the same route from two different neighbors, one of the neighbor routers is an OSPF neighbor and the other is an EIGRP neighbor. What is the administrative distance of the route that will be installed in the routing table?
questions.explanation: The Administrative distance (AD) of EIGRP is 90 while the AD of OSPF is 110 so EIGRP route will be chosen to install into the routing table.

questions.question: Which command must you enter to guarantee that an HSRP router with higher priority becomes the HSRP primary router after it is reloaded?
questions.explanation: 39 The "preempt" command enables the HSRP router with the highest priority to immediately become the active router.

questions.question: Which command should you enter to configure a device as an NTP sever?
options: C. ntp authenticate Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 40
questions.explanation: To configure a Cisco device as an Authoritative NTP Server, use the ntp master [stratum] command. To configure a Cisco device as a NTP client, use the command ntp server <IP address>. For example: Router(config)#ntp server 192.168.1.1. This command will instruct the router to query 192.168.1.1 for the time.

options: A. It enables AAA services on the device
B. It configures the device to connect to a RADIUS server for AAA
C. It associates a RADIUS server to an group.
D. It configures a local user on the device.
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: What is the destination MAC address of a broadcast frame?
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: Which command is used to enable LLDP globally on a Cisco IOS ISR?
options: E. cdp enable Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 42
questions.explanation: Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) is a industry standard protocol that allows devices to advertise, and discover connected devices, and there capabilities (same as CDP of Cisco). To enable it on Cisco devices, we have to use this command under global configuration mode: Sw(config)# lldp run

questions.question: You have configured a router with an OSPF router ID, but its IP address still reflects the physical interface. Which action can you take to correct the problem in the least disruptive way?
questions.explanation: Once an OSPF Router ID selection is done, it remains there even if you remove it or configure another OSPF Router ID. So the least disruptive way is to correct it using the command “clear ip ospf process”.

questions.question: Which command should you enter to configure an LLDP delay time of 5 seconds?
questions.explanation: + lldp holdtime seconds: Specify the amount of time a receiving device should hold the information from your device before discarding it + lldp reinit delay: Specify the delay time in seconds for LLDP to initialize on an interface + lldp timer rate: Set the sending frequency of LLDP updates in seconds Reference:

questions.question: Which keyword in a NAT configuration enables the use of one outside IP address for multiple inside hosts?
questions.explanation: By adding the keyword "overload" at the end of a NAT statement, NAT becomes PAT (Port Address Translation). This is also a kind of dynamic NAT that maps multiple private IP addresses to a single public IP address (many-to-one) by using different ports. Static NAT and Dynamic NAT 44 both require a one-to-one mapping from the inside local to the inside global address. By using PAT, you can have thousands of users connect to the Internet using only one real global IP address. PAT is the technology that helps us not run out of public IP address on the Internet. This is the most popular type of NAT. An example of using "overload" keyword is shown below: R1(config)# ip nat inside source list 1 interface ethernet1 overload

questions.question: Which unified access point mode continues to serve wireless clients after losing connectivity to the Cisco Wireless LAN Controller?
questions.explanation: In previous releases, whenever a FlexConnect access point disassociates from a controller, it moves to the standalone mode. The clients that are centrally switched are disassociated. However, the FlexConnect access point continues to serve locally switched clients. When the FlexConnect access point rejoins the controller (or a standby controller), all clients are disconnected and are authenticated again. This functionality has been enhanced and the connection between the clients and the FlexConnect access points are maintained intact and the clients experience seamless connectivity. When both the access point and the controller have the same configuration, the connection between the clients and APs is maintained.

questions.question: Which QoS Profile is selected in the GUI when configuring a voice over WLAN deployment?
questions.explanation: Cisco Unified Wireless Network solution WLANs support four levels of QoS: Platinum/Voice, Gold/Video, Silver/Best Effort (default), and Bronze/Background.

questions.question: When OSPF learns multiple paths to a network, how does it select a route?
options: A. It multiple the active K value by 256 to calculate the route with the lowest metric.
B. For each existing interface, it adds the metric from the source router to the destination to calculate the route with the lowest bandwidth.
C. It divides a reference bandwidth of 100 Mbps by the actual bandwidth of the existing interface to calculate the router with the lowest cost.
D. It count the umber of hops between the source router and the destination to determine the router with the lowest metric
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: Which command enables a router to become a DHCP client?
questions.explanation: If we want to get an IP address from the DHCP server on a Cisco device, we can use the command "ip address dhcp". Note: The command "ip helper-address" enables a router to become a DHCP Relay Agent.

questions.question: In a CDP environment, what happens when the CDP interface on an adjacent device is configured without an IP address?
options: A. CDP becomes inoperable on that neighbor
B. CDP uses the IP address of another interface for that neighbor
C. CDP operates normally,but it cannot provide IP address information for that neighbor
D. CDP operates normally,but it cannot provide any information for that neighbor
questions.explanation: Although CDP is a Layer 2 protocol but we can check the neighbor IP address with the "show cdp neighbor detail" command. If the neighbor does not has an IP address then CDP still operates without any problem. But the IP address of that neighbor is not provided. 48

questions.question: Which feature or protocol determines whether the QOS on the network is sufficient to support IP services?
questions.explanation: IP SLA allows an IT professional to collect information about network performance in real time. Therefore it helps determine whether the QoS on the network is sufficient for IP services or not. Cisco IOS Embedded Event Manager (EEM) is a powerful and flexible subsystem that provides real-time network event detection and onboard automation. It gives you the ability to adapt the behavior of your network devices to align with your business needs.

questions.question: An email user has been lured into clicking a link in an email sent by their company's security organization. The webpage that opens reports that it was safe but the link could have contained Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 49 malicious code. Which type of security program is in place?
questions.explanation: This is a training program which simulates an attack, not a real attack (as it says "The webpage that opens reports that it was safe") so we believed it should be called a "user awareness" program. Therefore the best answer here should be "user awareness". This is the definition of "User awareness" from CCNA 200- 301 Offical Cert Guide Book: "User awareness: All users should be made aware of the need for data confidentiality to protect corporate information, as well as their own credentials and personal information. They should also be made aware of potential threats, schemes to mislead, and proper procedures to report security incidents. " Note: Physical access control means infrastructure locations, such as network closets and data centers, should remain securely locked.

questions.question: What is the default behavior of a Layer 2 switch when a frame with an unknown destination MAC address is received?
options: A. The Layer 2 switch drops the received frame
B. The Layer 2 switch floods packets to all ports except the receiving port in the given VLAN.
C. The Layer 2 switch sends a copy of a packet to CPU for destination MAC address learning.
D. The Layer 2 switch forwards the packet and adds the destination MAC address to its MAC address table
questions.explanation: If the destination MAC address is not in the CAM table (unknown destination MAC address), the switch sends the frame out all other ports that are in the same VLAN as the received frame. This is called flooding. It does not flood the frame out the same port on which the frame was received.

questions.question: A user configured OSPF and advertised the Gigabit Ethernet interface in OSPF By default, which type of OSPF network does this interface belong to?
options: D. nonbroadcast Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 51
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: An engineer must configure a WLAN using the strongest encryption type for WPA2-PSK. Which cipher fulfills the configuration requirement?
questions.explanation: Many routers provide WPA2-PSK (TKIP), WPA2-PSK (AES), and WPA2-PSK (TKIP/AES) as options. TKIP is actually an older encryption protocol introduced with WPA to replace the very-insecure WEP encryption at the time. TKIP is actually quite similar to WEP encryption. TKIP is no longer considered secure, and is now deprecated. In other words, you shouldn't be using it. AES is a more secure encryption protocol introduced with WPA2 and it is currently the strongest encryption type for WPA2-PSK/

questions.question: When configuring an EtherChannel bundle, which mode enables LACP only if a LACP device is detected?
options: D. Auto Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 53
questions.explanation: The LACP is Link Aggregation Control Protocol. LACP is an open protocol, published under the 802.3ad. The modes of LACP are active, passive or on. The side configured as “pasive” will waiting the other side that should an Active for the Etherchannel to be established. PAgP is Port-Aggregation Protocol. It is Cisco proprietary protocol. The mode are On, Desirable or Auto. Desirable – Auto will establish a EtherChannel. An example of how to configure an Etherchannel: SwitchFormula1>enable SwitchFormula1#configure terminal SwitchFormula1(config)# interface range f0/5 -14 SwitchFormula1(config-if-range)# channel-group 13 mode ? active Enable LACP unconditionally auto Enable PAgP only if a PAgP device is detected desirable Enable PAgP unconditionally on Enable Etherchannel only passive Enable LACP only if a LACP device is detected

questions.question: Which command should you enter to view the error log in an EIGRP for IPv6 environment?
questions.explanation: show ip eigrp events

questions.question: If a notice-level messaging is sent to a syslog server, which event has occurred?
options: A. A network device has restarted
B. An ARP inspection has failed
C. A routing instance has flapped
D. A debug operation is running
questions.explanation: Router flapping would be level 3 (as it means that interface(s) are going up down multiple times in very short period).

questions.question: Which feature on the Cisco Wireless LAN Controller when enabled restricts management access from specific networks?
questions.explanation: Whenever you want to control which devices can talk to the main CPU, a CPU ACL is used. Note: CPU ACLs only filter traffic towards the CPU, and not any traffic exiting or generated by the CPU.

questions.question: Which command automatically generates an IPv6 address from a specified IPv6 prefix and MAC address of an interface?
questions.explanation: The "ipv6 address autoconfig" command causes the device to perform IPv6 stateless address auto-configuration to discover prefixes on the link and then to add the EUI-64 based addresses to the interface. Addresses are configured depending on the prefixes received in Router Advertisement (RA) messages. The device will listen for RA messages which are transmitted periodically from the router (DHCP Server). This RA message allows a host to create a global IPv6 address from: + Its interface identifier (EUI-64 address) + Link Prefix (obtained via RA) Note: Global address is the combination of Link Prefix and EUI-64 address

questions.question: Which command is used to specify the delay time in seconds for LLDP to initialize on any interface?
questions.explanation: + lldp holdtime seconds: Specify the amount of time a receiving device should hold the information from your device before discarding it + lldp reinit delay: Specify the delay time in seconds for LLDP to initialize on an interface + lldp timer rate: Set the sending frequency of LLDP updates in seconds

questions.question: A Cisco IP phone receive untagged data traffic from an attached PC. Which action is taken by the phone?
options: A. It allows the traffic to pass through unchanged
C. It tags the traffic with the default VLAN
D. It tags the traffic with the native VLAN
questions.explanation: Untagged traffic from the device attached to the Cisco IP Phone passes through the phone unchanged, regardless of the trust state of the access port on the phone.

questions.question: R1 has learned route 192.168.12.0/24 via IS-IS, OSPF, RIP and Internal EIGRP Under normal operating conditions, which routing protocol is installed in the routing table?
questions.explanation: With the same route (prefix), the router will choose the routing protocol with lowest Administrative Distance (AD) to install into the routing table. The AD of Internal EIGRP (90) is lowest so it would 62 be chosen. The table below lists the ADs of popular routing protocols. Note: The AD of IS-IS is 115. The "EIGRP" in the table above is "Internal EIGRP". The AD of "External EIGRP" is 170. An EIGRP external route is a route that was redistributed into EIGRP.

questions.question: Which IPv6 address block sends packets to a group address rather than a single address?
questions.explanation: FF00::/8 is used for IPv6 multicast and this is the IPv6 type of address the question wants to ask. FE80::/10 range is used for link-local addresses. Link-local addresses only used for communications within the local subnetwork (automatic address configuration, neighbor discovery, router discovery, and by many routing protocols). It is only valid on the current subnet. It is usually created dynamically using a link-local prefix of FE80::/10 and a 64-bit interface identifier (based on 48-bit MAC address).

questions.question: Which feature or protocol is required for an IP SLA to measure UDP jitter?
questions.explanation: Time synchronization, such as that provided by the Network Time Protocol (NTP), is required between the source and the target device to provide accurate one-way delay (latency) measurements.

questions.question: Which NAT term is defined as a group of addresses available for NAT use?

questions.question: Which component of an Ethernet frame is used to notify a host that traffic is coming?
questions.explanation: Preamble is a 7 Byte field in the Ethernet frame which helps to receiver to know that it is an actual data ( Ethernet Frame) and not some random noise in the transmission medium. It acts like a doorbell telling about the incoming data.

questions.question: You are configuring your edge routers interface with a public IP address for Internet connectivity. The router needs to obtain the IP address from the service provider dynamically. Which command is needed on interface FastEthernet 0/0 to accomplish this?
questions.explanation: See official Cisco CCNA documentation.

questions.question: Which IPv6 address type provides communication between subnets and cannot route on the Internet?
questions.explanation: A IPv6 Unique Local Address is an IPv6 address in the block FC00::/7. It is the approximate IPv6 counterpart of the IPv4 private address. It is not routable on the global Internet. Note: In the past, Site-local addresses (FEC0::/10) are equivalent to private IP addresses in IPv4 but now they are deprecated. Link-local addresses only used for communications within the local subnet. It is usually created dynamically using a link-local prefix of FE80::/10 and a 64-bit interface identifier (based on 48-bit MAC address).

questions.question: A user configured OSPF in a single area between two routers A serial interface connecting R1 and R2 is running encapsulation PPP. By default which OSPF network type is seen on this interface when the user types show ip ospf interface on R1 or R2?
questions.explanation: The default OSPF network type for HDLC and PPP on Serial link is point-to-point (while the default OSPF network type for Ethernet link is Broadcast).

questions.question: An engineer must configure a /30 subnet between two routers. Which usable IP address and subnet mask combination meets this criteria?

C. interface e0/0 description to HQ-A371:10975 ip address 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.248
D. interface e0/0 description to HQ-A371:10975 ip address 209.165.201.2 255.255.255.252
questions.explanation: A /30 subnet means subnet mask of 255.255.255.252. But 10.2.1.3 255.255.255.252 is a broadcast IP address; only 209.165.201.2/30 is the usable IP address.

questions.explanation: TTL is a Layer 3 mechanism that routers decrement after a successful hop.

questions.explanation: A IPv6 Unique Local Address is an IPv6 address in the block FC00::/7, which means that IPv6 Unique Local addresses begin with 7 bits with exact binary pattern as 1111 110 -> Answer B is correct. Note: IPv6 Unique Local Address is the approximate IPv6 counterpart of the IPv4 private address. It is not routable on the global Internet.

questions.question: Which type does a port become when it receives the best BPDU on a bridge?
questions.explanation: The port that receives the best BPDU on a bridge is the root port.

questions.question: Which value can you modify to configure a specific interface as the preferred forwarding interface?
questions.explanation: This example shows how to increase the probability that the spanning tree instance on access port interface 2/0 is chosen as the root bridge by changing the port priority to 32: switch(config-if)# spanning-tree port-priority 32

questions.question: Which statement about VLAN configuration is true?
options: A. The switch must be in VTP server or transparent mode before you can configure a VLAN
B. The switch must be in config-vlan mode before you configure an extended VLAN
C. Dynamic inter-VLAN routing is supported on VLAN2 through VLAN 4064
D. A switch in VTP transparent mode save the VLAN databases to the running configuration only
questions.explanation: You can only create, add, delete edit vlan in server and transparent mode, you won’t be able to create, delete vlan in client mode.

questions.question: Which value is used to determine the active router in an HSRP default configuration?
questions.explanation: Q. If there is no priority configured for a standby group, what determines which router is active? A. The priority field is used to elect the active router and the standby router for the specific group. In the case of an equal priority, the router with the highest IP address for the respective group is elected as active. Furthermore, if there are more than two routers in the group, the second highest IP address determines the standby router and the other router/routers are in the listen state.

questions.question: Which configuration command can you apply to a HSRP router so that its local interface becomes active if all other routers in the group fail?
options: A. no additional config is required
questions.explanation: Simply because that will be the default behavior routers would follow in the event all other routers in the HSRP group fail, then it would not keep attributes such as priority or preemption. What preemption does in summary is to make sure that the configured Priority on all routers within the same HSRP group is always respected. That is, if R1 is configured on the HSRP group with a priority of 150 but he stands as active since all other routers currently subscribed to that group have a priority 150, then will router will preempt the current active router and will take over hence becoming the new active router. With preemption disabled, the new router does not preempt the current active router, unless routers in the group have to renegotiate their roles based on each router’s priority at the time of negotiation.

questions.question: Which statement about Cisco Discovery Protocol is true?
options: A. It is a Cisco-proprietary protocol.
B. It runs on the network layer.
C. It can discover information from routers, firewalls, and switches.
D. It runs on the physical layer and the data link layer. Get Latest & Actual 200-301 Exam's Question and Answers from Passleader. http://www.passleader.com 85

questions.question: Which technique can you use to route IPv6 traffic over an IPv4 infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Overlay tunneling encapsulates IPv6 packets in IPv4 packets for delivery across an IPv4 infrastructure (a core network or the figure below). By using overlay tunnels, you can communicate with isolated IPv6 networks without upgrading the IPv4 infrastructure between 86 them. Overlay tunnels can be configured between border devices or between a border device and a host; however, both tunnel endpoints must support both the IPv4 and IPv6 protocol stacks. IPv6 supports the following types of overlay tunneling mechanisms: 1 Manual 2 Generic routing encapsulation (GRE) 3 IPv4-compatible 4 6to4 5 Intrasite Automatic Tunnel Addressing Protocol (ISATAP)
