questions.question: Which device enables hosts to communicate with hosts in other LANs?
questions.explanation: Router A router is a networking device that forwards data packets between computer net-works, enabling communication between hosts in different local area networks (LANs).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following device types typically has many physical ports for end hosts to connect to?
questions.explanation: Switch A switch is a device in a computer network that connects other devices together. It has multiple ports to which computers, printers, and other networking devices can be connected. A switch is used within a LAN to connect multiple devices and man-age their network traffic.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What type of software runs on an end host and filters traffic entering the host?
questions.explanation: Host-based firewall A host-based firewall is a software application installed on a single computer (the host) in a network. It monitors and filters incoming and outgoing network traffic based on a set of rules.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What kind of network is used to connect remote locations?
questions.explanation: WAN A wide area network (WAN) is a network that extends over a large geographical area, often connecting multiple smaller networks like LANs. WANs are used to connect remote or geographically dispersed locations.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which role describes a device that accesses services provided by another host?
questions.explanation: Client A client is a computer or a program that accesses services provided by another com-puter (the server). The client uses the network to send requests to the server and to receive data and services in return. This client-server model is a fundamental concept in networking.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the standard maximum cable length of a copper UTP cable?
questions.explanation: 100 meters The standard maximum length for copper unshielded twisted pair (UTP) cables in an Ethernet network is 100 meters (328 feet). Attempts to use UTP cables longer than 100 meters can result in signal attenuation and decreased performance.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which technology allows a device to automatically adjust which pin pairs it uses to transmit and receive data in a copper UTP cable?
questions.explanation: Auto MDI-X Automatic Medium-Dependent Interface Crossover (Auto MDI-X) is a technology that allows devices to automatically adjust their pin configurations without needing specific crossover or straight-through cables.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following cable types supports connections over the greatest distances?
questions.explanation: Single-mode fiber Single-mode fiber optic cables are designed for long-distance communication, often over many kilometers. They have a smaller core diameter than multimode fiber, allow-ing only one mode of light to travel along the cable. This reduces signal attenuation and allows data to travel longer distances.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: How many bits can a 1 Gbps interface transmit per second?
questions.explanation: 1,000,000,000 bps The prefix “giga” means “billion”, so a 1 Gbps (gigabit per second) interface can trans-mit 1,000,000,000 (one billion) bits per second.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following is equivalent to 1 Mbps?
questions.explanation: 1000 kbps The term Mbps stands for megabits per second, and kbps stands for kilobits per sec-ond. The prefix “mega” means “million” and the prefix “kilo” means “thousand”, so 1 Mbps is equivalent to 1000 kbps.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of fiber-optic cables over copper UTP cables?
questions.explanation: Lower cost The main advantages of fiber-optic cables over copper UTP cables include being less vulnerable to electromagnetic interference (EMI), supporting greater maximum transmission distances, and posing less security risk due to leaked signals. However, fiber-optic connections are generally more expensive due to both the cables and the small form-factor pluggable (SFP) transceivers.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the minimum copper UTP cable standard that supports 1 Gbps Ethernet?
questions.explanation: Cat 5e While the original Cat5 standard could theoretically support gigabit speeds, Cat5e is specifically designed to support 1 Gbps and is generally considered the minimum requirement for Gigabit Ethernet.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which layer of the TCP/IP Model defines standards related to the physical medium and how data should be encoded into signals sent over the medium?
questions.explanation: Layer 1 Layer 1, the Physical Layer, is responsible for the physical connections between devices and how to encode messages into signals (such as electrical or light signals) that are sent over those connections.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which layer of the TCP/IP Model is responsible for end-to-end delivery of a mes-sage from the source host to the destination host?
questions.explanation: Layer 3 Layer 3, the Network Layer, is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of messages across a network. It uses IP addressing to determine routes for data, ensuring that packets are delivered from the source host to the destination host.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which layer of the TCP/IP Model is responsible for delivering a message to the next node in the path to the final destination?
questions.explanation: Layer 2 Layer 2, the Data Link Layer, is responsible for delivering a message to the next node in the network path. It handles communication between adjacent network nodes and can include protocols like Ethernet or Wi-Fi, dealing with the MAC addresses of the devices.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which word describes a frame’s payload?
questions.explanation: Packet A frame is a Layer 2 Protocol Data Unit (L2PDU), and its payload (the message it encapsulates) is typically a packet. A packet is an L3PDU and contains the Layer 3 header (like an IP header) and its payload.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which layer provides a service to the Application Layer by delivering data to the appropriate application process on the destination host?
questions.explanation: Layer 4 Layer 4, the Transport Layer, provides services to the Application Layer by delivering data to the appropriate application process on the destination host. This is an example of adjacent-layer interaction—a layer servicing the layer above it.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Signals sent out of a physical port of one device are received by a physical port of another device. Which term describes this interaction?
questions.explanation: Same-layer interaction “Same-layer interaction” refers to the process where a layer in one device communi-cates with the same layer in another device. For example, when signals are sent from a physical port of one device and are received by a physical port of another device, it’s an instance of same-layer interaction, as each device’s Physical Layer is involved in the communication.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which layer of the TCP/IP Model provides an interface between a web browser like Google Chrome and the network?
questions.explanation: Application Layer The Application Layer provides an interface between application software, like a web browser, and the underlying network.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What kind of cable is typically used to connect to the console port of a Cisco router or switch?
questions.explanation: Rollover A rollover cable is typically used to connect a computer to the console port of a Cisco router or switch. This cable has a unique pinout where the wire connected to pin 1 on one end is connected to pin 8 on the other end, pin 2 to pin 7, pin 3 to pin 6, etc.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What mode in the IOS hierarchy is indicated by the prompt hostname>?
questions.explanation: User EXEC mode The prompt hostname> indicates that the device is in User EXEC mode. This mode provides limited access to the device and doesn’t support any configurations or disrup-tive commands.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which command can be used to view the device’s active configuration from global configuration mode?
questions.explanation: do show running-config The running-config is the device’s active configuration—the configuration that deter-mines the device’s current operation—and it can be viewed with show running-con-fig. To use show commands from global config mode, you must add do in front of the command.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Where is a device’s startup-config file stored?
questions.explanation: NVRAM The startup configuration file of a Cisco device is stored in Non-Volatile Random-Ac-cess Memory (NVRAM). Unlike standard RAM, NVRAM retains its contents even when the device is powered off.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following commands cannot be used to save a device’s configuration?
questions.explanation: copy startup-config running-config To save a device’s configuration, you must copy the contents of the running-config in RAM to the startup-config in NVRAM, but this command does the opposite. It copies the contents of the startup-config to the running-config.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following commands configures a password to limit access to privi-leged EXEC mode and stores the password as a secure hash?
questions.explanation: enable secret password The “enable secret” is a password for accessing privileged EXEC mode. The device stores the enable secret as a secure hash, providing better security than the “enable password”.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the effect of the service password-encryption command?
options: Passwords configured in the future will be encrypted.
questions.explanation: A and B The service password-encryption command has two effects: it encrypts cur-rently configured cleartext passwords and ensures that any passwords configured in the future are automatically encrypted. Note that this encryption is not secure, but it does provide basic protection against someone looking at the configuration over your shoulder.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the OUI of the BIA “0cf5.a452.b103”?
questions.explanation: 0cf5.a4 The Organizationally Unique Identifier (OUI) is the part of the burned-in address (BUA—another name for a MAC address) that uniquely identifies the manufacturer. It consists of the first 24 bits (the first half) of the MAC address.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the only field of the Ethernet trailer?
questions.explanation: FCS The only field in the Ethernet trailer is the Frame Check Sequence (FCS). The frame’s receiver uses the FCS to detect errors in the frame.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which field of an Ethernet frame does a switch use to dynamically learn MAC addresses?
questions.explanation: Source A switch dynamically learns MAC addresses by examining the source MAC address field of each frame it receives. It then adds the MAC address to its MAC address table, associating it with the switch port on which it received the frame.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following statements accurately describes ARP?
options: ARP maps a known Layer 3 address to an unknown Layer 2 address.
ARP maps a known Layer 3 address to a known Layer 2 address.
ARP maps an unknown Layer 3 address to an unknown Layer 2 address.
ARP maps an unknown Layer 3 address to an unknown Layer 2 address.
questions.explanation: ARP maps a known Layer 3 address to an unknown Layer 2 address. Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) is used to learn the unknown MAC address of another host by sending an ARP request to the host’s known IP address.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the destination MAC address of an unknown unicast frame?
options: The MAC address of the frame’s destination host
The MAC address of the frame’s sender
questions.explanation: The MAC address of the frame’s destination host An unknown unicast frame is addressed to a single intended recipient (not to the broadcast MAC address “ffff.ffff.ffff”). However, because the switch does not have the intended recipient’s MAC address in its table, the switch’s only option is to flood the frame.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which utility involves ICMP echo requests and responses?
questions.explanation: ping The ping utility involves sending Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) echo requests and waiting for echo responses. It is used to test the reachability of hosts on an IP network.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: In an ARP exchange, which of the following messages is unicast?
questions.explanation: ARP reply In an ARP exchange, the ARP request is broadcast to all devices on the local network, but the ARP reply is a unicast message sent back to the requester with the needed MAC address information.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What bit pattern is found at the beginning of an IPv4 packet?
questions.explanation: 0100 The first field of the IPv4 header is the four-bit Version field, which indicates the IP ver-sion of the packet. IP version 4 is indicated with 0b0100 (which is equal to 0d4).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is indicated by the Total Length field of the IPv4 header?
options: The length of the IPv4 header
The length of the Ethernet header and trailer
questions.explanation: The length of the Packet The Total Length field in the IPv4 header specifies the entire packet’s length, includ-ing both the header and the encapsulated payload, in bytes.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which field of the IPv4 header is used to prevent packets infinitely looping around the network?
questions.explanation: TTL The Time To Live (TTL) field in the IPv4 header is used to prevent packets from looping infinitely around the network. The TTL value is decremented by one by each router that forwards the packet. If the TTL reaches zero, the packet is discarded.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which netmask is equivalent to a /16 prefix length?
questions.explanation: 255.255.0.0 A /16 prefix length is indicated with a netmask consisting of 16 1s followed by 16 0s: 11111111.11111111.00000000.00000000, which is 255.255.0.0 in dotted decimal.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the result of a speed mismatch on two connected interfaces?
options: The interfaces will operate at the slower of the two speeds.
The interfaces will be in a down/down state.
Collisions and late collisions will occur.
questions.explanation: The interfaces will be in a down/down state. If there is a speed mismatch, the connection will not be operational; the devices will be unable to communicate. You can expect the connected interfaces to be in a down/ down state.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which type of route represents the exact IP address of a router’s interface with a /32 prefix length?
questions.explanation: L A local (L) route in the routing table represents the exact IP address of a router’s inter-face with a /32 prefix length. A local route is automatically created by the router for each of its active interfaces.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Given a /25 address block, what prefix length is appropriate to create 16 subnets of equal size?
questions.explanation: /29 To create 16 subnets, you need to borrow 4 bits from the host portion (since 24 = 16). This results in a /29 prefix length, adding 4 to the original /25 prefix length.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Given a /15 address block, what prefix length is appropriate to create 1024 sub-nets of equal size?
questions.explanation: /25 Making 1024 subnets requires 10 borrowed bits (since 210 = 1024). Therefore, the answer is /25, adding 10 to the original /15 prefix length.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: How many root ports will there be in a four-switch LAN?
questions.explanation: 3 In a network with four switches, there will be one root bridge and three non-root bridges. Each non-root bridge will select one root port—the port with the lowest cost to reach the root bridge. Therefore, there will be three root ports in total.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What happens when a switch port configured with spanning-tree bpduguard enable receives a BPDU?
options: BPDU Guard is disabled on the port.
The port immediately moves to the forwarding state.
questions.explanation: The port is error-disabled. When BPDU Guard is enabled on a port and it receives a BPDU, the port is put into an error-disabled state to prevent potential STP topology changes. This is a protective measure typically used on access ports that should be connected to end hosts.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: How many STP designated ports will there be in a four-switch LAN with a total of seven links?
questions.explanation: 7 In an STP network, every network segment (link) will have one designated port. With seven links, there will be seven designated ports.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Port-channel 1 and its member ports currently operate in access mode. What will happen after you configure switchport mode trunk on the port-channel 1 interface?
options: The port-channel is disabled until you configure trunk mode on the member ports.
The configuration is inherited by the member ports.
The member ports are disabled until you configure trunk mode on them.
questions.explanation: The configuration is inherited by the member ports. When you configure the port-channel interface, the member ports inherit this con-figuration. Changing the port-channel interface to trunk mode automatically updates the member ports to match this configuration, ensuring consistency across the EtherChannel.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: If a Cisco router learns multiple routes to the same destination via the same rout-ing protocol, and all routes have the same metric, what is the maximum number of routes to that destination that the router will insert into its routing table by default?
questions.explanation: 4 By default, Cisco routers can load-balance across up to four equal-cost paths; this is called equal-cost multi-path (ECMP) routing. If multiple routes to the same destina-tion have the same metric, up to four of them can be inserted into the routing table for load balancing.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: A router receives a packet destined for 10.23.107.126. It has the following four routes in its routing table. Which route will it select to forward the packet?
questions.explanation: D 10.23.107.112/28 [90/3456] via 172.16.1.13 In this case, three routes match the packet’s destination IP address: the route to 10.23.107.0/25 (A), the route to 10.23.107.96/27 (C), and the route to 10.23.107.112/28 (D). The route to 10.23.107.112/28 has the longest prefix length, so it is the most specific matching route; the router will forward the packet according to that route’s instructions.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: R1 has multiple OSPF neighbor relationships. R1’s OSPF RID is 192.168.1.1, which was inherited from a physical interface’s IP address. You then config-ure the IP address 10.0.0.2 on a loopback interface and configure router-id 1.1.1.1 in router config mode for the OSPF process. What is R1’s RID after you issue the router-id 1.1.1.1 command?
questions.explanation: 192.168.1.1 Once a router has determined its RID and established an OSPF neighbor relationship with another router, the only way to change the RID is to manually configure it with the router-id rid command and then reset the OSPF process with clear ip ospf process. However, the scenario does not indicate that you reset the OSPF process, so R1’s RID remains 192.168.1.1.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following commands activates OSPF on an interface without using the network command?
questions.explanation: R1(config-if)# ip ospf 1 area 0 You can use the ip ospf process-id area area-id in interface config mode to activate OSPF on the interface. This is generally preferred over the network command due to its simplicity.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Four routers connected to a segment with the OSPF broadcast network type have the default OSPF priority. R4 is the DR and R3 is the BDR. You configure ip ospf priority 100 on R2’s interface connected to the segment and reset OSPF on R4 with clear ip ospf process. Which of the following statements is true after you reset R4’s OSPF process?
options: R2 is the DR and R3 is the BDR.
R3 is the DR and R2 is the BDR.
R2 is the DR and R4 is the BDR.
R3 is the DR and R4 is the BDR
questions.explanation: R3 is the DR and R2 is the BDR. When the OSPF process of R4 (the DR) is reset, R3 (the BDR) immediately takes over its role as the DR, even though R2’s interface now has the highest priority (100, whereas others have the default of 1). Then, an election is held to determine the new BDR; R2 wins this due to its higher priority and becomes the BDR.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which IPv6 address is equivalent to an IPv4 address in the 127.0.0.0/8 range?
questions.explanation: ::1 This is the IPv6 loopback address. Whereas IPv4 reserves a whole range (127.0.0.0/8) for loopback addresses, IPv6 uses only ::1. Messages sent to this address are “looped back” to the local device; they are not sent out of any interface.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following commands configures a fully specified default route?
questions.explanation: ipv6 route ::/0 GigabitEthernet0/1 2001:db8::1 A default route is a route to ::/0, matching all possible IPv6 addresses. A fully specified route includes both the exit interface and the next-hop address, so option C is correct.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following extended ACL entries blocks requests from clients to an HTTPS server?
questions.explanation: deny tcp any host 192.0.2.1 eq 443 HTTPS uses TCP port 443. When clients send requests to an HTTPS server, they use destination port (not source port) 443. So, B is correct because it denies requests des-tined for port 443 on the server.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following extended ACL entries permits all messages from the server with IP address 203.0.113.1 except its responses to SSH clients?
questions.explanation: permit tcp 203.0.113.1 0.0.0.0 neq 22 any SSH uses TCP port 22. This time, we are filtering the server’s responses, so the ACE must filter based on source port 22, not destination port 22—the opposite of the pre-vious example. Use neq 22 (not equal to 22) to permit everything except responses to SSH clients (which would be sourced from port 22).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following extended ACL entries denies connections from clients to port 21 of an FTP server?
options: deny tcp any host 198.51.100.1 eq ftp-data
deny udp any host 198.51.100.1 eq ftp-data
deny tcp any host 198.51.100.1 eq ftp
deny udp any host 198.51.100.1 eq ftp
questions.explanation: deny tcp any host 198.51.100.1 eq ftp FTP uses TCP (not UDP) port 21 for control connections, and the equivalent keyword when configuring an ACE is ftp(ftp-data matches port 20).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following extended ACL entries permits Telnet connections from hosts in the 192.168.2.0/25 subnet to hosts in the 10.0.0.0/9 subnet?
questions.explanation: permit tcp 192.168.2.0 0.0.0.127 10.0.0.0 0.127.255.255 eq 23 Telnet uses TCP port 23. This ACE correctly specifies source subnet 192.168.2.0/25 (wildcard mask 0.0.0.127) and destination subnet 10.0.0.0/9 (wildcard mask 0.127.255.255).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which command can be used to create an extended named ACL?
questions.explanation: ip access-list extended TEST_ACL The correct command to create a standard named ACL is ip access-list extended name. This command brings you to extended named ACL configuration mode, where you can configure rules for the ACL.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the effect of the command ip access-list resequence 100 10 5?
options: Change the sequence number of ACL 100’s first ACE to 10 and add 5 for each subsequent ACE.
Change the sequence number of the ACL’s first ACE to 100, add 10 for the second ACE, and 5 for the third ACE.
Change the sequence numbers of ACL 100’s first 10 ACEs to use increments of 5.
Change the sequence number of ACL 5’s first ACE to 1000 and add 10 for each subsequent ACE.
questions.explanation: Change the sequence number of ACL 100’s first ACE to 10 and add 5 for each sub-sequent ACE. The syntax for the command is ip access-list resequence {name|number} starting-seq-num increment. The command specifies ACL 100, a starting sequence number of 10, and an increment of 5. index Numbers /8 address block 176
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol1 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: By default, how many LLDP messages can a device miss from its neighbor before removing the neighbor from its LLDP neighbor table?
questions.explanation: 4 The default hold time for LLDP is 120 seconds, and LLDP messages are sent every 30 seconds by default. Therefore, a device can miss up to 4 LLDP messages (120 seconds / 30 seconds per message) before it removes the neighbor from its table.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the default NTP stratum level of a Cisco router that has been configured with ntp master ?
questions.explanation: 8 When a Cisco router is configured with ntp master without specifying a stratum level, it defaults to stratum 8.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which concept refers to the variation in delay?
questions.explanation: Jitter Jitter refers to the variation in delay between packets, which can negatively affect user experience in real-time apps like voice and video calls.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which command guarantees power to a PoE-enabled device connected to the port?
questions.explanation: power inline static The power inline static command reserved a fixed amount of power for the specified port, ensuring the device connected to the port receives power.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which element of the CIA triad aims to protect systems and data from unauthorized access?
questions.explanation: C The “C” in the CIA triad stands for “Confidentiality”, which aims to protect systems and data from unauthorized access, ensuring that sensitive information is only accessible by authorized entities. The “I” stands for “Integrity”, and the “A” stands for “Availability”.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: After configuring switchport port-security on a port, how many MAC addresses can be learned on the port by default?
questions.explanation: 1 By default, when port security is enabled, a switch port is allowed to learn only one MAC address. If a frame from another MAC address arrives on the port, a Port Security violation is triggered.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which type of machine learning involves labeled data sets?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning Supervised learning is a type of machine learning (and deep learning) that involves labeled data sets. This is highly effective when clear and accurate labels are available, enabling precise predictions and classifications.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which of the following can be provided by predictive AI?
questions.explanation: Network traffic forecasting AI models can analyze network traffic to predict future network load, allowing for proactive provisioning of additional resources or QoS policy optimization.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which Catalyst Center AI feature can be used to automate troubleshooting?
questions.explanation: Machine reasoning Catalyst Center’s Machine reasoning (MR) engine automates network troubleshooting, performing a root cause analysis when issues arise and potentially resolving problems without requiring manual intervention.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using OAuth 2.0 for accessing resources?
options: OAuth 2.0 allows third-party applications to access resources without sharing the user’s credentials.
OAuth 2.0 is easier to implement than basic authentication.
questions.explanation: OAuth 2.0 allows third-party applications to access resources without sharing the user’s credentials. OAuth 2.0 provides a secure way to delegate access to resources by issuing tokens, thereby eliminating the need to share the user’s credentials with third-party applications. OAuth 2.0 is a key component of many modern web applications.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: In which data serialization language is whitespace significant?
questions.explanation: YAML In YAML (YAML Ain’t Markup Language), whitespace (specifically, indentation) is significant and is used to denote structure of the data. In XML (Extensible Markup Language) and JSON (JavaScript Object Notation), whitespace is not significant for the structure of the data.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which data serialization language uses HTML-like tags?
questions.explanation: XML XML uses HTML-like tags to indicate key-value pairs: <key>value</key> .
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What is the JSON name for an unordered set of key-value pairs?
questions.explanation: Object In JSON, an unordered set of key-value pairs is known as an “object”. It is enclosed in curly braces, with keys and values separated by colons.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which language are most Ansible files written in?
questions.explanation: YAML Most Ansible files, including inventory and playbook files, are written in YAML , which is popular for its readability and ease of use.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: Which Ansible file defines automation tasks to manage configuration on managed devices?
questions.explanation: Playbook In Ansible, playbooks are the primary files where automation tasks are defined. Playbooks are written in YAML and describe the configurations of managed devices.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Exam Vol2 (Jeremy McDowell)

questions.question: What are the three steps in the basic Terraform workflow?
questions.explanation: Write, plan, apply The basic Terraform workflow consists of three main steps. The first is write, in which you define the desired state of your infrastructure resources in configuration files using HCL. The second is plan, in which you verify the changes that will be executed. The third is apply, in which you execute the plan to provision and manage the infrastructure resources. index Numbers 2.4 GHz band 317 2FA (two-factor authentication) 186, 196 3G (third generation) questions.question: The process of HTTP asking TCP to send some data and making sure that it is received correctly is an example of what?
options: All of these answers are correct.

questions.question: The process of TCP on one computer marking a TCP segment as segment 1 and the receiving computer then acknowledging the receipt of TCP segment 1 is an example of what?
options: All of these answers are correct.

questions.question: The process of a web server adding a TCP header to the contents of a web page, followed by adding an IP header and then adding a data-link header and trailer, is an example of what?
options: All of these answers are correct.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following terms is used specifically to identify the entity created when encapsulating data inside data-link layer headers and trailers?
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Some end-user devices connect to a LAN using a cable while others use wireless. Which answer best characterizes which devices use Ethernet to connect to the LAN?
options: Only the end-user devices that use cables are using Ethernet.

Only the end-user devices that use wireless are using 
	Ethernet.
Both the end-user devices using cables and those using 	wireless are using Ethernet.
Neither the end-user devices using cables nor those using 	wireless are using Ethernet.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following Ethernet standards defines Gigabit Ethernet over UTP cabling?
options: None of the other answers are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about Ethernet crossover cables for Fast Ethernet?
options: Pins 1 and 2 are reversed on the other end of the cable.
Pins 1 and 2 on one end of the cable connect to pins 3 and 6 	on the other end of the cable.
Pins 1 and 2 on one end of the cable connect to pins 3 and 4 	on the other end of the cable.
The cable can be up to 1000 meters long to cross over 	between buildings.
None of the other answers are correct.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following is true about the CSMA/CD algorithm?
options: The algorithm never allows collisions to occur.
Collisions can happen, but the algorithm defines how the 	computers should notice a collision and how to recover.
The algorithm works with only two devices on the same 	Ethernet.
None of the other answers are correct.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following is true about the Ethernet FCS field?
options: Ethernet uses FCS for error recovery.
It resides in the Ethernet trailer, not the Ethernet header.

questions.question: Which of the following terms is not commonly used to describe a serial link?
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Imagine a network in which PC1 connects to the same Ethernet LAN as Router1, PC2 connects to the same LAN as Router2, and the two routers connect to each other with a PPP serial link. When PC1 sends data to PC2, and Router1 removes the Ethernet header and trailer, which of the following is true?
options: Router1 does not use the removed Ethernet header/trailer 	again.
Router1 re-encapsulates the packet in a PPP frame that uses 	the Ethernet addresses from the removed header.
Router1 also removes the IP header before forwarding the 	data to Router2.
Router1 re-encapsulates the packet in a new Ethernet frame 	before forwarding the packet to Router2.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following does a router normally use when making a decision about routing TCP/IP packets?
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following are true about a LAN-connected TCP/IP host and its IP routing (forwarding) choices?
options: The host always sends packets to its default gateway.
The host never sends packets to its default gateway.

The host sends packets to its default gateway if the 
	destination IP address is in a different subnet than the host.
The host sends packets to its default gateway if the 	destination IP address is in the same subnet as the host.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following is a difference between Telnet and SSH as supported by a Cisco switch?
options: SSH encrypts the passwords used at login, but not other 	traffic; Telnet encrypts nothing.
SSH encrypts all data exchange, including login passwords; 	Telnet encrypts nothing.
Telnet is used from Microsoft operating systems, and SSH is 	used from UNIX and Linux operating systems.
Telnet encrypts only password exchanges; SSH encrypts all 	data exchanges.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question:
What type of switch memory is used to store the 
configuration used by the switch when it is up and working?
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: What command copies the configuration from RAM into NVRAM?
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following statements describes part of the process of how a switch decides to forward a frame destined for a known unicast MAC address?
options: It compares the unicast destination address to the bridging, 	or MAC address, table.
It compares the unicast source address to the bridging, or 	MAC address, table.
It forwards the frame out all interfaces in the same VLAN 	except for the incoming interface.
It compares the destination IP address to the destination MAC 	address.
It compares the frame's incoming interface to the source 	MAC entry in the MAC address table.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question:
Which of the following statements best describes the 
forwarding logic that a LAN switch, with all interfaces assigned to VLAN 1 as per default settings, uses for an incoming frame with a destination MAC address of FFFF.FFFF.FFFF?
options: It forwards the frame out all switch ports.
It forwards the frame out all switch ports except the arrival 	port.
It forwards the frame out all ports that had earlier registered 	to ask to receive broadcasts.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes what a switch does with a frame destined for an unknown unicast address?
options: It forwards out all interfaces in the same VLAN except for the 	incoming interface.
It forwards the frame out the one interface identified by the 	matching entry in the MAC address table.
It compares the destination IP address to the destination MAC 	address.
It compares the frame's incoming interface to the source 	MAC entry in the MAC address table.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which of the following comparisons does a switch make when deciding whether a new MAC address should be added to its MAC address table?
options: It compares the unicast destination address to the bridging, 	or MAC address, table.
It compares the unicast source address to the bridging, or 	MAC address, table.
It compares the VLAN ID to the bridging, or MAC address, 	table.
It compares the destination IP address's ARP cache entry to 	the bridging, or MAC address, table.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: A Cisco Catalyst switch has 24 10/100 ports, numbered 0/1 through 0/24. Ten PCs connect to the ten lowest numbered ports, with those PCs working and sending data over the network. Port Fast Ethernet 0/5 is in a connected state.
Port Ethernet 0/15 is in a notconnected state.

The switch has learned three MAC addresses since the switch 	powered on.

Address 02CC.CCCC.CCCC was learned from the source MAC 	address of a frame that entered port Gi0/3.

questions.question:
An IP subnetting design effort is under way at a company. So far, the senior engineer has decided to use private Class B

The design also requires planning for 20 percent growth in the number of subnets and number of hosts/subnet in the largest subnet. questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: An engineer has planned to use private Class B network 172.19.0.0 and a single subnet mask throughout the network. The answers list the masks considered by the engineer. The router was never configured with an ip address 	command on the interface.

Which of the following best describes how R1 uses those recently learned LSAs to choose which IP routes to add to its IP routing table?
options: R1 copies a route from every LSA into its routing table.
R1 copies a route from certain types of LSAs into its routing 	table.
R1 runs SPF against the LSAs to calculate the routes.
R1 does not use the LSAs when choosing what routes to add.

It reduces the frequency of running the SPF algorithm due to 	interface status changes.
It allows for simpler planning and operations.

questions.question:
When reloading and choosing a new OSPF router ID (RID), a router had working interfaces loopback 1 with IP address 10.8.8.8, loopback 2 with address 10.7.7.7, and

options: The router would fail to choose an RID.

The ip ospf command in interface configuration mode
The router ospf command in interface configuration mode
The network command in interface configuration mode

Interface    PID   Area      IP Address/Mask   Co Gi0/0        1     0         10.1.1.1/24       1

questions.question:
Routers R1 and R2, which use default priority settings, 
become neighbors, with R1 as the DR and R2 as the BDR. The engineer then configures R2's interface to use OSPF priority 100. Which answers correctly predict any changes in the OSPF neighbor relationship?
options: Router R2 will immediately become the DR.
Router R2 will become the DR after the neighbor relationship 	fails.
Router R2 will immediately stop filling the BDR role.
Router R2 will become the DR after four OSPF Hello intervals.

A network engineer configures the ip ospf hello-interval 15 subcommand under the interfaces that connect OSPF neighbors R1 and R2 but with no use of the ip ospf dead-interval

questions.question: An engineer connects Routers R13 and R14 to the same Ethernet LAN and configures them to use OSPFv2. Which answers describe a combination of settings that would prevent the two routers from becoming OSPF neighbors?

Both routers' OSPF process uses process ID 13.
Both routers' OSPF process uses router ID 13.13.13.13.
Both routers' interfaces use an OSPF Dead interval of 40.

options: R15 empties its IP routing table of all OSPF routes but keeps 	its LSDB intact.
R15 empties its OSPF routes and LSDB but keeps OSPF 	neighbor relationships active.

options: Only the route with R2 as the next-hop router

The metric 2000 OSPF route and the metric 2,0000,000 EIGRP 	route

IPv6 neighbors (both routers and hosts) plus their MAC addresses, also noting which are routers
IPv6 routers, with no information about nonrouters, with no 	MAC address info
IPv6 routers, with no information about nonrouters, but with 	MAC address info

subnet. The user of PC1 pings the IPv6 address of a host that sits at a remote site so that the packets flow through R1, PC1's

options: All lines list LLAs with no GUAs.
The first line lists an address that matches PC1's default 	route.
All lines list GUAs with no LLAs.
The last line lists the GUA of the final router in the route.

With a prefix value ::/0 in the line for the route
With a prefix value ::/128 in the line for the route
With a prefix value 2000::/3 in the line for the route
With the keyword "default" in the line for the route

questions.question:
Router R1 has a useful IPv6 configuration so that Router R1 learns a route for subnet 2001:db8:1:2::/64 with OSPF. The route lists an outgoing interface that is an Ethernet WAN link. The engineer then installs cellular interfaces on both routers for WAN backup. As part of the configuration, the engineer 
configures the ipv6 route 2001:db8:1:2::/64 cellular0/2/0 200 command. What does the router do in response?
options: It rejects the ipv6 route command because of the existing 	OSPF route for the same prefix.
It accepts the ipv6 route command into the configuration but 	does not add a route to the routing table.
It accepts the ipv6 route command into the configuration and adds the route to the routing table, but it leaves the OSPF-learned route in the routing table.
It accepts the ipv6 route command into the configuration, adds the route to the routing table, and removes the OSPF-learned route in the routing table.

questions.question:
An engineer types the command ipv6 route 
2001:DB8:8:8::/64 2001:DB8:9:9::9 129 in configuration mode of Router R1 and presses Enter. Later, a show ipv6 route 
command lists no routes for subnet 2001:DB8:8:8::/64. Which of the following could have caused the route to not be in the IPv6 routing table?

The command must include an outgoing interface parameter, 	so IOS rejected the ipv6 route command.
The router has no routes that match the next-hop address 	2001:DB8:9:9::9.
A route for 2001:DB8:8:8::/64 with administrative distance 	110 already exists.
questions.source_ref: CCNA 200-301 Official Cert Guide Vol 1 (Wendell Odom)

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model makes resources available to the general public over the internet and is owned by a cloud service provider?
questions.explanation: A public cloud is owned and operated by a third-party cloud service provider (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and delivers resources—such as compute, storage, and networking—to the general public over the internet. Customers share the underlying infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model is operated exclusively for a single organization, may be hosted on-premises or by a third party, and offers the highest degree of control?
questions.explanation: A private cloud is provisioned for exclusive use by a single organization. It can be located on the organization's own data center or hosted by a third party. Private clouds offer the greatest control, customization, and security but require more capital investment.

questions.question: A company keeps sensitive customer data in its own on-premises data center but uses a public cloud provider for burst capacity during peak traffic periods. This is an example of which cloud model?
questions.explanation: A hybrid cloud combines two or more distinct cloud infrastructures (private, public, or community) that remain unique entities but are connected by standardized technology. Organizations use hybrid cloud to keep sensitive workloads private while leveraging public cloud scalability for variable demand.

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model is shared among several organizations with common concerns (e.g., government agencies, healthcare providers) and may be managed by one or more of the organizations?
questions.explanation: A community cloud is provisioned for exclusive use by a specific community of organizations that share common concerns such as mission, security requirements, policy, or compliance considerations. It may be owned, managed, and operated by one or more of the organizations in the community.

questions.question: In a public cloud model, who is responsible for managing the underlying physical infrastructure such as servers and network switches?
questions.explanation: In a public cloud, the CSP owns and manages all underlying physical infrastructure — servers, storage, networking, and the data center facility. This is a core value proposition of public cloud: customers consume resources without managing hardware.
options: The customer and cloud provider share equal responsibility
A third-party hardware vendor contracted by the customer

questions.question: Which of the following is a key advantage of a public cloud deployment model compared to a private cloud?
questions.explanation: Public cloud converts capital expenditure (CapEx) to operational expenditure (OpEx). Organizations pay only for what they use without purchasing hardware. Private clouds require significant upfront investment in data center infrastructure.

questions.question: An organization needs to meet strict government data residency laws requiring all data to remain within a specific country. Which cloud deployment model is most appropriate?
options: Public cloud with geographic region selection
questions.explanation: A private cloud gives the organization full control over data location and residency. While some public cloud providers offer region-specific storage, a private cloud ensures the organization maintains direct physical control over where data resides, making compliance easier to guarantee and audit.

questions.question: Which characteristic is common to ALL four cloud deployment models (public, private, hybrid, community)?
options: Resources are shared with the general public
Physical infrastructure owned by the tenant
questions.explanation: On-demand self-service is one of the five essential characteristics of cloud computing defined by NIST. All deployment models—public, private, hybrid, and community—allow consumers to provision resources as needed without requiring human interaction with the service provider.

questions.question: Cloud bursting is a configuration where an application runs in a private cloud and bursts into which other environment when demand spikes?
questions.explanation: Cloud bursting is a hybrid cloud technique where an application normally runs in the private cloud but dynamically 'bursts' into a public cloud when computing demand exceeds the private cloud's capacity. This provides cost-efficient scalability without over-provisioning private resources.

questions.question: What characteristic is required for a hybrid cloud to enable data and application portability between its private and public components?
questions.explanation: By NIST definition, a hybrid cloud is a composition of two or more distinct cloud infrastructures bound together by standardized or proprietary technology that enables data and application portability. This integration layer is what distinguishes hybrid from simply using multiple separate clouds.
options: Both components must use identical hardware from the same vendor
The private cloud must always be larger than the public cloud component
Standardized or proprietary technology that allows integration between components
All data must be replicated in real time between both components

questions.question: Which NIST cloud characteristic describes the ability to scale resources up or down rapidly, sometimes automatically, to match demand?
questions.explanation: Rapid elasticity is the NIST characteristic that allows cloud capabilities to be elastically provisioned and released—automatically or manually—to scale rapidly with demand. To the consumer, available resources often appear unlimited and can be appropriated at any time.

questions.question: Which NIST cloud characteristic describes that cloud services are accessible over the network through standard mechanisms (e.g., smartphones, laptops, thin clients)?
questions.explanation: Broad network access means capabilities are available over the network and accessed through standard mechanisms that promote use by heterogeneous thin or thick client platforms (e.g., mobile phones, tablets, laptops, and workstations).

questions.question: In cloud computing, 'resource pooling' means that the provider's resources serve multiple consumers using which model?
questions.explanation: Resource pooling uses a multi-tenant model where computing resources are pooled to serve multiple consumers with different physical and virtual resources dynamically assigned and reassigned according to consumer demand. Customers generally have no control or knowledge over the exact location of the provided resources.

questions.question: Which statement about private cloud hosting is correct?
questions.explanation: A private cloud can be on-premises (organization-owned data center) OR hosted by a third-party provider on infrastructure dedicated solely to that organization. The key is exclusive use — the shared-tenant model is what defines public cloud, not the physical location.
options: A private cloud can only be hosted in the organization's own on-premises data center
A private cloud can be hosted on-premises or by a third-party provider on dedicated resources
Private clouds must always be managed exclusively by internal IT staff
Private clouds are hosted exclusively in co-location facilities

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model would be most suitable for five competing banks that need a shared trading platform with strict financial regulation compliance but do not want to share resources with unrelated industries?
questions.explanation: A community cloud is ideal here. It allows multiple organizations with shared concerns (in this case financial regulation compliance) to share cloud infrastructure while keeping resources exclusive to their community. This balances cost sharing with the privacy and compliance requirements they have in common.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly differentiates a hybrid cloud from a multi-cloud strategy?
options: Hybrid cloud uses two public providers; multi-cloud uses one public and one private
Hybrid cloud integrates private and public clouds with standardized connectivity; multi-cloud uses multiple public providers without required integration
Hybrid cloud requires on-premises hardware; multi-cloud does not
There is no difference — the terms are interchangeable
questions.explanation: A hybrid cloud specifically combines private and public (or community) cloud infrastructures with standardized technology enabling workload portability. A multi-cloud strategy uses services from multiple public cloud providers (e.g., AWS + Azure) but does not require the formal integration layer that defines a hybrid cloud.

questions.question: What does the term 'measured service' mean in NIST's definition of cloud computing?
options: Resources are allocated in fixed monthly blocks
Resource usage is monitored, controlled, and reported, providing transparency for both provider and consumer
Only metered electricity is used in cloud data centers
Service levels are measured by latency only
questions.explanation: Measured service means cloud systems automatically control and optimize resource use by leveraging a metering capability. Resource usage is monitored, controlled, and reported — providing transparency for both the provider and consumer. This is the 'pay only for what you use' principle.

questions.question: Which statement most accurately describes community cloud costs compared to other cloud models?
questions.explanation: Public clouds spread infrastructure costs across millions of tenants, achieving massive economies of scale. Community clouds share costs among a much smaller group (e.g., healthcare organizations sharing HIPAA-compliant infrastructure), so per-organization costs can be higher than public cloud despite the shared model.
options: Community clouds are always less expensive than public clouds
Community clouds are always more expensive than private clouds
Community clouds may cost more per organization than public clouds due to fewer cost-sharing participants
Community clouds eliminate infrastructure costs entirely through resource pooling

questions.question: A university uses a public cloud for student email but maintains its own private cloud for research data subject to grant restrictions. When grant project demand spikes, it uses public cloud compute for processing (but not storage). Which model best describes this architecture?
questions.explanation: This is a hybrid cloud: the university maintains its private cloud for sensitive research data (meeting grant compliance) while using the public cloud for variable compute needs (cloud bursting). The two environments are integrated to allow workload mobility while keeping data in the private cloud.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage specific to private cloud compared to public cloud?
options: Data must be shared with other tenants
Higher upfront capital expenditure for infrastructure
questions.explanation: Private clouds require the organization to purchase, install, and maintain physical infrastructure — a significant capital expenditure. Public clouds eliminate this CapEx by shifting to an OpEx model where you pay per use. Private clouds also require dedicated IT staff to manage the infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cloud service model provides virtualized computing infrastructure — including virtual machines, storage, and networking — managed by the provider, while the customer manages the OS, middleware, and applications?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) provides virtualized computing resources over the internet. The provider manages physical infrastructure, hypervisors, and virtualization. The customer manages the OS, runtime, middleware, data, and applications. Examples: AWS EC2, Azure VMs, Google Compute Engine.

questions.question: Which cloud service model provides a platform for developers to build, deploy, and manage applications without managing the underlying infrastructure or operating system?
questions.explanation: Platform as a Service (PaaS) provides a development and deployment environment in the cloud. The provider manages everything from the infrastructure up through the runtime/middleware. Developers focus on code and data. Examples: AWS Elastic Beanstalk, Google App Engine, Microsoft Azure App Service.

questions.question: Which cloud service model delivers fully functional software applications over the internet, where the provider manages everything including the application itself?
questions.explanation: Software as a Service (SaaS) delivers complete applications via a web browser or thin client. The provider manages all layers: infrastructure, OS, middleware, runtime, and the application. Customers only interact with the software. Examples: Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Salesforce, Webex.

questions.question: In an IaaS model, which components is the CUSTOMER responsible for managing? (Select the best answer)
options: Physical servers, network switches, and power systems
All layers from hardware to application
questions.explanation: In IaaS, the provider manages physical hardware and the hypervisor. The customer is responsible for everything above: installing and patching the OS, installing middleware, deploying applications, and managing data. This gives customers maximum control but also maximum responsibility.

questions.question: A software company wants to deploy a web application without worrying about server patching, OS updates, or scaling infrastructure. They want to focus solely on writing code. Which cloud service model is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: PaaS is ideal for developers who want to focus on building and deploying applications without managing the underlying infrastructure. The PaaS provider handles OS, runtime, middleware, and scaling. The customer only manages the application code and data.

questions.question: Microsoft 365 (formerly Office 365) is an example of Software as a Service (SaaS).
questions.explanation: True. Microsoft 365 is a classic SaaS example. Microsoft manages all underlying infrastructure, OS, middleware, and the application itself (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams, etc.). Users access fully-functional software through a browser or thin client without managing any infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cloud service model gives the customer the MOST control over the computing environment?
questions.explanation: IaaS gives customers the most control because they manage the OS, middleware, runtime, applications, and data. In PaaS, the provider also manages the OS and runtime. In SaaS, the provider manages everything. More control in IaaS comes with more management responsibility.

questions.question: In a PaaS model, which layers does the cloud PROVIDER typically manage?
options: Physical hardware, virtualization, OS, and runtime/middleware
Physical hardware, virtualization, OS, runtime, and the customer's application
questions.explanation: In PaaS, the provider manages: physical hardware, virtualization/hypervisor, OS, and runtime/middleware. The customer manages: applications and data. This contrasts with IaaS where the customer also manages the OS and runtime, and SaaS where the provider manages the application too.

questions.question: A network team needs to spin up 50 Linux virtual machines with specific network configurations in minutes, install their own security software, and have full root access. Which cloud service model is most appropriate?
options: IaaS — provides virtual machines with full OS control
questions.explanation: IaaS is correct because the team needs full OS-level access (root access) and control to install custom software and configure networking. PaaS abstracts away the OS, preventing direct OS access. SaaS provides finished applications, not configurable VMs.

questions.question: In a SaaS cloud service model, who is responsible for patching and updating the application software?
questions.explanation: SaaS providers manage the entire stack from infrastructure through the application layer, including all patching, updates, and maintenance. This is a primary benefit of SaaS — customers use the application without worrying about software lifecycle management.
options: A third-party MSP hired by the customer
The application vendor and customer share responsibility equally

questions.question: Which cloud service model shifts the most management responsibility to the cloud provider, leaving the customer with the least amount of work?
options: All models have equal provider responsibility
questions.explanation: SaaS places the most responsibility on the provider. In SaaS, the provider manages physical infrastructure, virtualization, OS, runtime, middleware, and the application. The customer only manages user accounts, data, and access permissions. This is the least management overhead for the customer.

questions.question: Amazon Web Services (AWS) EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud), which provides virtual machines that customers can configure with their own OS and software, is an example of which cloud service model?
questions.explanation: AWS EC2 is a classic IaaS offering. AWS provides the physical hardware and hypervisor; the customer selects the OS (from AMIs), installs software, and configures networking and security. The customer manages everything above the hypervisor layer.

questions.question: Which cloud service model is most similar to renting a fully-furnished apartment where everything from furniture to utilities is included and managed by the landlord?
options: IaaS — like renting an empty warehouse
PaaS — like renting a partially-furnished apartment
SaaS — like renting a fully-furnished apartment
DaaS — like renting a managed desktop
questions.explanation: The fully-furnished apartment analogy maps to SaaS: everything is managed and provided — you just move in and use it. IaaS is like renting an empty warehouse (you build everything yourself). PaaS is like renting a space with basic utilities — you bring your own furniture (application).

questions.question: Across all cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS), which element does the customer always retain responsibility for?
questions.explanation: Regardless of cloud model, the customer always retains responsibility for their own data and for managing who has access to it. In IaaS the customer manages OS and above; in PaaS the customer manages apps and data; in SaaS the customer only manages data and user access — but data responsibility never transfers to the provider.
options: Their own data and access management

questions.question: A company's development team needs a pre-configured environment with a specific version of Python, a database, and a web server framework already installed. They do not want to manage any servers. Which model fits best?
options: IaaS — build the environment from scratch on a VM
PaaS — get a pre-configured runtime/platform for development
questions.explanation: PaaS provides the runtime, middleware, and development platform pre-configured. Developers bring their code and data; the provider handles OS, server, database engine installation, and runtime. PaaS is specifically designed for development teams that want to focus on code, not infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cloud service model best represents Cisco Webex (formerly Webex Teams/Meetings) as a collaboration tool accessed via browser or app?
questions.explanation: Cisco Webex is a SaaS application. Cisco manages all infrastructure and the collaboration application. Customers access it via browser or installed client without managing any servers, OS, or application updates. This is a complete, ready-to-use software delivered over the internet.

questions.question: In the shared responsibility model of cloud computing, which statement correctly describes a responsibility split in IaaS?
options: Provider patches guest OS; customer manages hypervisor
Customer patches guest OS; provider manages physical hardware and hypervisor
Provider manages all layers including customer applications
Customer is responsible for physical hardware security
questions.explanation: In IaaS, the shared responsibility model places physical hardware, data center, and hypervisor management on the provider. The customer is responsible for the guest OS (including patching), applications, data, and security configurations above the hypervisor. This is a fundamental IaaS concept.

questions.question: In a PaaS cloud model, who is responsible for managing operating system patches and updates?
questions.explanation: In PaaS, the provider manages all infrastructure below the application layer, including OS patching and runtime updates. Developers focus on writing and deploying applications, not managing the underlying platform. This is the key distinction between PaaS and IaaS.
options: The developer who deployed the application
A shared responsibility between developer and provider equally

questions.question: Which cloud service model provides the fastest path to deploying a new application for end users with no infrastructure management?
questions.explanation: SaaS provides the fastest path to end-user deployment because the application is already built, hosted, and maintained by the provider. Users simply sign up and start using the service — no infrastructure setup, no OS installation, no application configuration beyond basic user preferences.

questions.question: A company runs a custom CRM application on AWS EC2 instances (IaaS). They want to reduce management overhead. Moving the application to which model would eliminate OS patching while keeping the same application code?
options: SaaS — use a pre-built CRM like Salesforce
PaaS — migrate the application to a managed platform service
Community cloud — share the VM management with other companies
Colocation — move servers to a data center
questions.explanation: Migrating from IaaS to PaaS keeps the same application code but offloads OS and runtime management to the provider. In PaaS, the provider handles OS patching, runtime updates, and infrastructure scaling. The team keeps full control of the application logic and data without OS administration.

questions.question: A Type 1 hypervisor (bare-metal hypervisor) runs directly on which layer of the computing stack?
options: On top of a host operating system
Directly on the physical hardware (bare metal)
questions.explanation: A Type 1 hypervisor runs directly on physical hardware without requiring a host OS. It IS the operating system for the hardware, managing VMs directly. Examples include VMware ESXi, Microsoft Hyper-V (bare metal), and KVM. Type 1 hypervisors are used in enterprise data centers and cloud environments.

questions.question: A Type 2 hypervisor is also called a hosted hypervisor because it runs as an application on top of which layer?
questions.explanation: A Type 2 hypervisor runs as a software application on a host OS. The host OS runs on the physical hardware, and the hypervisor runs on top of the OS creating VMs. Examples include VMware Workstation, Oracle VirtualBox, and Parallels Desktop. Type 2 is common for desktop virtualization and development.

questions.question: Which type of hypervisor provides better performance for enterprise data center virtualization and why?
options: Type 2, because it has direct OS support
Type 1, because it has direct hardware access without a host OS overhead layer
Type 2, because it can use all host OS drivers
questions.explanation: Type 1 hypervisors provide better performance because they access hardware directly without the overhead of a host OS layer. Fewer software layers between the hypervisor and hardware means lower latency, better resource efficiency, and higher VM density — critical requirements for production data centers.

questions.question: VMware ESXi is an example of which type of hypervisor?
questions.explanation: VMware ESXi is a Type 1 (bare-metal) hypervisor. It installs directly on physical server hardware without requiring a host OS and manages virtual machines directly. It is the foundational component of VMware vSphere, widely used in enterprise data centers.

questions.question: VirtualBox by Oracle is which type of hypervisor, and where is it most commonly used?
questions.explanation: VirtualBox is a Type 2 (hosted) hypervisor — it runs as an application on top of a host OS such as Windows, macOS, or Linux. Type 1 (bare-metal) hypervisors like VMware ESXi or Microsoft Hyper-V run directly on hardware and are used in enterprise data centers. VirtualBox is free and popular for local dev/lab environments.
options: Type 1 hypervisor used in enterprise data centers
Type 1 hypervisor used for desktop development
Type 2 hypervisor used for desktop and development environments
Type 2 hypervisor used exclusively by cloud service providers

questions.question: How do containers differ from virtual machines in terms of OS usage?
options: Containers each run their own complete OS kernel; VMs share one OS
VMs each run their own OS; containers share the host OS kernel
Both containers and VMs share a single OS kernel
Containers require a Type 1 hypervisor; VMs do not
questions.explanation: Virtual machines each include a complete OS (kernel + user space), making them heavier. Containers share the host OS kernel and isolate application processes using Linux namespaces and cgroups. This makes containers much lighter (MB vs GB), faster to start (seconds vs minutes), and more efficient with resources.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key advantage of containers over virtual machines for microservices deployments?
options: Stronger isolation due to separate OS kernels
Faster startup times and lower resource overhead
Better hardware compatibility with Type 1 hypervisors
questions.explanation: Containers start in seconds (vs. minutes for VMs), consume less memory (MB vs. GB), and have minimal overhead since they share the host kernel. This makes them ideal for microservices where many small, independent services must be deployed and scaled rapidly.

questions.question: Docker is a popular example of which virtualization technology?
questions.explanation: Docker is a container platform that packages applications and their dependencies into lightweight, portable containers that share the host OS kernel. Containers are not VMs — they do not include a full OS. Docker is the most widely used container platform for development and deployment.

questions.question: In server virtualization, a virtual switch (vSwitch) provides connectivity between which components?
options: Between physical servers and the internet
Between virtual machines and the physical network (or between VMs on the same host)
questions.explanation: A virtual switch (vSwitch) is a software-based Layer 2 switch that runs inside the hypervisor. It connects VMs to each other (on the same host) and to the physical network via physical NICs (uplink ports). Examples include VMware vSwitch, Cisco Nexus 1000V, and Open vSwitch (OVS).

questions.question: What is Network Function Virtualization (NFV)?
options: Replacing physical network hardware with software-based network functions running on standard servers
Using VLANs to segment virtual machine traffic
Running SDN controllers on virtual machines
questions.explanation: NFV (Network Function Virtualization) decouples network functions (firewalls, routers, load balancers, IDS/IPS, WAN optimizers) from dedicated proprietary hardware and runs them as Virtual Network Functions (VNFs) on standard commodity servers. This reduces hardware costs, increases flexibility, and accelerates service deployment.

questions.question: A virtual machine includes its own guest operating system, while a container shares the host operating system kernel.
questions.explanation: True. Each VM includes a full guest OS (kernel + libraries + user space), making it a complete isolated system. Containers share the host OS kernel using namespaces and cgroups for isolation. This architectural difference makes VMs more isolated but heavier, and containers lighter but with a shared kernel attack surface.

questions.question: In a virtualized data center, VM traffic between two VMs on the SAME physical host typically traverses which path?
options: Physical switch → physical router → physical switch
Virtual switch inside the hypervisor without leaving the physical server
The internet and back through the cloud provider
A dedicated physical backplane in the server
questions.explanation: When two VMs on the same physical host communicate, traffic passes through the virtual switch (vSwitch) inside the hypervisor — it never leaves the physical server. This is a major performance advantage: east-west traffic between co-located VMs gets wire-speed switching without traversing physical network infrastructure.

questions.question: What does a Virtual Network Function (VNF) replace in a traditional network?
options: Dedicated proprietary hardware appliances such as firewalls, routers, or load balancers
The management plane of physical switches
questions.explanation: A VNF (Virtual Network Function) is a software implementation of a network function traditionally performed by dedicated hardware. For example, a VNF firewall replaces a hardware firewall appliance; a VNF router replaces a physical router. VNFs run on standard commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) servers.

questions.question: Which technology allows multiple virtual machines to share the CPU, memory, and storage of a single physical server?
questions.explanation: Server virtualization uses a hypervisor to create multiple isolated virtual machines on a single physical server, with each VM sharing the server's physical CPU, memory, and storage resources. The hypervisor manages resource allocation and isolation between VMs.

questions.question: Why do virtual machines provide stronger security isolation than containers?
questions.explanation: VMs provide stronger isolation because each VM runs its own OS kernel — a kernel-level exploit in one VM cannot directly affect others. Containers share the host OS kernel, so a container escape exploit could potentially impact all containers on that host. Containers offer process isolation, not full OS isolation.
options: Containers use namespaces that provide kernel-level isolation equivalent to VMs
VMs are more lightweight, giving the hypervisor better control over resources
Each VM has its own separate OS kernel, so a kernel compromise cannot spread to other VMs
VMs use hardware-enforced memory encryption that containers cannot access

questions.question: Kubernetes (K8s) is a tool primarily used for which purpose?
options: Managing Type 1 hypervisors in data centers
Orchestrating and managing containerized applications at scale
questions.explanation: Kubernetes is an open-source container orchestration platform that automates the deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications. It schedules containers across a cluster of nodes, handles load balancing, self-healing, and rolling updates for container-based microservices.

questions.question: A company wants to deploy 500 microservices that start quickly, use minimal memory, and can be rapidly scaled. Which technology is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Containers are ideal for microservices at scale: they start in seconds, use MB of RAM (vs GB for VMs), and Kubernetes can automatically scale them based on demand. Running 500 separate VMs would consume enormous resources; containers provide the density and agility needed for modern microservices architectures.

questions.question: In server virtualization, what is the purpose of the hypervisor's vNIC (virtual NIC) assigned to a VM?
